title: feat(read_file): add chunked reading with start_line/end_line parameters
body:
### Related Issue

**Issue:** #8659  
Reopened from #9655 (closed in error). New branch: `feat/chunked-read-file`, rebased on latest main.

### Description

Builds on #9371 (line labels) to add chunked file reading to `read_file`. Instead of dumping entire files into context, the tool now:

- Returns at most 1000 lines per call (configurable via `start_line`/`end_line`)
- Appends a continuation hint when truncated: `(Showing lines 1-1000 of 6750 total. Use start_line=1001 to continue reading.)`
- Lets the model paginate to specific sections on follow-up calls

This matches the chunked reading pattern used by Codex, OpenCode, and Gemini CLI.

**Changes:**
- `src/core/assistant-message/index.ts` — Add `start_line`/`end_line` to `toolParamNames`
- `src/core/prompts/system-prompt/tools/read_file.ts` — Add optional `start_line`/`end_line` parameters to tool spec for all variants
- `src/core/task/tools/handlers/ReadFileToolHandler.ts` — Slice to requested range, add line labels, append continuation hints
- Snapshot updates across all model families

### Test Procedure

- `npm run test:unit` — all passing
- 10 new test cases covering: line labels, chunked reads, clamping of out-of-range values, continuation hints, default max lines enforcement, and byte-truncation interaction
- Manual testing confirmed chunking works correctly

### Type of Change

-   [x] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)

### Pre-flight Checklist

-   [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore
-   [x] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted
-   [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)


Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)
author: robinnewhouse

reviews.body:
Like the intent, details need work.

Hey why did we choose start_line, end_line? I'm wondering if we should design these tools to be hard to use incorrectly. See the inline comment.

LGTM

I think we should consider start_line, count but consistency is good; we can change it later.

## Pull request overview

Fixes the Kanban launch modal video playback across IDEs by providing both an H.264/MP4 source (for VS Code/Electron) and a VP9/WebM source (for JetBrains/JCEF), and ensures CI validates both LFS-managed media assets are fully materialized during publishing.

- Update the Kanban launch modal to serve both MP4 and WebM sources via `<video><source .../></video>`.
- Add the MP4 asset to Git LFS tracking.
- Extend publish workflows to verify both MP4 and WebM files are not LFS pointer files at publish time.

commits.message:
fix: Serve H.264/MP4 for VSCode and VP9/WebM for JetBrains

VSCode's webview doesn't reliably play VP9/WebM, while JetBrains'
JCEF lacks H.264 decoding. Restore the original H.264/MP4 video and
use HTML5 <source> elements with explicit MIME types so each platform
picks the format it supports:

  <source src=... type="video/mp4" />   <!-- VSCode -->
  <source src=... type="video/webm" />  <!-- JetBrains -->

Also:
- Restore .mp4 Git LFS tracking in .gitattributes
- Update CI LFS verification to check both files
- Add webm to JetBrains MIME type map (BrowserRequestHandler)

title: fix: Transcode kanban video to VP9/WebM for JetBrains
body:
## Problem

The kanban demo video is encoded with H.264. JetBrains IDEs use JCEF (Chromium Embedded Framework) for their webviews, and Chromium builds often do **not** include H.264 decoding due to licensing restrictions. This causes the kanban demo video to fail to play in JetBrains-based editors.

Transcode the video to **VP9/WebM**, which is a royalty-free codec with universal support across all Chromium derivatives (Chrome, Edge, JCEF, Electron, etc.).

Bonus: File size reduced from **3.6 MB → 895 KB** (~75% smaller) at the same resolution 1156×720, 30fps.

## Test Plan

```
./gradlew :runIde
```

Check the kanban sizzle video plays.
author: dominiccooney

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Transcodes the Kanban demo video asset from H.264/MP4 to VP9/WebM so it reliably plays in Chromium-based webviews that may not ship with H.264 decoding (e.g., JetBrains JCEF), while updating references and CI/LFS tracking accordingly.

**Changes:**
- Replace `cline_kanban_demo.mp4` with `cline_kanban_demo.webm` (tracked via Git LFS).
- Update the webview UI import to reference the new WebM asset.
- Update CI publish workflows’ LFS verification to check the new asset path.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 6 out of 6 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `webview-ui/src/components/common/ClineKanbanLaunchModal.tsx` | Switches the demo video import from `.mp4` to `.webm`. |
| `webview-ui/src/assets/cline_kanban_demo.webm` | Adds the new WebM demo video (Git LFS pointer). |
| `webview-ui/src/assets/cline_kanban_demo.mp4` | Removes the old MP4 demo video (Git LFS pointer). |
| `.github/workflows/publish.yml` | Updates LFS media asset verification to point at the WebM file. |
| `.github/workflows/publish-nightly.yml` | Updates LFS media asset verification to point at the WebM file. |
| `.gitattributes` | Updates Git LFS tracking rule to track the WebM file instead of MP4. |
</details>

commits.message:
Transcode kanban demo video from H.264/MP4 to VP9/WebM

JetBrains IDEs use JCEF (Chromium Embedded Framework) for webviews,
which often does not include H.264 decoding due to licensing
restrictions. This causes the kanban demo video to fail to play.

Transcode the video from H.264/MP4 to VP9/WebM, which is a royalty-free
codec universally supported in Chromium derivatives. This also reduces
the file size from 3.6MB to ~900KB.

Updated all references:
- Source component import (ClineKanbanLaunchModal.tsx)
- Git LFS tracking (.gitattributes)
- CI workflow LFS checks (publish.yml, publish-nightly.yml)

title: fix: exclude new_task tool from system prompt in yolo/headless mode
body:
### Related Issue

**Issue:** N/A (bug discovered during CLI headless mode investigation)

### Description

The `new_task` tool requires interactive user input — it creates an `ask` message of type `"new_task"` and blocks waiting for a response. In headless CLI mode (`-y`/`--yolo`, piped, `--json`) and ACP agent mode, no component responds to this ask, causing the task to **hang indefinitely**.

This PR adds a `contextRequirements` filter to exclude `new_task` from the system prompt when `yoloModeToggled` is true, matching the existing pattern used by `ask_followup_question`.
**Known limitations:**
- This only covers `-y`/`--yolo` headless mode. Piped mode without `-y` is not covered, though that scenario already hangs at every approval prompt so `-y` is effectively required for headless operation.
- ACP agent mode only benefits if the agent's auto-approval settings result in `yoloModeToggled` being set. If not, the tool still appears in the prompt (though the agent cannot respond to it).
- A more complete fix would require a dedicated `isHeadlessMode`/`isNonInteractive` context flag that precisely targets non-interactive runtimes regardless of approval settings.

### Test Procedure

- Added 5 unit tests in `new-task-yolo-filter.test.ts` verifying:
  - `contextRequirements` is defined on the tool spec
  - Tool is enabled when `yoloModeToggled` is `false` or `undefined`
  - Tool is disabled when `yoloModeToggled` is `true`
  - `new_task` and `ask_followup_question` follow the identical filtering pattern through the `ClineToolSet` registry
- All 61 existing snapshot tests pass unchanged
- All 122 prompt-related unit tests pass
### Type of Change

-   [x] Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
-   [ ] New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
-   [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected)
-   [ ] Refactor Changes
-   [ ] Cosmetic Changes
-   [ ] Documentation update
-   [ ] Workflow Changes

### Pre-flight Checklist

-   [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs)
-   [x] Tests are passing and code is formatted and linted
-   [x] I have reviewed contributor guidelines

### Screenshots

N/A - backend-only change with no UI impact.

### Additional Notes

The code change is a single line addition to `src/core/prompts/system-prompt/tools/new_task.ts`, following the exact same `contextRequirements` pattern already used by `ask_followup_question`, `browser_action`, `access_mcp_resource`, and other context-gated tools.
author: robinnewhouse

reviews.body:
Looks good.

The naming and/or design could be better, but up to you whether you have appetite for doing that now. Not necessary, just something to think about.

title: Hooks: `Notification` hook polish
body:
### Related Issue

**Issue:** CLINE-1572

### Description

This PR makes Notification hook behavior consistent and production-ready. It moves Notification handling into one centralized path, clarifies the payload contract for hook authors, and ensures notifications are emitted in a predictable way for both user-attention pauses and task completion.

It also tightens the reliability of this flow by keeping Notification hooks observation-only and fail-open, so they can’t interrupt task execution or mutate context. Finally, it adds focused test coverage around the new behavior so the contract is explicit and easier to maintain.


### Test Procedure

This branch introduces new unit tests.  The tests pass.

I manually verified that the `Notification` hook type runs as intended for `task_complete` and `user_attention` event triggers.  To do so I created a hook like the following:

```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

afplay /System/Library/Sounds/Glass.aiff
INPUT=$(cat)
echo $INPUT | jq . >> "/tmp/out.log"

LOG_DIR="/tmp/logs"
mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR"
TS=$(date +%s)
echo "$INPUT" > "$LOG_DIR/notification-$TS.json"
echo "[notification-hook] wrote $LOG_DIR/notification-$TS.json" >&2
printf '{"cancel":false,"contextModification":"","errorMessage":""}'
```

_**Note:** There is no UI block for the `Notification` hook the way there is for other hooks currently.  This is something we might want to revisit for other hooks too (i.e. maybe in the future we don't want hooks to render in the UI at all when they're executed)._

* To trigger `task_complete` event notification: running a simple task to completion successfully triggers the `Notification` hook.

* To trigger `user_attention` event notification: prompting Cline to ask you a question (like `Please ask me a question.`) successfully triggers the `Notification` hook.

Example output:
```json
{
  "clineVersion": "3.74.0",
  "hookName": "Notification",
  "timestamp": "1774034179253",
  "taskId": "1774030394024",
  "workspaceRoots": [
    "/Users/foo/bar/bah/..."
  ],
  "userId": "usr-...",
  "model": {
    "provider": "openai-codex",
    "slug": "gpt-5.4"
  },
  "notification": {
    "event": "user_attention",
    "source": "completion_result",
    "waitingForUserInput": true
  }
}
```


### Type of Change

title: feat(webview): add Cline Kanban launch modal and queue announcements
body:
### Related Issue

Issue: N/A

### Description

This PR adds a dedicated launch modal for Cline Kanban that is independent from existing announcement flows.

The modal is rendered from `App.tsx` and includes:
- a bundled local MP4 demo
- product copy provided in this thread
- install command display and platform-specific action handling
  - VS Code runs the command in terminal via `StateServiceClient.installClineCli`
  - non-VS Code hosts copy the command to clipboard
- a persistent `Do not show again` option saved via `dismissedBanners`

There were two key integration issues during implementation:
1. Media loading in webviews returned 403 for local assets in dev because Vite was emitting absolute `/assets/...` URLs that do not map cleanly in VS Code webview resource roots.
2. Video playback policy in webviews needed explicit CSP support for media sources.

To fix this, the webview build now uses a relative Vite base (`base: "./"`), media source resolution in the modal is normalized for dev/prod URL shapes, and CSP includes `media-src` in both static and HMR contexts.

This PR also resolves modal stacking jank by queuing update announcements behind the Kanban modal. If both are eligible, Kanban shows first. Once it is closed or skipped, the update modal is shown.

### Test Procedure

I validated this change with repeated local runs while iterating on modal order and media loading:

- `npm run lint`
- `npm run check-types`
- `npm run test:webview`

Checks pass after final changes.

Manual behavior verified during development:
- Kanban modal appears on launch when not dismissed
- `Do not show again` persists and suppresses future display
- install CTA behavior differs correctly by host type
- update announcement waits until Kanban modal is no longer blocking
- MP4 renders in webview once built with relative asset paths

title: feat(cli): launch kanban by default with migration view
body:
### Related Issue

Issue: #XXXX

### Description

This PR changes the default CLI entrypoint so a bare `cline` launch opens Cline Kanban instead of dropping into the terminal UI.

The old plan for this work was a separate `--kanban` flag plus an in-TUI banner. We moved away from that after trying the flow end to end. Asking users to remember a second flag felt like the wrong default if Kanban is the direction we actually want to send them, and the banner kept the migration message buried inside the legacy experience instead of meeting users at the entrypoint. The branch originally opened with that older approach, which is why the existing PR text had to be rewritten as the implementation evolved.

The final behavior is split by user type:

- New CLI users go straight to Kanban when they run `cline` with no prompt or other task options.
- Returning CLI users see a one-time migration screen before Kanban launches.
- Anyone who wants the old terminal flow can still run `cline --tui`.
- `cline --kanban` still works, but it is no longer the primary path.

The migration view is intentionally simple. It uses the same Ink-style menu treatment as the existing onboarding screens, but only offers two actions:

- `Open the new experience`
- `Exit`

The copy tells users that Cline is moving out of the terminal and that they can rerun with `cline --tui` if they want the old CLI. We specifically removed the earlier version that offered a direct legacy-TUI branch from inside the migration menu because that kept the transition UI doing too much and made the message less clear.

A few implementation details were easy to get wrong and are worth calling out:

- The returning-user heuristic is not based on a single onboarding boolean. It looks at task history, `isNewUser`, whether `welcomeViewCompleted` has ever been set, and whether auth has been configured. That gives us a better signal for existing CLI users than just checking whether they have seen the welcome flow.
- The migration announcement is persisted with a dedicated global-state key, `cliKanbanMigrationAnnouncementShown`, so we only interrupt returning users once.
- Bare-launch routing only flips to Kanban for the true interactive no-prompt case. Prompted runs, piped stdin, resume flows, continue flows, and explicit `--tui` launches keep their normal behavior.
- During iteration, `Ctrl+C` inside the migration screen was falling through to the default selection and launching Kanban. That is fixed now by treating escape paths as exit instead of defaulting to Kanban when the Ink app closes without an explicit selection.
- I removed the old banner-based handoff from the TUI, since once `cline` itself opens Kanban by default the banner becomes redundant and confusing.
- The new migration-state key is excluded from the config viewer so it does not show up as user-facing editable config noise.

There is also a small but important follow-up fix in the latest commit around the spawned Kanban process lifecycle. Once `cline` starts launching Kanban for people by default, shutdown behavior matters a lot more than it did for an explicit helper command. The branch now detaches the Kanban child into its own process group on Unix-like systems, forwards `SIGINT` and `SIGTERM` to that group for graceful shutdown, escalates to `SIGKILL` after 10 seconds if needed, and maps signal exits back to the conventional shell codes like `130` for `SIGINT` and `143` for `SIGTERM`. That work came out of noticing that without more deliberate process handling, Ctrl+C and terminal shutdown paths could leave the spawned process in a messy state or return less useful exit codes.

This branch was also rebased onto the latest `origin/main` before updating the PR.

### Test Procedure

Commands run:

```sh
cd cli && npx vitest run src/index.test.ts src/utils/kanban.test.ts src/components/KanbanMigrationView.test.tsx
cd cli && npx tsc --noEmit
```

What I checked:

- The command surface still parses correctly, including `--kanban` and the new `--tui` path.
- The Kanban launch helper and migration-announcement heuristics behave as expected in unit tests.
- The migration screen renders the final copy and selecting the default option still launches the Kanban path.
- The new process-lifecycle tests cover detached spawn options on Unix-like platforms plus signal forwarding behavior on both Unix-like platforms and Windows.
- The focused CLI TypeScript build passes after wiring the new default-launch, migration-screen, and process-shutdown flow.

What I did not run:

- Full repo test suites
- CLI TUI e2e coverage via `tui-test`

### Type of Change

-   [ ] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
-   [x] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
-   [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected)
-   [ ] ♻️ Refactor Changes
-   [x] 💅 Cosmetic Changes
-   [ ] 📚 Documentation update
-   [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes

### Pre-flight Checklist

-   [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs)
-   [ ] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`)
-   [ ] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Screenshots

Not included. This changes a CLI migration screen and launch flow.

### Additional Notes

This PR was originally opened with an older banner-focused implementation and was later updated in place as we switched to making bare `cline` launch Kanban by default.

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR refreshes the CLI welcome experience and centralizes the Kanban launcher logic so `cline kanban` and `cline --kanban` share a single spawn path.

**Changes:**
- Introduces a shared `spawnKanbanProcess` helper and updates CLI command/flag wiring to use it.
- Replaces the standalone `WelcomeView` with a `ChatView`-owned welcome header that includes a new `KanbanBanner`.
- Updates CLI command parsing tests to match the updated Kanban help text.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 6 out of 6 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| cli/src/utils/kanban.ts | Adds a reusable Kanban launcher helper/constants for spawning `npx` consistently across platforms. |

| cli/src/index.test.ts | Updates command/option descriptions for Kanban to match the new help text. |
| cli/src/components/WelcomeView.tsx | Removes the old standalone welcome screen component. |
| cli/src/components/KanbanBanner.tsx | Adds a new banner component with manual terminal-width centering and display-width calculation. |
| cli/src/components/ChatView.tsx | Renders `KanbanBanner` within the welcome header (static + animated welcome states). |
</details>

commits.message: feat(cli): refresh welcome banner and kanban launcher
feat(cli): launch kanban by default with migration view

feat(cli): add kanban process lifecycle management

Detach the kanban child process on Unix so it gets its own process
group, forward signals to the group for graceful shutdown, and fall
back to SIGKILL after a 10s timeout. Resolves exit codes from signals
correctly (130 for SIGINT, 143 for SIGTERM).

title: feat: add repeated tool call loop detection
body:
### Related Issue

Addresses #9923, #9916, #9846, #9816, #8237, #8637 — LLM gets stuck calling the same tool with identical arguments repeatedly, wasting tokens and money.

### Description

Adds detection for **exact repeated tool calls** — when the LLM calls the same tool with identical arguments multiple times consecutively. This is not a general "loop" detector; it specifically catches the case where the model is stuck repeating the exact same action.

**Detection:** After every tool execution, compare `(tool name, toolCallSignature(params))` to the previous call. Uses `JSON.stringify` with a sorted key replacer array for deterministic comparison. Metadata params like `task_progress` (which change every call even when actual tool arguments are identical) are stripped from the comparison.

**Two-stage escalation:**
- **Stage 1 (3 identical calls):** Inject a warning into the model context via `formatResponse.repeatedToolCall()`, giving the LLM one chance to self-correct
- **Stage 2 (5 identical calls):** Set `consecutiveMistakeCount` to `maxConsecutiveMistakes`, triggering the existing escalation path

**Loop detection re-arms after user continues:** When hard escalation triggers `mistake_limit_reached` and the user clicks "continue", all loop detection state (`consecutiveIdenticalToolCount`, `lastToolName`, `lastToolParams`) is reset alongside `consecutiveMistakeCount`. This ensures the detector fully re-arms — if the model keeps looping, the user gets the full soft-warning → hard-escalation cycle again.

**Why existing mechanisms don't catch this:**
- `consecutiveMistakeCount` only counts errors, not identical successful calls
- `lastToolName` only blocks consecutive `act_mode_respond` (PR #8576), not general tools
- `fileReadCache` deduplicates file content but still allows the tool call to consume a turn

**Verified manually:** CLI test with loop-inducing `.clinerules` confirms soft warning fires at call 3 and YOLO mode fails at call 5. E2e script confirms loop detection re-arms after reset and doesn't false-positive when the model switches tools.

### Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/core/task/loop-detection.ts` | Shared helper: `toolCallSignature` (strips `task_progress`, sorts keys), `checkRepeatedToolCall` |
| `src/core/task/TaskState.ts` | Add `lastToolParams` and `consecutiveIdenticalToolCount` fields |
| `src/core/task/ToolExecutor.ts` | Call `checkRepeatedToolCall()` in `handleCompleteBlock()` before updating state |
| `src/core/task/index.ts` | Reset loop detection state in `mistake_limit_reached` handler so detector re-arms |
| `src/core/prompts/responses.ts` | Add `formatResponse.repeatedToolCall()` |
| `src/core/task/__tests__/loop-detection.test.ts` | 7 tests covering thresholds, resets, re-arming after continue, tool switching, task_progress stripping |

### Test results

- [x] `npm run lint` clean
- [x] `npm run format:fix` clean
- [x] `npm run test:unit` — 1298 passing, 0 failing
- [x] Manual CLI test: soft warning at call 3, YOLO failure at call 5
- [x] task_progress metadata correctly stripped from comparison
- [x] Loop detection re-arms after user continues (verified via e2e script)
author: johnwschoi

reviews.body: Identified one thing that might be worth addressing.

commits.message:
feat: add repeated tool call loop detection

Detect when the LLM calls the same tool with identical arguments
repeatedly, which wastes tokens without making progress. This is
the #1 active complaint in the Cline issue tracker (13+ open issues
including #9923, #9916, #9846, #9816).

Two-stage escalation:
- Stage 1 (3 identical calls): inject a warning nudging the LLM
  to try a different approach
- Stage 2 (5 identical calls): trigger the existing
  consecutiveMistakeCount escalation (asks user or fails in YOLO)

Detection uses JSON.stringify with a sorted key replacer for
deterministic comparison. Metadata params like task_progress
(which change every call even when actual tool arguments are
identical) are stripped from the comparison.

Complementary to fileReadCache, which deduplicates file content but
still allows the tool call to succeed and consume a turn. Loop
detection catches the repeated call pattern itself.

Changes:
- loop-detection.ts: shared helper (toolCallSignature, checkRepeatedToolCall)
- TaskState.ts: add lastToolParams, consecutiveIdenticalToolCount
- ToolExecutor.ts: call checkRepeatedToolCall in handleCompleteBlock
- responses.ts: add formatResponse.repeatedToolCall
- loop-detection.test.ts: 5 tests

Manually verified: CLI test confirms soft warning at call 3,
YOLO mode failure at call 5.

Refs: #9923, #9916, #9846, #9816

fix: address review feedback on loop detection

- Change hardEscalation threshold from >= to === so it fires exactly
  once at count 5, matching softWarning behavior
- Widen toolCallSignature param type to Partial<Record<string, string>>
  to match actual block.params type from ToolExecutor
- Add negative boundary assertions to verify no false positives at
  calls 0, 1, 3, 4

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: reset loop detection state when user continues after mistake_limit_reached

When hard escalation fired at count === 5 and the user clicked
"continue", consecutiveIdenticalToolCount was never reset. The count
would exceed 5 but === 5 never matched again, silently disabling
loop detection for the rest of the task.

Reset consecutiveIdenticalToolCount, lastToolName, and lastToolParams
alongside the existing consecutiveMistakeCount reset so the detector
fully re-arms after the user continues.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: fix: make Workspace and Favorites history filters independent
body: ### Related Issue **Issue:** #8289 ### Description Fixes a regression from #8228 where the Workspace and Favorites filters in the History view became mutually exclusive. The issue was that both filters were placed inside a `VSCodeRadioGroup`, which enforces that only one radio button can be selected at a time. The fix moves Workspace and Favorites into their own container outside the radio group, allowing them to function as independent toggles while maintaining visual continuity with the sort options. ### Test Procedure 1. Open History view 2. Click "Favorites" filter - should toggle on 3. Click "Workspace" filter - should toggle on (both now active) 4. Click either again - should toggle off independently 5. Verify sort options (Newest, Oldest, etc.) still work correctly 6. title: fix(cli): correct provider model ID key generation for anthropic and separate providers
body: ## Summary Fixes two bugs in provider model ID key handling that were introduced in commit 6e9a4ff0f: - Anthropic provider returned invalid settings key (`actModeapiModelId` instead of `actModeApiModelId`) due to lowercase "a" in ProviderKeyMap - Settings panel used act provider for both act and plan model key lookups, causing incorrect key targeting when providers differ ## Changes 1. Removed `anthropic: "apiModelId"` from ProviderKeyMap so it falls through to the generic `actModeApiModelId`/`planModeApiModelId` keys (as the comment already indicated it should) 2. Updated SettingsPanelContent to derive plan model keys from `planModeApiProvider` instead of `actModeApiProvider`: - Model ID reading in useMemo - Model syncing when disabling separate models - Model selection handler - Manual model ID editing (handleSave) ## Test plan - [ ] Verify Anthropic model selection persists correctly in CLI settings - [ ] Verify changing models with "separate models" enabled writes to correct keys - [ ] Verify model IDs display correctly after selection ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Fixes incorrect provider model ID key handling for Anthropic and ensures correct provider usage in settings panel. > > - **Behavior**: > - Fixes Anthropic provider key mapping by removing `anthropic: "apiModelId"` from `ProviderKeyMap` in `provider-keys.ts`. > - Updates `SettingsPanelContent` in `SettingsPanelContent.tsx` to use `planModeApiProvider` for plan model keys instead of `actModeApiProvider`. > - **Functions**: > - Modifies `useMemo` in `SettingsPanelContent.tsx` to derive model keys using both `actProvider` and `planProvider`. > - Updates model syncing logic when disabling separate models in `SettingsPanelContent.tsx`. > - Adjusts model selection handler and manual model ID editing in `SettingsPanelContent.tsx`. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for e16ce7c263ebe3843bd2d50cbe9db56e494252d1. You can [customize](https://app.ellipsis.dev/cline/settings/summaries) this summary. It will automatically update as commits are pushed.
author: saoudrizwan

commits.message:
fix(cli): correct provider model ID key generation for anthropic and separate providers

Two bugs fixed:

1. getProviderModelIdKey() returned invalid key for Anthropic because
   ProviderKeyMap used "apiModelId" (lowercase "a"), producing
   "actModeapiModelId" instead of "actModeApiModelId". Removed anthropic
   from the map so it falls through to the generic key as intended.

2. Settings panel derived both act/plan model keys from actModeApiProvider.
   If plan and act providers differ, plan model reads/writes targeted wrong
   keys. Now uses planModeApiProvider for plan model key lookups.

title: feat(cli): Fixes CLI authentication redirect error
body: ### Description ### Description Fixes CLI authentication redirect error where the browser would display "Bad request" after successful authentication. The issue occurred because `AuthHandler.ts` attempted to call `getIdeRedirectUri()`, which is not implemented in the CLI's host bridge, causing the auth callback handler to crash despite authentication succeeding. **Changes:** - Added graceful error handling in `AuthHandler.ts` to catch missing `getIdeRedirectUri()` implementation - Made success page messaging platform-aware (shows "terminal" for CLI, "IDE" for VSCode/JetBrains) **Impact:** - CLI users now see a clean success page instead of "Bad request" error - No breaking changes to VSCode authentication flow - Future-proofs for JetBrains support (which will also lack redirect URI) --- ### Test Procedure **Testing Approach:** 1. **CLI Authentication Flow:** - Ran `cline auth` from terminal - Completed OAuth flow in browser - Verified browser shows "Authentication Successful" with "terminal" messaging - Confirmed no "Bad request" error appears - Verified CLI successfully receives auth token and user is authenticated 2. **Backward Compatibility:** - The fix uses a try-catch wrapper around `getIdeRedirectUri()`, so VSCode's existing implementation continues to work - When redirect URI is available (VSCode), page redirects to IDE as before - When redirect URI is unavailable (CLI), page shows success without redirect **Why Ready for Merge:** - Fix is minimal and surgical (only wraps one call in try-catch) - Maintains backward compatibility with existing VSCode flow - Tested end-to-end with CLI authentication - Logging added helps future debugging without affecting functionality --- ### Type of Change - [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality) - [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected) - [ ] ♻️ Refactor Changes - [x] 💅 Cosmetic Changes (platform-specific messaging) - [ ] 📚 Documentation update - [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes --- ### Pre-flight Checklist - [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [ ] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - [ ] I have created a changeset using `npm run changeset` (required for user-facing changes) - [ ] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) --- ### Screenshots --- ### Additional Notes **Root Cause:** The CLI's host bridge (`cli/pkg/hostbridge/`) doesn't implement `getIdeRedirectUri()` because there's no IDE to redirect to - the user is already in their terminal. When `AuthHandler.ts` called this method, it threw an unhandled error that crashed the callback handler, resulting in a "Bad request" response. **Solution Design:** Rather than implementing a stub `getIdeRedirectUri()` in the CLI host bridge, we made the auth handler resilient to missing implementations. This is cleaner because: 1. It doesn't require CLI-specific code in the host bridge 2. It automatically handles future platforms (like JetBrains) that may also lack redirect URIs 3. The HTML generation already supported optional redirects via the ternary operator

title: fix: prevent OOM crash from globby's eager .gitignore scanning in lis…
body:
## fix: prevent OOM crash from eager .gitignore scanning in `listFiles`

### What's crashing

`listFiles` does a recursive BFS directory traversal to populate the file tree shown to the model as context. It uses [globby](https://www.npmjs.com/package/globby) for pattern matching, with `gitignore: true` to automatically exclude gitignored files.

The problem is how globby implements `gitignore: true`: it walks the **entire** directory tree upfront to find every `.gitignore` file, then combines all their patterns into a single regex — **including `.gitignore` files inside directories that are themselves gitignored**.

In projects with large gitignored vendored dependencies (e.g., `vendor/` containing hundreds of sub-repos, each with their own `.gitignore`), globby collects thousands of patterns, builds a massive regex, and V8 runs out of memory trying to compile it. The extension host crashes at ~488MB.

The crash trace confirms this — V8 dies inside `RegExpCompiler::Assemble` → `RegExpMacroAssemblerARM64::GetCode`, triggered by `RegExpPrototypeTestFast` inside an `ArrayForEach` over the pattern array, all kicked off by `node::fs::AfterScanDir` (globby's directory scan). Full trace in the [Appendix](#appendix-crash-dump).

### The fix

Instead of letting globby scan the whole tree for `.gitignore` files upfront, we read them **incrementally during BFS traversal** — only from directories we actually enter:

1. **`gitignore: false`** — disable globby's eager scan entirely
2. **Root `.gitignore` seeded at start** — `buildIgnorePatterns()` reads the root `.gitignore` and adds its patterns to the initial ignore list
3. **Subdirectory `.gitignore` read lazily** — as `globbyLevelByLevel` enters each non-ignored directory, it reads that directory's `.gitignore` (if any) and appends patterns to a growing `currentIgnore` accumulator
4. **Ignored directories never entered** — so their `.gitignore` files are never parsed, and the pattern count stays small

The key insight: the directories causing the blowup (e.g., `vendor/`) are gitignored at the root level. By reading the root `.gitignore` first and respecting it before descending, we never enter `vendor/`, never read its nested `.gitignore` files, and never build the monster regex.

### What changed

**`src/services/glob/list-files.ts`**
- New `readGitignorePatterns(dirPath)` — reads one `.gitignore`, converts patterns to glob ignore format
- `buildIgnorePatterns()` — seeds root `.gitignore` patterns into the initial ignore list
- `listFiles()` — `gitignore: false` replaces `gitignore: recursive`
- `globbyLevelByLevel()` — accumulates ignore patterns from each entered directory's `.gitignore`

**`src/services/glob/__tests__/list-files.test.ts`** — four new tests, each in an isolated subdirectory:
- Root `.gitignore` directory patterns (`ignored-dir/`) exclude contents
- File/glob patterns (`*.log`, `secret.env`) exercise the non-directory parser branch
- Subdirectory `.gitignore` (`src/.gitignore` ignoring `generated/`) works during BFS
- OOM-prevention: `.gitignore` inside gitignored `third-party/` is never read (uses `third-party/` instead of `vendor/` to avoid false positive from `DEFAULT_IGNORE_DIRECTORIES`)

### Known tradeoffs

The custom `.gitignore` parser is intentionally simplified — this is a file listing for context, not a git implementation:

- **Pattern scoping is global**: a pattern from `src/.gitignore` becomes `**/pattern` and could match elsewhere too (real git scopes it to that directory). Unlikely to matter in practice.
- **Negation patterns (`!`) are skipped**: these are rare and complex to convert correctly.
- **Anchored patterns (`/build`) lose their anchor**: treated as `**/build` instead of only matching at the `.gitignore`'s directory level.

### Testing

```bash
npm run test:unit -- --grep "gitignore"
```

---

### Appendix: Crash Dump

commits.message:
fix: prevent OOM crash from globby's eager .gitignore scanning in listFiles

Replace globby's gitignore:true (which reads ALL .gitignore files in the
entire tree upfront, including inside gitignored directories) with
incremental .gitignore reading during BFS traversal.

In projects with large gitignored vendored dependencies containing many
nested repos, globby collects thousands of patterns, builds a massive
regex, and V8 runs out of memory during regex compilation (~488MB).

The fix reads .gitignore files only from directories the BFS actually
enters. Gitignored directories are never entered, so their .gitignore
files are never parsed and the pattern count stays small.

- Set gitignore:false, handle .gitignore ourselves
- Read root .gitignore in buildIgnorePatterns() to seed initial patterns
- Read subdirectory .gitignore lazily during globbyLevelByLevel BFS
- Accumulate patterns in currentIgnore so deeper levels respect them
- Add 4 tests: root patterns, file patterns, subdirectory .gitignore,
  and OOM-prevention (no reading inside gitignored dirs)
Code review followup

Potential fix for code scanning alert no. 147: Incomplete string escaping or encoding

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <62310815+github-advanced-security[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Merge branch 'main' into igor/gitignore-oom

title: fix: add tool-specific error messages for replace_in_file and execute_command
body:
### Related Issue

Reopened from #9685 (closed in error).

### Description

When the model sends `replace_in_file` without `diff` or `execute_command` without `command`, it gets a generic ~30-line error about XML formatting that doesn't help recovery. In SWE-bench evaluation, 11/182 failures (6%) were caused by the model getting stuck in loops of malformed tool calls that never recover.

This PR adds tool-specific error messages for these two tools (following the existing `writeToFileMissingContentError` pattern):
- **`replace_in_file`** missing `diff` — shows the exact SEARCH/REPLACE block format
- **`execute_command`** missing `command` — shows a concrete XML example

The new messages skip the generic `toolUseInstructionsReminder` boilerplate since the model knows the format but dropped a parameter.

### Test Procedure

- Added 11 unit tests verifying both error messages contain the expected tool-specific guidance and do NOT include the generic boilerplate
- Ran `npm run test:unit` — all new tests pass, no regressions

### Type of Change

-   [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)

### Pre-flight Checklist

-   [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore
-   [x] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted
-   [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)


Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)

Replace generic "missing parameter" errors with targeted guidance for
the two tools observed failing most in SWE-bench (6% of failures).
The new messages include the expected format (SEARCH/REPLACE blocks,
XML example) without the 30-line boilerplate reminder.

body:
### Related Issue

title: fix: prevent resume asks from unblocking on abort
body:
### Related Issue

**Issue:** n/a

### Description

This is a follow-up fix to address [this code review comment](https://github.com/cline/cline/pull/9905#discussion_r2966415953).

This fixes a regression in task cancellation/resume behavior introduced by the deadlock fix in `ask()`. Non-resume asks should still unblock immediately when a task is aborted, but `resume_task` / `resume_completed_task` asks need to remain pending so the resume button can still work.

The change keeps the abort wake-up behavior for normal asks while preserving the original wait-for-user-response behavior for resume asks. It also adds targeted tests covering both cases.



### Test Procedure

Here's what I did to verify that the general task cancel, resume, start, close, etc. buttons work:

* Run a task and wait until the task completes and the "Start New Task" button appears.
* Click __New Task__ or the __X/close__ action from that completed task.
   - Expected: the UI should exit that task immediately and not hang.
* Try cancelling a running task and then click the "Resume" button.
   - Expected: the resume button should still work; the task should actually resume rather than doing nothing.

What to look for:

- No frozen/hung task UI after clicking __New Task__ or __close__.
- Resume button, if shown, should not be a dead button.
- After clicking Resume, the task should continue or reopen correctly instead of silently doing nothing.
- No obvious console/runtime errors related to aborting, resuming, or ask handling.


### Type of Change

title: fix: resolve deadlock when clicking New Task/Exit after task completion
body:
The presentation scheduler introduced in ff05ec3bb awaits in-flight
flushes during dispose(), but ask() blocks indefinitely on pWaitFor
waiting for user input. When abortTask() sets abort=true and then
awaits scheduler.dispose(), the in-flight flush (blocked on
ask("completion_result")) never resolves, deadlocking the UI.

Add abort flag check to ask()'s pWaitFor condition so blocked asks
unblock immediately when the task is aborted.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

The presentation scheduler introduced in ff05ec3bb awaits in-flight

reviews.body: The new BYO auth section is potentially misleading around custom `clineDir`. It currently says `cline auth ...` writes credentials to `~/.cline/data/` "or your custom `clineDir`", but the example commands do not show how the CLI is pointed at that same custom directory. I’d either remove the custom-`clineDir` clause here or add the exact mechanism required to target the same directory.
Left one comment.
Thank you!

title: Latency improvements for remote workspaces
body:
### Related Issue

**Issue:** ENG-1612

### Description

This PR improves the user perceived latency of the Cline VSCode extension when using remote workspaces.  In particular, generative file writes are noticeably quicker.


### Test Procedure

To test manually I created a VSIX of the changes from this branch on a remote workspace (I used the smallest instance size in GitHub Codespaces) and installed the VSIX, and then I ran a complex task with lots of generated file write output to observe the user perceived latency in typical use of the extension.

Also, the programmatic tests all pass.

author: candieduniverse

commits.message:
Implement minimal change set for latency improvements

Cline's code review improvements

Further code review improvements

fix: address code review issues for presentation scheduler

- Add reset() method to TaskPresentationScheduler to prevent stale
  timer leaks between API request retries within the same task
- Call presentationScheduler.reset() in streaming state reset section
- Fix hadVisibleAssistantContent to track reasoning content, not just
  text, preventing every reasoning chunk from getting immediate priority
- Remove unused 'low' priority from PresentationPriority type and
  associated cadence configuration (YAGNI)
- Trim TaskLatencyTrigger to only values actually used: text/reasoning/tool
- Add JSDoc to isRemoteWorkspaceEnvironment documenting the heuristic
  fallback and its false-positive risk
- Add Logger.warn for invalid cadence env-var overrides
- Add comment at scheduler construction explaining detection promise
  dependency on remoteWorkspaceDetectionPromise
- Add comments to CLI host bridge about intentional remoteName omission
- Expand test coverage: reset(), coalescing, priority upgrade tests

fix: address code review issues in latency/presentation scheduler

- Fix #1/#3: Set didPresentAnyContent=true for text and tool_calls chunks
  so coalescing actually activates for text-only and tool streams (was
  only set for reasoning chunks, defeating the 40/90ms cadence goal)

- Fix #2: Re-check flushInProgress after awaiting in-flight flush in
  runFlushCycle to prevent two concurrent callers from both proceeding
  past the guard and starting concurrent flushes

- Fix #4: Document flushNow() disposed no-op contract so callers
  understand the finalization guarantee

- Fix #5: Add disposed guard to reset() to prevent post-dispose state
  mutation

- Fix #6: Remove platform/version heuristic from isRemoteWorkspaceEnvironment
  — only use remoteName. The substring match on 'remote' produced false
  positives for version strings like '1.0.0-remote-fix'. Non-VSCode hosts
  should populate remoteName explicitly to opt in to the higher cadence.

- Fix #7: Add remoteWorkspaceDetectionSettled flag and warn if getDelayMs
  is called before detection resolves, making the timing dependency
  explicit and detectable in production logs

- Fix #8: Upgrade disabled-scheduler bypass error from Logger.debug to
  Logger.warn so silent flush failures are visible

- Update latency.test.ts: replace heuristic test with false-positive
  regression tests and add null/empty-field coverage

fix: address code review issues in latency/presentation scheduler

- Fix flushNow race condition: wait for all in-flight flushes to drain
  before setting pendingPriority so the post-flush continuation cannot
  steal it, guaranteeing at least one flush runs after flushNow() returns
- Add regression test for the flushNow race condition
- Fix disabled-path (CLINE_DISABLE_PRESENTATION_SCHEDULER): use
  presentationScheduler.flushNow() instead of a fire-and-forget void
  call so the presentAssistantMessage lock/pending-updates mechanism
  is respected when the scheduler is bypassed
- Rename didPresentAnyContent -> didScheduleAnyContent to accurately
  reflect that content has been scheduled, not necessarily flushed
- Update stale comment in streaming loop to match new variable semantics
- Upgrade remote workspace detection failure log level from debug to warn
- Document reset() interaction with in-flight flush on already-reset state
- Document empty-string remoteName edge case in getHostVersion.ts

refactor: polish presentation scheduler for production readiness

title: feat: move reasoning effort to model config and settings UX
body: ### Related Issue Issue: N/A ### Description ## Summary This PR changes reasoning effort from a global setting into model configuration scoped by mode: - `planModeReasoningEffort` - `actModeReasoningEffort` It updates all affected layers: - storage and proto conversion - controller update paths - provider request construction - webview settings UI - CLI and TUI UX It also removes duplicate model selection UX under the chat input and routes model selection through settings. ## Goals 1. Make reasoning effort mode specific (Plan and Act can differ). 2. Use one reasoning effort concept for GPT and Gemini families where applicable. 3. Keep provider specific mapping in provider/request code. 4. Keep UI behavior consistent across settings surfaces. 5. Prevent request payload conflicts such as sending incompatible reasoning fields together. ## What changed by layer ### 1) Types, storage, proto conversion - Added canonical reasoning effort values in shared storage types. - Added mode-scoped reasoning effort fields in API config flow. - Updated proto conversion and controller update paths to carry these values end to end. Key files: - `src/shared/storage/types.ts` - `src/shared/storage/state-keys.ts` - `src/shared/proto-conversions/models/api-configuration-conversion.ts` - `proto/cline/state.proto` ### 2) Controller and state updates - Added normalization helper for incoming reasoning effort values. - Wired mode-scoped reasoning effort through: - settings update - task settings update - new task initialization - updateApiConfigurationProto flow Key files: - `src/core/controller/state/reasoningEffort.ts` - `src/core/controller/state/updateSettings.ts` - `src/core/controller/state/updateSettingsCli.ts` - `src/core/controller/state/updateTaskSettings.ts` - `src/core/controller/task/newTask.ts` - `src/core/controller/models/updateApiConfigurationProto.ts` ### 3) Provider request construction - Passed mode-scoped reasoning effort into provider handlers. - Updated OpenRouter, Cline, Vercel gateway request assembly to avoid conflicting reasoning fields. - Preserved provider specific behavior for thinking budgets where applicable. - Gemini provider maps `none` to `low` for models that require thinking mode. Key files: - `src/core/api/index.ts` - `src/core/api/providers/openrouter.ts` - `src/core/api/providers/cline.ts` - `src/core/api/providers/vercel-ai-gateway.ts` - `src/core/api/providers/gemini.ts` - `src/core/api/providers/openai.ts` - `src/core/api/providers/openai-native.ts` - `src/core/api/providers/openai-codex.ts` - `src/core/api/transform/openrouter-stream.ts` - `src/core/api/transform/vercel-ai-gateway-stream.ts` ### 4) Webview settings UI - Added shared reasoning effort selector component. - Displayed reasoning effort selector where supported. - Hid thinking budget controls for Gemini and GPT families where reasoning effort applies. - Updated Gemini provider to use reasoning effort selector instead of thinking slider for supported models. Key files: - `webview-ui/src/components/settings/ReasoningEffortSelector.tsx` - `webview-ui/src/components/settings/OpenRouterModelPicker.tsx` - `webview-ui/src/components/settings/VercelModelPicker.tsx` - `webview-ui/src/components/settings/providers/GeminiProvider.tsx` - `webview-ui/src/components/settings/providers/OpenAICompatible.tsx` - `webview-ui/src/components/settings/providers/OpenAINative.tsx` - `webview-ui/src/components/settings/providers/OpenAiCodexProvider.tsx` - `webview-ui/src/components/settings/utils/providerUtils.ts` ### 5) CLI and TUI - Added CLI flag: ```bash --reasoning-effort ``` - Wired flag into current mode state key. - Added TUI setting rows for reasoning effort. - Synced plan and act reasoning/thinking settings when separate models is turned off. Key files: - `cli/src/index.ts` - `cli/src/index.test.ts` - `cli/src/components/SettingsPanelContent.tsx` ### 6) Chat model selection UX cleanup - Removed the mini model picker modal under the chat field. - Model selection now goes through settings where provider-specific controls are visible. Key files: - `webview-ui/src/components/chat/ModelPickerModal.tsx` (removed) - `webview-ui/src/components/chat/ChatTextArea.tsx` - `webview-ui/src/components/chat/chat-view/components/layout/WelcomeSection.tsx` ## Important behavior notes ### Default behavior Reasoning effort defaults now resolve to `low` in fallback paths touched by this PR. ### Gemini behavior For Gemini models that require thinking mode, `none` is coerced to `low` in provider mapping. Reason: - Some Gemini endpoints reject zero-thinking mode with request errors. ### OpenRouter and Vercel gateway behavior Request payload construction avoids conflicting combinations. Before risk: - sending incompatible reasoning fields together After: - effort-based payload when reasoning effort applies - budget-based payload when thinking budget applies - no duplicate conflicting reasoning controls ## Reviewer guide ### Suggested review order 1. Data flow and types 2. Controller update paths 3. API provider request payload logic 4. Webview UI visibility rules 5. CLI/TUI behavior and sync logic 6. Chat model picker removal impact ### Minimal path checks 1. Confirm mode-scoped fields are persisted and loaded. 2. Confirm payload contains expected reasoning config for each provider. 3. Confirm Gemini `none` path does not send invalid zero-thinking config. 4. Confirm OpenRouter and Vercel model pickers show the correct control type. 5. Confirm separate-model toggle sync behavior for model, thinking, reasoning. ## Concrete examples ### Example 1: Cline/OpenRouter GPT with high effort Expected payload shape: ```json { "include_reasoning": true, "reasoning": { "effort": "high" } } ``` ### Example 2: Cline/OpenRouter GPT with none Expected payload shape: ```json { "include_reasoning": false } ``` No effort payload should be present in this case. ### Example 3: Gemini with none in UI Expected internal mapping: ```ts reasoningEffortInput: "none" normalizedReasoningEffort: "low" thinkingLevel: "LOW" ``` ## Non-obvious issues found during implementation 1. Some GPT/Codex variants reject `minimal` and allow only a narrower subset. 2. OpenRouter can reject requests if both legacy and structured reasoning fields are sent together. 3. Gemini thinking requirements differ from other providers and require mapping safeguards. 4. CLI settings sync behavior needed explicit parity fixes when disabling separate mode. ## Scope note This PR is large because the change crosses many integration boundaries. The logic changes are cohesive around one theme: model-scoped reasoning effort. ### Test Procedure ## Automated checks run ```bash npx tsc --noEmit npm run build:webview cd cli && npx vitest run src/index.test.ts ``` ## Manual verification matrix Providers tested: - Cline - OpenRouter - Vercel AI Gateway - Gemini - OpenAI Native - OpenAI Codex Scenarios tested: 1. `none`, `low`, `medium`, `high`, `xhigh` in settings and CLI flag 2. model families with reasoning effort support vs thinking-budget support 3. separate-models toggle on/off sync behavior 4. chat field model-id click path to settings ## Manual payload sanity checks - Verified behavior using temporary local logging during validation phase: - `none` produced no reasoning chunks for Cline/OpenRouter GPT path - `high` produced reasoning chunks/details for the same path - Gemini `none` correctly mapped to low thinking level ### Type of Change - [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [x] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality) - [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected) - [x] ♻️ Refactor Changes - [ ] 💅 Cosmetic Changes - [ ] 📚 Documentation update - [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes ### Pre-flight Checklist - [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [ ] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - [ ] I have created a changeset using `npm run changeset` (required for user-facing changes) - [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Screenshots UI touched in multiple settings surfaces. If helpful, I can add focused screenshots for: - OpenRouter picker with Gemini selected (reasoning effort visible, thinking hidden) - Vercel picker with GPT selected (reasoning effort visible) - Gemini provider settings with reasoning effort selector - TUI settings reasoning effort rows and separate-mode sync behavior ### Additional Notes - Lockfile churn was removed from this branch to keep review scope focused. - Temporary debug instrumentation used during provider validation was removed before final state. ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Moves reasoning effort to model-specific configurations, updating storage, controller, provider requests, and UI components for consistent behavior. > > - **Behavior**: > - Moves reasoning effort to model-specific configurations with `planModeReasoningEffort` and `actModeReasoningEffort`. > - Updates storage, proto conversion, controller paths, provider requests, and UI components. > - Removes duplicate model selection UI under chat input. > - **UI Components**: > - Adds `ReasoningEffortSelector` to `ReasoningEffortSelector.tsx` for selecting reasoning effort. > - Updates `OpenRouterModelPicker.tsx`, `VercelModelPicker.tsx`, and other model pickers to use `ReasoningEffortSelector`. > - Removes `ModelPickerModal.tsx` and updates `ChatTextArea.tsx` to navigate to settings for model selection. > - **Provider Logic**: > - Updates provider request construction to handle mode-scoped reasoning effort. > - Ensures no conflicting reasoning fields in requests for OpenRouter and Vercel. > - **State Management**: > - Updates `ExtensionStateContext.tsx` to manage new reasoning effort fields. > - Adds navigation to settings model picker in `ExtensionStateContext.tsx`. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for 9617bd1cb8b0491213d1569423b764d7ebd96400. You can [customize](https://app.ellipsis.dev/cline/settings/summaries) this summary. It will automatically update as commits are pushed.

## Pull request overview

This PR relocates “reasoning effort” from a global setting into per-mode (plan vs act) model configuration, wires it through webview settings, extension/controller state, API provider request builders, and adds CLI/TUI support. It also removes the chat footer model picker modal in favor of routing model changes through the settings UX.

**Changes:**
- Introduces per-mode reasoning effort fields and a shared normalization helper, and propagates these values into provider request payloads.
- Updates webview settings/provider UIs to show a shared `ReasoningEffortSelector` where supported and removes the legacy/global OpenAI reasoning effort UI.
- Removes the chat footer mini model picker modal and adds CLI/TUI wiring + flag parsing for reasoning effort.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 44 out of 47 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

| ---- | ----------- |
| webview-ui/src/context/ExtensionStateContext.tsx | Removes chat model selector state + legacy global reasoning effort from webview state. |
| webview-ui/src/components/settings/utils/settingsHandlers.ts | Drops proto conversion logic for removed global `openaiReasoningEffort`. |
| webview-ui/src/components/settings/utils/providerUtils.ts | Adds model-id based capability check for reasoning effort (used to toggle UI). |
| webview-ui/src/components/settings/sections/FeatureSettingsSection.tsx | Removes the old global “OpenAI Reasoning Effort” setting UI. |
| webview-ui/src/components/settings/providers/VertexProvider.tsx | Switches Vertex thinking-level UI to shared reasoning effort selector. |
| webview-ui/src/components/settings/providers/OpenAiCodexProvider.tsx | Shows reasoning effort selector when supported for selected Codex model. |

## Pull request overview

This PR updates the Cline API provider’s “free model” detection so it no longer relies on a hardcoded list, instead resolving free model IDs at runtime from the recommended models source (with a static fallback) and normalizing model IDs for consistent comparisons.

**Changes:**
- Added model ID normalization and a free-model ID `Set` derived from `CLINE_RECOMMENDED_MODELS_FALLBACK`.
- Introduced `getFreeModelIdSet()` that refreshes recommended models at runtime with logging + fallback behavior.
- Updated usage/cost handling to use the resolved free-model set (including in the generation-endpoint fallback).


<details>
<summary>Comments suppressed due to low confidence (1)</summary>

**src/core/api/providers/cline.ts:236**
* This change introduces new cost behavior based on dynamically resolved free model IDs (including normalization). There are existing unit tests for `ClineHandler`, but none exercise the free-model path (cost forced to 0 when model is in the free list, and non-zero otherwise), nor the normalization behavior (case/whitespace). Adding focused tests here would help prevent regressions as the recommended models list evolves.
```
					const modelId = this.getModel().id
					const isFreeModel = freeModelIds.has(normalizeModelId(modelId))

					if (isFreeModel) {
						totalCost = 0
					}
```
</details>

title: fix(ui): align ClineRulesToggleModal padding with ServersToggleModal
body:
### Related Issue

**Issue:** N/A (small cosmetic fix)

### Description

The `ClineRulesToggleModal` popup (Rules/Workflows/Hooks/Skills tabs) had several padding inconsistencies compared to the sibling `ServersToggleModal` popup:

1. **Tabs clipping**: The "Skills" tab text was being truncated/clipped at the right edge of the panel on narrow sidebar widths. Reduced tab button horizontal padding from `16px` to `12px`, added `white-space: nowrap`, and `flexWrap: wrap` on the tabs container so they wrap gracefully instead of being clipped.

2. **Container padding mismatch**: The header and scrollable content areas used `px-2` (8px) while `ServersToggleModal` uses `px-3` (12px). Aligned both to `px-3`.

3. **Missing top padding**: The header had `pt-0`, causing tabs to sit flush against the top border with no breathing room. Changed to `pt-2` to match `ServersToggleModal`.

4. **Banner over-padding**: The remote config banner and Windows warning banner used `px-5` (20px) inner padding, causing misalignment with list items below. Reduced to `px-3`.

### Test Procedure

- Launched the Extension Development Host via F5
- Opened the Cline sidebar and clicked the ⚖️ law icon to open the Rules/Workflows/Hooks popup
- Verified all four tabs (Rules, Workflows, Hooks, Skills) are fully visible without clipping
- Confirmed padding is now consistent with the MCP Servers popup
- Checked that the top of the popup has appropriate breathing room
- Tested on a narrow sidebar width to confirm tabs wrap properly

title: feat: add file read deduplication cache to prevent repeated reads
body:
### Related Issue

**Issue:** N/A — This addresses a common behavior observed across models where they repeatedly read the same unchanged files, wasting API tokens and context window space.

### Description

This PR adds a per-task file read deduplication cache that prevents models from endlessly reading the same unchanged files, reducing wasted API tokens and context window consumption.

**What problem does this PR solve?**

Models frequently re-read files they've already read in the same task — sometimes 5+ times for the same file that hasn't changed. Each redundant read wastes API tokens (both input from the tool call and output from the file content) and fills the context window with duplicate information.

**Changes:**
- **TaskState.ts**: Added `fileReadCache: Map<string, { content: string; readCount: number }>` to track file reads per task
- **ReadFileToolHandler.ts**: Checks cache before performing disk I/O — returns cached content on repeat reads. After 3+ reads of the same file, returns a stronger `[DUPLICATE READ DETECTED]` warning instructing the model to use information it already has
- **WriteToFileToolHandler.ts**: Invalidates cache entry when a file is written, so subsequent reads get fresh content
- **ApplyPatchHandler.ts**: Invalidates cache entries for all changed/deleted/moved files after patch application

**How it works:**
1. First read of a file → normal disk read, result cached
2. Second read of same file → returns cached content with `[File already read]` prefix
3. Third+ read → returns cached content with `[DUPLICATE READ DETECTED]` warning
4. Any write/patch to a file → cache entry invalidated, next read is fresh from disk

**Provider compatibility — this is completely provider-agnostic and safe for all providers:**
- The cache operates at the **tool handler level** (`ReadFileToolHandler.execute()`), which is called identically for every provider (Anthropic, OpenRouter, Gemini, Ollama, etc.). No provider-specific code is involved.
- **Cached content is byte-identical to a fresh read.** If a file hasn't changed since the last read, `extractFileContent()` would return the exact same content. The cache simply avoids redundant disk I/O — the model sees the same content either way.
- **Writes properly invalidate the cache.** Any `write_to_file`, `replace_in_file`, or `apply_patch` operation invalidates the affected file's cache entry, so the model always gets fresh content after edits. There's no risk of serving stale data.
- **The cache is per-task scoped** (lives on `TaskState`), so it auto-cleans up when the task ends with no cross-task contamination.
- **Repeated file reads are a universal model behavior**, not specific to any one provider. Any model can get into a loop re-reading files — weaker/smaller models may do it even more frequently. This cache protects all users equally.

### Test Procedure

- Verified the cache correctly returns cached content on second reads of the same file
- Verified that writing to a file (via `write_to_file`) properly invalidates the cache, causing the next read to fetch fresh content from disk
- Verified that applying patches (via `apply_patch`) properly invalidates cache entries for all changed, deleted, and moved files
- Verified the escalating warning at 3+ reads returns the stronger `[DUPLICATE READ DETECTED]` message
- Verified cache keys are case-insensitive (Windows path compatibility)
- Verified no interference with existing file read error handling (try/catch around `extractFileContent` is preserved — errors bypass the cache)

### Type of Change

-   [ ] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
-   [x] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
-   [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected)
-   [ ] ♻️ Refactor Changes
-   [ ] 💅 Cosmetic Changes
-   [ ] 📚 Documentation update
-   [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes

### Pre-flight Checklist

-   [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs)
-   [x] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`)
-   [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Screenshots

N/A — Backend-only change. No UI modifications.

### Additional Notes

- The cache uses case-insensitive absolute paths as keys (`absolutePath.toLowerCase()`) for Windows compatibility
- Only 4 files changed with 41 lines of additions — minimal footprint, easy to review
- The deduplication is transparent to the model — it still receives file content, just served from cache when unchanged
author: mathis1337

commits.message:
feat: add file read deduplication cache to prevent repeated reads

- Add fileReadCache to TaskState for tracking read files per task
- ReadFileToolHandler checks cache before reading, returns cached content on repeat reads
- Warns model after 3+ reads of same file to stop re-reading
- WriteToFileToolHandler and ApplyPatchHandler invalidate cache on file changes
- Reduces wasted API tokens from models reading same files repeatedly
fix: address file read cache gaps - image blocks, execute_command, redundant invalidation

Update src/core/task/tools/handlers/ExecuteCommandToolHandler.ts

Co-authored-by: greptile-apps[bot] <165735046+greptile-apps[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

PR changes

- __`TaskState.ts`__ — Simplified cache type from `{ content: string; readCount: number; imageBlock? }` to `{ readCount: number; mtime: number; imageBlock? }`. Dropped `content` to save memory; added `mtime` for external change detection.

- __`ReadFileToolHandler.ts`__ — Four improvements:

  - __Removed redundant `.set()` call__ — reviewer was correct that objects are modified by reference in Map
  - __Added mtime-based cache validation__ — on cache hit, `stat()` the file and compare mtime. If the file was modified externally (user edited in their editor), the cache entry is evicted and a fresh read occurs
  - __Dropped content from cache__ — cache now only stores metadata (readCount, mtime, imageBlock). On cache hits, the file is re-read from disk, addressing memory concerns
  - __Softened readCount >= 3 warning__ — removed the aggressive "Do NOT read this file again" language; now says "Please use the information you already have and proceed with your task"

title: fix(browser): skip WebP screenshots for GLM and Devstral models (llama.cpp STB doesn't support WebP)
body:
## Summary

`modelDoesntSupportWebp()` only skipped WebP screenshots for Grok models. Users running **GLM** and **Devstral** vision models through a local llama.cpp server hit a 400 error whenever Cline used the browser tool, because llama.cpp's STB image library doesn't support the WebP format.

Closes #8203

## Root cause

```typescript
// Before — only Grok
export function modelDoesntSupportWebp(apiHandlerModel: ApiHandlerModel): boolean {
    const modelId = apiHandlerModel.id.toLowerCase()
    return modelId.includes("grok")
}
```

Browser screenshots default to WebP. llama.cpp decodes images using STB, which has no WebP support. Any vision model running through llama.cpp server (openai-compatible endpoint) will return `400 Failed to load image or audio file` unless we downgrade to PNG.

## Changes

**`src/utils/model-utils.ts`**
- Extend `modelDoesntSupportWebp()` to also return `true` for GLM and Devstral model families, using the existing `isGLMModelFamily()` and `isDevstralModelFamily()` detection functions.
- Update `isGLMModelFamily()` to handle space-separated model IDs like `"GLM 4.6V"` (the format llama.cpp server reports for this model, which previously wasn't matched by the hyphen-based checks).

**`src/utils/__tests__/model-utils.test.ts`**
- Add `describe("isGLMModelFamily")` tests covering both hyphen-separated and space-separated variants.
- Add `describe("modelDoesntSupportWebp")` tests covering Grok, GLM (both formats), Devstral, and WebP-capable models that should not be affected.

## Testing

```
1263 passing (44s), 0 failing
```

All new test cases pass, including the exact model ID from the bug report (`"GLM 4.6V"`).

## What this doesn't fix

This is a targeted fix for the reported model families. A proper long-term solution would add a `supportsWebP` flag to model configuration with a UI checkbox, so any local model user can opt out of WebP regardless of model name. That's tracked as a follow-up (PR 2).

## Models covered

| Model | Reported by | Previously broken |
|---|---|---|
| GLM 4.6V (via llama.cpp) | @ramblingcoder | ✅ |
| GLM 4.5 / GLM-4.6 (via llama.cpp) | @AbirFaisal | ✅ |
| Devstral-Small (via llama.cpp) | @AbirFaisal, @Incongruent | ✅ |
author: ClineXDiego

endpoint) were hitting a 400 error when using the browser tool because Cline
sends screenshots as WebP by default.

like 'GLM 4.6V' (the format llama.cpp server reports for this model).

title: fix(litellm): respect user-configured context window in getModel()
body:
### Related Issue

**Issue:** #9748

### Description

`LiteLlmHandler.getModel()` silently ignores the user-configured model info (`this.options.liteLlmModelInfo`) when building the model info object. The fallback chain was:

```typescript
// Before
const modelInfo = cachedModelInfo || liteLlmModelInfoSaneDefaults
```

`this.options.liteLlmModelInfo` is populated from the stored `planModeLiteLlmModelInfo`/`actModeLiteLlmModelInfo` settings — this is where users set custom context windows (e.g. 1M tokens). The StateManager cache (`cachedModelInfo`) is never populated for LiteLLM models in practice (no `setModelInfo("liteLlm", ...)` call exists in the codebase), so every call always fell through to `liteLlmModelInfoSaneDefaults` which has `contextWindow: 128_000`.

**Effect:** The `ContextManager` reads context window size from `getModel()`. Users who configured 1M context window saw 88% auto-condense trigger at ~112K tokens (88% of 128K) instead of ~880K tokens (88% of 1M). The UI displayed the correct 1M value but the compaction logic used 128K.

**Fix:** One line — add `this.options.liteLlmModelInfo` to the fallback chain:

```typescript
// After
const modelInfo = cachedModelInfo || this.options.liteLlmModelInfo || liteLlmModelInfoSaneDefaults
```

The priority is: StateManager cache (from LiteLLM server) → user-configured model info → hardcoded defaults.

### Test Procedure

Added three new unit tests to `litellm.test.ts` covering `getModel()`:

1. **Returns sane defaults** when no `liteLlmModelInfo` option is provided (existing behavior preserved)
2. **Returns user-configured model info** when `liteLlmModelInfo` is provided and no StateManager cache exists (the bug fix)
3. **Prefers StateManager cache** over user-configured `liteLlmModelInfo` (cache still wins)

All 3 new tests pass. Full test suite: 1255 passing, 0 failures, 4 pending (run with `TS_NODE_TRANSPILE_ONLY=true` to bypass unrelated WANDB proto type error on current main).

No existing tests were broken. The change only affects the fallback order — if StateManager cache is populated (future scenario), it still takes precedence.

### Type of Change

-   [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)

### Pre-flight Checklist

-   [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs)
-   [x] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`)
-   [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Screenshots

No UI changes. The fix is in provider logic.

**Root cause trace:**
- `LiteLlmHandler.getModel()` → returns `liteLlmModelInfoSaneDefaults` (128K) instead of user config
- `getContextWindowInfo(api)` → reads `api.getModel().info.contextWindow` → gets 128K
- `ContextManager` compaction threshold → `128K * 0.88 = 112K` (triggers around ~100K tokens)
- **After fix:** `getModel()` returns `this.options.liteLlmModelInfo` → gets 1M → threshold = 880K ✓

### Additional Notes

The comment above the fixed line already described the intended behavior: *"Fall back to provided model info or defaults if not in cache"* — but the implementation only fell back to defaults, not provided model info. This was a simple oversight.

This same bug may affect the OCA provider (`oca.ts`) which has a similar pattern (`this.options.ocaModelInfo || liteLlmModelInfoSaneDefaults` — already correct there).

title: fix(wandb): honor explicit model IDs outside static catalog
body:
### Related Issue

**Issue:** #XXXX

### Description

This PR fixes a W&B provider model resolution bug where explicitly configured model IDs outside the static `wandbModels` catalog were silently replaced by the default model.

Before this change:
- `WandbHandler.getModel()` fell back to `meta-llama/Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct` when `apiModelId` was not in the static list.
- Example impact: `cline auth -p wandb -m moonshotai/Kimi-K2.5` did not actually use `moonshotai/Kimi-K2.5`.

After this change:
- Known catalog IDs still use their catalog metadata.
- Unknown but explicitly configured IDs now pass through as-is.
- Omitted model IDs still fall back to the existing W&B default.
- Added unit tests for known, unknown, and unset model-id paths.

### Test Procedure

- Ran proto generation:
  - `npm run protos`
- Ran unit tests for this area:
  - `TS_NODE_PROJECT=./tsconfig.unit-test.json ./node_modules/.bin/mocha src/core/api/providers/__tests__/wandb.test.ts`
  - Result: passing (the repo's mocha config executed the unit suite; 1263 passing).
- Manual validation done before and after patch:
  - Reproduced that unknown W&B model IDs previously fell back to the default model.
  - Confirmed explicit unknown IDs now resolve to the configured model ID.

[Your team has set up Codex to review pull requests in this repo](http://chatgpt.com/codex/settings/general). Reviews are triggered when you - Open a pull request for review - Mark a draft as ready - Comment "@codex review". If Codex has suggestions, it will comment; otherwise it will react with 👍. Codex can also answer questions or update the PR. Try commenting "@codex address that feedback".

## Pull request overview

Refreshes Cline’s curated Fireworks model defaults/list to align with currently serverless-supported Fireworks model IDs, keeping the UI default selection, shared config, and provider docs in sync.

**Changes:**
- Updated the Fireworks default model ID to `accounts/fireworks/models/kimi-k2p5`.
- Replaced deprecated Fireworks curated model IDs with the new supported serverless model set and refreshed their metadata/pricing.
- Updated Fireworks provider docs and the settings UI test assertion to match the new default.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated 4 comments.

| File | Description |

| `webview-ui/src/components/settings/__tests__/APIOptions.spec.tsx` | Updates the Fireworks default model expectation in the settings UI test. |
| `docs/provider-config/fireworks.mdx` | Updates Fireworks provider documentation to match the new curated model list/default. |
