title: fix: Claude Code provider failing with 4.6 models and newer CLI versions
body: - Update --disallowedTools list to match current Claude Code CLI tools (12 new tools were unblocked, causing models to use native tool_use instead of Cline's XML tools) - Fix rate_limit_event handling for new CLI format (top-level type instead of system subtype) - Handle unknown content block types and new message types gracefully - Fix assistantHasContent check to account for tool calls accumulated via toolUseHandler even when useNativeToolCalls is false ### Related Issue **Issue:** #9723 ### Type of Change - [X ] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality) - [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected) - [ ] ♻️ Refactor Changes - [ ] 💅 Cosmetic Changes - [ ] 📚 Documentation update - [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes ### Pre-flight Checklist - [X ] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [ ] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - [ ] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Screenshots Everything seems good with it. Good news is I did get output and... ...Can also confirm my Claude Code usage is going up: Asked multiple follow up questions and everything also seemed fined: Act also worked: ## PR Summary ### Fix Claude Code provider 100% failure with Claude 4.6 models __Problem:__ Claude Code provider returns "Invalid API Response: The provider returned an empty or unparsable response" for all requests when using Claude Sonnet 4.6 or Opus 4.6 models. The issue started when these models were released alongside Claude Code CLI v2.1+. __Root Cause:__ Multiple issues compounded: 1. __Stale `--disallowedTools` list__ (primary cause): The hardcoded tool names in `run.ts` were outdated. 12 new Claude Code CLI tools (`TaskOutput`, `ToolSearch`, `AskUserQuestion`, `ExitPlanMode`, etc.) were not being blocked, so 4.6 models would prefer Claude Code's native tools over Cline's XML tools — producing `tool_use` blocks with no text, triggering the "empty response" error. 2. __Unhandled `rate_limit_event` format__: The CLI now emits `{"type":"rate_limit_event",...}` as a top-level type, but the handler only recognized `{"type":"system","subtype":"rate_limit_event",...}`. Unrecognized chunks could interfere with partial data assembly in the stream parser. 3. __`assistantHasContent` check too narrow__: When the model responded with only thinking + `tool_use` blocks (no text), `assistantMessage` stayed empty and `useNativeToolCalls` was false (Claude Code doesn't use API tool definitions), causing the check to incorrectly conclude no content was received. __Changes:__ - __`src/integrations/claude-code/run.ts`__: Updated `claudeCodeTools` disallow list to include all current CLI tools (Task, TaskOutput, Bash, Glob, Grep, Read, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit, WebFetch, TodoWrite, WebSearch, TaskStop, AskUserQuestion, Skill, EnterPlanMode, ExitPlanMode, EnterWorktree, ExitWorktree, CronCreate, CronDelete, CronList, ToolSearch) - __`src/integrations/claude-code/types.ts`__: Fixed `RateLimitEvent` type to match actual CLI output format; added `UserMessage` type and `error` field on `AssistantMessage` - __`src/core/api/providers/claude-code.ts`__: Added explicit handling for `rate_limit_event`, `user` messages, `error` field on assistant messages, unknown content block types (with logging + text fallback), and unrecognized chunk types - __`src/core/task/index.ts`__: Extended `assistantHasContent` check to include `toolUseHandler.getAllFinalizedToolUses().length > 0` as a safety net __Testing:__ Diagnosed by running `claude --output-format stream-json --model claude-sonnet-4-6` directly and comparing CLI output against handler expectations. Confirmed tool names changed, new message types added, and format evolved.
author: mathis1337

commits.message:
fix: Claude Code provider failing with 4.6 models and newer CLI versions

- Update --disallowedTools list to match current Claude Code CLI tools
  (12 new tools were unblocked, causing models to use native tool_use
  instead of Cline's XML tools)
- Fix rate_limit_event handling for new CLI format (top-level type
  instead of system subtype)
- Handle unknown content block types and new message types gracefully
- Fix assistantHasContent check to account for tool calls accumulated
  via toolUseHandler even when useNativeToolCalls is false
resolved .include mismatch to .containEql

Update src/integrations/claude-code/types.ts

Co-authored-by: greptile-apps[bot] <165735046+greptile-apps[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Removed `LegacyRateLimitEvent` type and its union reference from `types.ts`
Merge branch 'main' into claudecodeissue
Merge branch 'main' into claudecodeissue
Fixed loop with async tool calls in new claude code.

PR review feedback fixes

__Fix #2 (claude-code.ts):__ Cleaned up the error field check — replaced verbose `"error" in message` guard + ternary chain with a simpler `message.error` check using optional chaining and nullish coalescing (`message.content?.[0]` + `?? fallback`).

__Fix #3 (claude-code.ts):__ Replaced `message.content.length > 0 ? message.content[0] : undefined` with `message.content?.[0]` using optional chaining for the `stop_reason` block.

__Fix #4 (claude-code.ts):__ Replaced repeated `(content as any)` casts in the `default` switch case with a single typed cast: `const unknownBlock = content as { type: string; text?: string }`, making the code cleaner and safer.

__Fix #5 (ApplyPatchHandler.ts):__ Replaced both `await import("node:path")` and `require("node:path")` dynamic imports with a static `import { resolve as resolvePath } from "node:path"` at the top of the file.
Remove file read deduplication feature (moved to separate PR)
Remove ReadFileToolHandler file-not-found test (moved to separate PR)
Add LegacyRateLimitEvent type for older CLI format
Restore ReadFileToolHandler.ts and test from upstream/main (fix stale local main revert)
Revert ReadFileToolHandler.ts to match fork main (no try/catch, no test file)
manually reverting back

Update src/core/api/providers/claude-code.ts

Restore ReadFileToolHandler.ts and test to match cline/cline upstream main

title: [Aikido] Fix 3 security issues in dompurify, preact, mermaid and 2 more
body:
Upgrade dependencies to fix XSS in DOMPurify, HTML injection in Preact, and prototype pollution in Lodash.
✅ 3 CVEs resolved by this upgrade
This PR will resolve the following CVEs: | Issue | Severity           | Description | | --- | --- | --- | | 
[CVE-2026-0540](https://app.aikido.dev/issues/23372247/detail?groupId=39595#CVE-2026-0540)
| 
MEDIUM
| [dompurify] A cross-site scripting vulnerability allows attackers to bypass attribute sanitization by exploiting missing rawtext elements in the SAFE_FOR_XML regex, enabling JavaScript execution through crafted payloads in attribute values when output is placed in vulnerable contexts. | | 
[CVE-2026-22028](https://app.aikido.dev/issues/19573793/detail?groupId=39595#CVE-2026-22028)
| 
MEDIUM
| [preact] A regression weakened JSON serialization protection, allowing specially-crafted JSON payloads to be treated as valid VNodes, enabling HTML injection and arbitrary script execution in applications passing unsanitized user data directly to the render tree. | | 
[CVE-2025-13465](https://app.aikido.dev/issues/20124551/detail?groupId=39595#CVE-2025-13465)
| 
MEDIUM
| [lodash-es] A prototype pollution vulnerability in _.unset and _.omit functions allows attackers to delete methods from global prototypes via crafted paths. While this prevents property overwriting, it can cause denial of service by removing critical functionality. |

title: Fix Windows CLI tests related to `/q` and `/exit`
body: ### Related Issue **Issue:** n/a ### Description Fix flaky/failing CLI unit tests on Windows CI by removing timing-sensitive slash-command behavior and stabilizing related test coverage. This makes CLI-only slash commands (including `/q` and `/exit`) available synchronously in `ChatView`, improves slash-command matching so exact/prefix matches win over fuzzy matches, and extracts standalone slash-command resolution into testable utilities. As follow-up cleanup, the branch also hardens several CLI tests (`ChatView`, `SkillsPanelContent`, quit/slash command tests) so the full `cli` Vitest suite now passes reliably on Windows. Fixes this error: ### Test Procedure #### Verification - `cd cli && npm run typecheck` - `cd cli && npm run test:run -- src/components/QuitCommand.test.tsx src/utils/slash-commands.test.ts src/components/SkillsPanelContent.test.tsx src/components/ChatView.test.tsx` - `cd cli && npm run test:run` With these changes, the full CLI test suite is green locally on Windows. ### Type of Change - [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality) - [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected) - [ ] ♻️ Refactor Changes - [ ] 💅 Cosmetic Changes - [ ] 📚 Documentation update - [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes ### Pre-flight Checklist - [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [x] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Screenshots n/a ### Additional Notes n/a

title: Export global, workspace and secrets from VSCode to share with CLI
body: ### Description Adds a step to VSCode extension startup which exports global state, workspace state, and secrets to the same files that CLI and JetBrains use. Changes VSCode, CLI and JetBrains to use a common storage backend called StorageContext that sits below StateManager. Caveats: - This doesn't watch the filesystem for updates, and clients have a local cache of settings, so settings overwrite fights may result if you try to edit settings thru multiple Clines at once. Future work. - JetBrains writes its workspace file at a different path, so these won't 100% interoperate yet. ### Test Procedure Specific tests for the VSCode state migration: ``` npm run test:unit -- --grep 'vscode-to-file-migration|createStorageContext' ``` ...but this integrates the CLI, VSCode and JetBrains storage backends, so additionally: ``` npm run test:unit pushd cli && npx vitest run && popd ``` Manual test plan, check that basic VSCode functions work: 1. Run VSCode. Note you will have ~/.cline/data/workspaces/0000002f/workspaceState.json (2f is the hash of / because VSCode's cwd is / if you don't have a workspace open and we use that.) 2. Open or create a project. Check you now have ~/.cline/data/workspaces//workspaceState.json . 3. In the chat window, scales of justice, add a new workspace Cline rule. Close the popup. cat the workspaceState.json file from step 2 and see that the details are persisted there. 4. Edit the workspace Cline rule .md to tell Cline to speak like a pirate. 5. `cd cli && npm run build && cd && node /cli/dist/cli.mjs` to run CLI 6. Chat to Cline, observe that it speaks like a pirate. Check that migration works: 1. Open a project in VSCode that has Cline rules settings which you've configured in an old version of Cline. 2. Observe that a .cline/data/workspaces//workspaceSettings.json file is created when you open the project, and it is populated with your settings. Check that global state migration happens, and secrets are stored (basically) securely: 1. Delete ~/.cline/data/globalState.json and ~/.cline/data/secrets.json (if you have them... may want to back these up.) 2. Run VSCode where you have been using Cline. 3. Check that ~/.cline/data/globalState.json exists and is full of stuff. 4. `ls -ald ~/.cline/data/secrets.json` and check it is -rw------, that is, only the user can read and write it; cat the file and see the glittering jewels are there. ### Type of Change - [X] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality) - [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected) - [X] ♻️ Refactor Changes - [ ] 💅 Cosmetic Changes - [ ] 📚 Documentation update - [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes ### Pre-flight Checklist - [X] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [X] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - [X] I have created a changeset using `npm run changeset` (required for user-facing changes) - [X] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Introduces a shared file-backed storage system for VSCode, CLI, and JetBrains, migrating existing VSCode storage to this new system and updating state management accordingly. > > - **Behavior**: > - Adds a migration step in `vscode-to-file-migration.ts` to export VSCode's `globalState`, `workspaceState`, and `secrets` to shared file-backed storage. > - Introduces `StorageContext` in `storage-context.ts` as a common storage backend for VSCode, CLI, and JetBrains. > - Updates `StateManager` in `StateManager.ts` to use `StorageContext` for all persistent storage operations. > - **Migration**: > - Implements migration logic in `vscode-to-file-migration.ts` with sentinels to track migration status. > - Ensures existing data in file-backed storage is not overwritten during migration. > - Adds tests in `vscode-to-file-migration.test.ts` to verify migration behavior and idempotency. > - **Refactoring**: > - Refactors `ClineFileStorage` in `ClineFileStorage.ts` to support batch operations and file permissions. > - Removes direct use of `ExtensionContext` for storage in favor of `StorageContext`. > - **Misc**: > - Updates `initializeCliContext` in `vscode-context.ts` to create and use `StorageContext`. > - Modifies `initialize` function in `common.ts` to accept `StorageContext`. > - Adjusts `openAiCodexOAuthManager` in `oauth.ts` to remove dependency on `ExtensionContext`. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for ea3ba0bf3c052fea3ec622d37b1d3f3a51bebd9e. ## Pull request overview

This PR unifies persistent storage across VSCode, CLI, and JetBrains by introducing a shared file-backed `StorageContext` and migrating VSCode’s legacy `ExtensionContext`-backed storage into the shared `~/.cline/data/` JSON files at startup.

**Changes:**
- Introduces `StorageContext` (`globalState`, `workspaceState`, `secrets`) backed by JSON files under `~/.cline/data/`.
- Updates `StateManager` (and CLI/standalone entrypoints) to initialize from `StorageContext` and persist via batched file writes.
- Adds a VSCode one-time export/migration from `ExtensionContext` storage into shared files, with unit tests and new storage architecture docs.

### Reviewed changes

title: fix: consolidate Parallel tool-calling fixes
body:
### Related Issue

**Issue:** #XXXX (supersedes #9727 and #9728)

### Description

This PR consolidates and supersedes:

- #9727 (OpenRouter Gemini 3 parallel tool-calling enablement)
- #9728 (Gemini 3 RULES parallel guidance)

It also addresses the unresolved review feedback from both PRs:

1. **Respect user setting at API level**
   - Thread `enableParallelToolCalling` through API configuration into OpenRouter/Cline handlers.
   - Set `parallel_tool_calls` directly from the setting (model-family agnostic), so behavior is consistent across supported OpenRouter/Cline models.

2. **Make streaming parser safe for interleaved parallel deltas**
   - Refactor `ToolCallProcessor` to track tool-call state by `index` instead of a single shared `lastToolCall`.
   - Prevent id/name cross-association when multiple tool calls stream in parallel.

3. **Keep Gemini 3 RULES parallel guidance and add coverage**
   - Add the Gemini 3 RULES batching instruction behind `context.enableParallelToolCalling`.
   - Add a dedicated Gemini 3 snapshot fixture for `enableParallelToolCalling: true`.

4. **Unrelated stale snapshot follow-up**
   - Refresh `vertex_gemini3.tools.snap` in a separate commit for review clarity.

### Test Procedure

1. Ran:

2. Ran:

title: fix gemini toolcall id collision
body:
### Related Issue

**Issue:** Follow-up fix for [#9738](https://github.com/cline/cline/pull/9738)

### Description

This PR fixes a Gemini-native tool-calling bug where multiple function calls in the same Gemini response could collide on tool-call ID.

Root cause:
- In `GeminiHandler`, emitted tool calls used `chunk.responseId` as the tool-call ID.
- Gemini can emit multiple `functionCall` parts under one `responseId`.
- When IDs collide, downstream tool-call aggregation (keyed by tool-call ID) can merge/overwrite calls, causing argument/name contamination and dropped calls.

Fix:
- In [`src/core/api/providers/gemini.ts`](src/core/api/providers/gemini.ts), use `functionCall.id` when Gemini provides one.
- If `functionCall.id` is absent, generate stable per-response synthetic IDs:
  - `<responseId>-tool-0`, `<responseId>-tool-1`, ...
- Set both `tool_call.call_id` and `tool_call.function.id` to that unique ID.

Scope:
- Provider-specific fix only (Gemini handler).
- No shared `ToolCallProcessor` changes in this PR.

Tests added:
- [`src/core/api/providers/__tests__/gemini.test.ts`](src/core/api/providers/__tests__/gemini.test.ts)
  - verifies multiple function calls sharing one response produce distinct IDs
  - verifies Gemini-provided `functionCall.id` is preserved

### Test Procedure

#### 1) Unit tests (new coverage)

```bash
npx cross-env TS_NODE_PROJECT=./tsconfig.unit-test.json mocha --grep "GeminiHandler" src/core/api/providers/__tests__/gemini.test.ts
```

Result:
- 4 passing

#### 2) Real CLI validation (Gemini native provider)

```bash
npm run cli:build
node cli/dist/cli.mjs auth -p gemini -k "$GEMINI_API_KEY" -m gemini-2.5-flash --config /tmp/cline-gemini-idfix-config-l8yDzL
node cli/dist/cli.mjs task "Read alpha.txt and beta.txt using separate read_file tool calls in parallel when possible, then output exactly PARALLEL_DONE." --config /tmp/cline-gemini-idfix-config-l8yDzL -c /tmp/cline-gemini-idfix-work-B5zIVh --yolo --act --timeout 240 --json
```

Artifact:
- `/tmp/cline-gemini-idfix-config-l8yDzL/data/tasks/1773243726285/api_conversation_history.json`

Observed tool-call IDs:
- `To2xaZOcLYmb-sAPy9HW8Ag-tool-0` (`read_file`)
- `To2xaZOcLYmb-sAPy9HW8Ag-tool-1` (`read_file`)

Validation outcome:
- multiple tool calls in one response path are uniquely identified
- no ID collision (`HAS_DUPES=false`)
- task completed successfully (`PARALLEL_DONE`)

#### 3) Non-Gemini control runs (to show no obvious regression in this patch)

OpenRouter (GPT-4o-mini):
- config: `/tmp/cline-openrouter-control-config-WaKKJI`
- task artifact: `/tmp/cline-openrouter-control-config-WaKKJI/data/tasks/1773243790919/api_conversation_history.json`
- outcome: task completed successfully (`PARALLEL_DONE`)

OpenRouter (Anthropic model):
- config: `/tmp/cline-openrouter-anthropic-config-Tuygul`
- task artifact: `/tmp/cline-openrouter-anthropic-config-Tuygul/data/tasks/1773243917970/api_conversation_history.json`
- outcome: task completed successfully (`PARALLEL_DONE`)

Note:
- Direct Anthropic provider test attempted but blocked by account credits (`invalid_request_error: credit balance is too low`).

### Type of Change

-   [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
-   [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
-   [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected)

title: fix: catch errors in path-based tool handlers instead of crashing
body:
## Problem

`ListCodeDefinitionNamesToolHandler`, `ListFilesToolHandler`, and `SearchFilesToolHandler` let exceptions from their core operations (`parseSourceCodeForDefinitionsTopLevel`, `listFiles`, `determineSearchPaths`) propagate through `ToolExecutor`'s re-throw path, crashing the CLI process. This is the same class of bug fixed for `ReadFileToolHandler` in #9730.

When a model hallucinates a bad path, the unhandled exception terminates the task instead of letting the model see the error and try a different path.

Additionally, `consecutiveMistakeCount` was reset to 0 **before** the core operation, so repeated failures could never accumulate toward the yolo-mode mistake limit.

## Fix

For each of the three handlers:

- Wrap the core operation in `try`/`catch`, returning `formatResponse.toolError()` on failure so the model receives a clean error and can recover gracefully.
- Move `consecutiveMistakeCount = 0` to **after** a successful operation so repeated failures accumulate correctly.
- Increment `consecutiveMistakeCount` on caught errors.

## Test plan

```bash
npx cross-env TS_NODE_PROJECT=./tsconfig.unit-test.json mocha --grep "error recovery" --timeout 30000
```

13 end-to-end tests exercise the actual handlers with a mock `TaskConfig` (following the `SubagentToolHandler.test.ts` pattern):

- Non-existent paths return a tool result, not a thrown exception
- File paths passed to directory-only tools return graceful messages
- Missing parameters increment `consecutiveMistakeCount`
- Repeated missing-param failures accumulate across calls
- Successful operations reset `consecutiveMistakeCount` to 0

- Wrap the core operation in try/catch, returning formatResponse.toolError()
  on failure so the model can see the error and recover gracefully.
- Move consecutiveMistakeCount reset to after a successful operation so
  repeated failures accumulate toward the yolo-mode mistake limit.
- Increment consecutiveMistakeCount on caught errors.

Add end-to-end tests exercising each handler with a mock TaskConfig,
covering: non-existent paths, missing parameters, failure accumulation,
and success-based counter reset.

address review: expand try/catch scope, add stub-based tests

- Include resolveWorkspacePath inside try/catch in
  ListCodeDefinitionNamesToolHandler and ListFilesToolHandler (matching
  SearchFilesToolHandler's pattern) so path resolution failures are
  also caught gracefully.
- Fix trivially-true assertion in file-not-a-directory test.
- Add 6 new stub-based tests that force core operations to throw:
  parseSourceCodeForDefinitionsTopLevel, listFiles, and
  determineSearchPaths — verifying the catch paths return
  formatResponse.toolError() and increment consecutiveMistakeCount.
- Total: 19 passing tests (up from 13).

address review: move clineignore check before IO in ListFilesToolHandler

Move the .clineignore access validation before resolveWorkspacePath and
listFiles so blocked paths are rejected without incurring IO cost.
Also ensures consecutiveMistakeCount is only reset after all
validations and the core operation succeed.

address review: increment counter on clineignore denial

Clineignore denial in ListFilesToolHandler now increments
consecutiveMistakeCount so repeated attempts at blocked paths
accumulate toward the yolo-mode mistake limit. Added 2 tests
verifying single and repeated clineignore denials.
Total: 21 passing tests.

fix: increment consecutiveMistakeCount when SearchFilesToolHandler searches fail

Previously, SearchFilesToolHandler's executeSearch() caught regexSearchFiles
errors and returned {success: false}, but the handler unconditionally reset
consecutiveMistakeCount to 0 even when ALL searches failed. This contradicted
the PR's goal of accumulating failures toward the yolo-mode mistake limit.

Now we check if any search succeeded before resetting the counter:
- If at least one search succeeded: reset to 0 (existing behavior for successes)
- If all searches failed: increment the counter (new fix)

Also added comprehensive test coverage for this scenario, including tests for:
- regexSearchFiles throwing errors
- Repeated search failures accumulating
- Successful search resetting the counter after failures

fix: detect error strings in ListCodeDefinitionNamesToolHandler

parseSourceCodeForDefinitionsTopLevel returns error strings instead of
throwing exceptions for file paths and non-existent directories. The
handler now detects these error conditions and increments
consecutiveMistakeCount so repeated failures accumulate correctly.

This addresses Greptile's feedback that the counter was unconditionally
resetting to 0 for all real-world failure modes of this handler.

title: fix: catch file-not-found in ReadFileToolHandler instead of crashing CLI
body:
## Problem

When the model asks to `read_file` a path that doesn't exist (for example, a hallucinated path), `extractFileContent` throws `"File not found: …"`. Because `ReadFileToolHandler.execute()` has no try/catch around this call, the exception propagates up to `ToolExecutor` which displays and rethrows the error, causing CLI to exit with a status code.

I noticed this causing a failure in astropy__astropy-13977 with openrouter:google/gemini-3-flash-preview when the model makes some file path mistakes.

## Fix

Return read_file errors as tool failures that bump the consecutive failure count, instead of hard failing immediately.

## Test Plan

```bash
npm run test:unit -- --grep "ReadFileToolHandler"
```
author: dominiccooney

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR aims to prevent the CLI from crashing when `read_file` is invoked with a non-existent path by handling `extractFileContent()` failures directly inside `ReadFileToolHandler.execute()` and returning a tool error string instead of letting the exception propagate to `ToolExecutor`’s re-throw.

**Changes:**
- Add a try/catch around `extractFileContent()` in `ReadFileToolHandler.execute()` to return `formatResponse.toolError(...)` on failure.
- Increment `consecutiveMistakeCount` when the read fails (intended to keep YOLO mistake limiting functional).
- Add a new unit test file covering the file-not-found scenario.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 5 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `src/core/task/tools/handlers/ReadFileToolHandler.ts` | Catch `extractFileContent()` errors and return a tool error response instead of throwing. |
| `src/core/task/tools/handlers/__tests__/ReadFileToolHandler.fileNotFound.test.ts` | Add tests intended to validate non-existent path handling and mistake counter behavior. |





---

You can also share your feedback on Copilot code review. [Take the survey](https://www.surveymonkey.com/r/XP6L3XJ).

commits.message:
fix: catch extractFileContent errors in ReadFileToolHandler

When extractFileContent throws (e.g. file not found), the exception
propagated through ToolExecutor which re-threw it, crashing the CLI
process with exit code 1.

Now file read errors are caught and returned as formatResponse.toolError()
so the model can see the error and recover gracefully (e.g. try a
different file path) instead of terminating the entire task.

Also increments consecutiveMistakeCount so the yolo-mode mistake limit
still functions correctly.

test: add unit test for read_file file-not-found graceful recovery

Verifies that when extractFileContent throws (e.g. non-existent path),
the error is caught and returned as formatResponse.toolError() instead
of propagating as an unhandled exception. Also verifies that
consecutiveMistakeCount is incremented for yolo-mode limit tracking.

address review: move reset to success path, add type, normalize message, fix tests

Addresses Copilot and Greptile review comments:

Handler (ReadFileToolHandler.ts):
- Add explicit FileContentResult type for fileContent variable
- Move consecutiveMistakeCount = 0 from before the read to after a
  successful read, so repeated file-not-found errors accumulate
- Normalize error message to avoid double 'Error reading file:' prefix

Tests (ReadFileToolHandler.fileNotFound.test.ts):
- Use os.tmpdir() + path.join() for cross-platform temp paths
- Fix accumulation test to reflect actual handler semantics
- Replace vacuous identity test with real file read + counter reset

test: remove self-fulfilling counter tests, keep only real assertions

The 'accumulate' and 'reset' tests were incrementing/resetting
consecutiveMistakeCount in the test itself and then asserting on
what they just set — proving nothing about the handler. Removed
them. The remaining two tests exercise extractFileContent and
formatResponse.toolError with real calls.

test: rewrite as end-to-end handler test with mock TaskConfig

Actually calls ReadFileToolHandler.execute() with a mock TaskConfig
(following SubagentToolHandler.test.ts pattern), real ToolValidator,
real extractFileContent, and a real temp directory. Asserts on:

- Non-existent file returns tool error string, not thrown exception
- consecutiveMistakeCount increments on each failure without reset
- 3 consecutive failures accumulate to count=3
- Successful read of a real file resets count to 0
- Missing path param increments count via sayAndCreateMissingParamError

test: fix test failures - stub isLocatedInWorkspace, disable hooks

- Stub isLocatedInWorkspace to avoid HostProvider initialization
- Set hooksEnabled=false to skip PreToolUse hook (avoids StateManager)
- Remove telemetryService stub (isSubagentExecution skips telemetry)

All 4 tests pass.

title: feat(telemetry): add provider to task.tokens event
body:
### Related Issue

**Issue:** DAT-16

### Description

The `captureTokenUsage` method (which emits the `task.tokens` telemetry event) was missing a `provider` field, even though the sibling method `captureConversationTurnEvent` (which emits `task.conversation_turn`) already includes it. This meant the dbt analytics pipeline (`silver_token_usage_daily`) couldn't populate the `inference_provider` column from `task.tokens` events.

This is a simple plumbing fix:
- Added `provider` as a required parameter to `captureTokenUsage()` in `TelemetryService.ts`
- Included `provider` in both the event properties and all metric attributes (counters + histograms)
- DRY-ed up the method by extracting a shared `attributes` object instead of repeating `{ ulid, model }` inline ~10 times
- Passed the existing `providerId` variable at the call site in `src/core/task/index.ts`

### Test Procedure

- **Unit tests:** All 4 existing `captureTokenUsage` tests updated with the new `provider` parameter and passing: `npm run test:unit -- --grep "captureTokenUsage"` (4 passing)
- **Compile check:** `npm run compile` passes cleanly with no TypeScript errors
- **Type safety:** The new `provider` parameter is required, so TypeScript would catch any callers that don't pass it. Searched the codebase — the only call site is in `src/core/task/index.ts` which is updated.
- **No runtime risk:** This only adds a field to telemetry payloads — no behavioral change to the extension. Existing downstream consumers that don't use `provider` are unaffected.

### Type of Change

-   [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
-   [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
-   [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected)
-   [x] ♻️ Refactor Changes
-   [ ] 💅 Cosmetic Changes
-   [ ] 📚 Documentation update
-   [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes

### Pre-flight Checklist

-   [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs)
-   [x] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`)
-   [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Screenshots

N/A — backend telemetry change only, no UI impact.

### Additional Notes

The `providerId` was already available at the call site (destructured from `this.getCurrentProviderInfo()`) but simply wasn't being passed through. This change also DRY-ed up the metric attribute objects in `captureTokenUsage` by extracting a shared `attributes` const, reducing repetition across ~10 `recordCounter`/`recordHistogram` calls.
author: alex-lum

reviews.body: LGTM - Thank you!

Add `provider` parameter to `captureTokenUsage` so the `task.tokens`
telemetry event includes the inference provider. This aligns it with
`captureConversationTurnEvent` which already emits `provider`, and
unblocks the dbt `silver_token_usage_daily` pipeline from populating
the `inference_provider` column.

  properties, and all metric attributes

title: Jupyter Notebook Enhancements
body: # Add Comprehensive Jupyter Notebook Support for Cline ## Description This pull request introduces comprehensive Jupyter Notebook support for Cline, enabling AI-assisted editing of `.ipynb` files with full cell-level context awareness. The feature allows users to seamlessly work with Jupyter notebooks using Cline's AI capabilities while preserving the notebook's JSON structure. ## Related Discussion This feature addresses the need for better Jupyter Notebook integration in Cline, allowing AI-assisted development workflows for data science and research projects. The implementation provides: - Enhanced notebook interaction capabilities - Cell-level context awareness for AI operations - Structure-preserving editing of notebook JSON format - Seamless integration with existing Cline workflows **Discussion:** https://github.com/cline/cline/discussions/1201 ## Overview ## ✨ Key Features ### 1. Enhanced Notebook Interaction Toggle - **Location**: Settings → Features → "Enhanced Notebook Interaction" - **Functionality**: Users can enable/disable notebook-specific processing - **Default**: Disabled (opt-in feature) ### 2. Jupyter-Specific Commands Three new VS Code commands for notebook interaction: - **`cline.generateCell`** - Generate new notebook cells with AI assistance - **`cline.explainCell`** - Get AI explanations of notebook cell code - **`cline.improveCell`** - Enhance existing notebook cells with AI suggestions ### 3. Intelligent Cell Context Extraction - Automatically detects when working with `.ipynb` files - Extracts cell JSON context from VS Code's notebook API - Passes complete cell structure to AI for better understanding - Preserves notebook metadata and formatting ### 4. AI-Aware Notebook Editing - AI receives special instructions for notebook JSON format - Understands cell types (code, markdown, raw) - Maintains proper JSON structure during edits - Includes comprehensive examples and error prevention ## 🏗️ Architecture ### Core Components Modified #### 1. Protocol Definitions (`proto/`) - **`commands.proto`**: Added `notebook_cell_json` field for cell context - **`state.proto`**: Added `enhanced_notebook_interaction_enabled` setting - **`file.proto`**: Enhanced file processing flags #### 2. Command System (`src/core/controller/commands/`) - **`addToCline.ts`**: Enhanced with notebook cell context support - **`explainWithCline.ts`**: Added notebook-specific explanations - **`improveWithCline.ts`**: Enhanced for notebook cell improvements - **`fixWithCline.ts`**: Added notebook compatibility #### 3. State Management (`src/core/`) - **`updateSettings.ts`**: Added notebook setting persistence - **`state-helpers.ts`**: Enhanced state management for notebook flag - **`mentions/index.ts`**: Propagated notebook flag through system #### 4. Task System (`src/core/task/`) - **`index.ts`**: Added notebook setting update methods - **`ToolExecutor.ts`**: Enhanced with notebook interaction support - **`TaskConfig.ts`**: Added notebook configuration interface - **Tool Handlers**: Enhanced read/write handlers for notebook processing #### 5. AI System Prompts (`src/core/system-prompt/`) - **`editing_files.ts`**: Added comprehensive Jupyter notebook instructions - **`examples.ts`**: Added two detailed notebook editing examples - **`replace_in_file.ts`**: Added notebook-specific replacement rules #### 6. VS Code Integration (`src/extension.ts`, `src/hosts/vscode/`) - **`extension.ts`**: Registered new Jupyter commands - **`commandUtils.ts`**: Added notebook cell extraction utilities - **`VscodeDiffViewProvider.ts`**: Enhanced diff handling for notebooks #### 7. User Interface (`webview-ui/`) - **`FeatureSettingsSection.tsx`**: Added notebook interaction toggle - **`ExtensionStateContext.tsx`**: Enhanced state management #### 8. Text Processing (`src/integrations/misc/`) - **`extract-text.ts`**: Enhanced to return raw JSON for notebooks when enabled - **`extract-file-content.ts`**: Added notebook-aware processing ## 🔧 Technical Implementation ### Feature Flag Architecture ```typescript interface ExtensionState { enhancedNotebookInteractionEnabled: boolean; // Default: false } ``` ### Data Flow ``` User opens .ipynb file ↓ User runs Jupyter command (Generate/Explain/Improve Cell) ↓ VS Code API extracts cell context ↓ Command handler receives cell JSON + user input ↓ Task system processes with notebook flag enabled ↓ AI receives: - Cell JSON structure - Notebook-specific instructions - Format preservation guidelines ↓ AI generates response maintaining JSON structure ↓ File updated with preserved notebook format ``` ### Cell Context Extraction ```typescript interface NotebookCellContext { cellType: 'code' | 'markdown' | 'raw'; source: string[]; metadata: Record; executionCount?: number; outputs?: any[]; } ``` ### Test Procedure - Enable/disable notebook interaction setting in UI - Open various Jupyter notebook file types (.ipynb) - Extract cell context from different cell types (code, markdown, raw) - Execute Jupyter-specific commands (Generate, Explain, Improve Cell) - Verify JSON structure preservation during AI edits - Test with complex notebook structures and metadata - Validate graceful fallbacks when feature is disabled - Cross-platform compatibility testing ### Type of Change - [ ] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [x] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality) - [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected) - [ ] ♻️ Refactor Changes - [ ] 💅 Cosmetic Changes - [ ] 📚 Documentation update - [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes ### Pre-flight Checklist - [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [x] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - [x] I have created a changeset using `npm run changeset` (required for user-facing changes) - [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Screenshots ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Adds comprehensive Jupyter Notebook support to Cline, enabling AI-assisted editing with cell-level context awareness and preserving JSON structure. > > - **Behavior**: > - Adds AI-assisted editing for `.ipynb` files with cell-level context awareness. > - Introduces `cline.generateCell`, `cline.explainCell`, and `cline.improveCell` commands for Jupyter Notebooks. > - Preserves JSON structure during notebook edits. > - **Settings**: > - Adds `enhancedNotebookInteractionEnabled` setting in `FeatureSettingsSection.tsx` and `ExtensionStateContext.tsx`. > - Default setting is `false` (opt-in feature). > - **VS Code Integration**: > - Registers new Jupyter commands in `extension.ts`. > - Implements notebook cell extraction in `commandUtils.ts`. > - **Protocol and State Management**: > - Updates `commands.proto` and `state.proto` for notebook cell context. > - Modifies `TaskConfig.ts` to include `enhancedNotebookInteractionEnabled`. > - **Diff View and File Handling**: > - Enhances `VscodeDiffViewProvider.ts` and `ExternalDiffViewProvider.ts` for notebook diff handling. > - Updates `extract-file-content.ts` and `extract-text.ts` for `.ipynb` file processing. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for 3982d606f236a4a0f360539a58384a044bda2530. You can [customize](https://app.ellipsis.dev/cline/settings/summaries) this summary. It will automatically update as commits are pushed.
author: tekulam

reviews.body:
This is great! 

I commented with a few suggestions below.

Mainly, I think there are differences in the UX between execution paths that make it a little confusing.
Currently:
1) explain cell with cline -> immediately sends prompt to task 🟢 
2) select text + improve cell with cline -> enter user prompt on top bar -> immediately sends prompt to task 🟢 
3) improve cell with cline _with no text selected_ -> enter user prompt on top bar -> sends prompt to window, but needs user action to send prompt 🟡 
4) generate cell with cline -> enter user prompt on top bar -> sends prompt to window, but needs user action to send prompt 🟡 

It would be good if they all had a unified behaviour, preferably the 🟢 behaviour.


Secondly, I've run in to at least one case where the json cell ordering got corrupted and the model had to regenerate the whole thing
```
I inserted the `.iloc` plot cell, but I see the notebook cell order got corrupted (the file now starts with the `.iloc` cell instead of the title markdown). Next I’ll restore the correct original ordering and keep the new plot cell in the right place, then re-validate the notebook JSON.
```
fortunately it was able to recover, but maybe not so hopeful for smaller models.

I wonder if there's a more controlled way to only allow edit access to a specific json section. This would likely require another tool, and my be a good TODO down the line. I'm curious how other editors do it.


I have some more comments inline.

This is a great work, thanks a lot!

commits.message: Jupyter Notebook Enhancements

fix: implement dynamic notebook instructions for replace_in_file

Leverages the new dynamic prompt infrastructure to conditionally inject Jupyter Notebook-specific instructions into the `replace_in_file` tool. This ensures that the model receives guidance on handling JSON structure in `.ipynb` files only when the `enhancedNotebookInteractionEnabled` setting is active, keeping the default prompt clean for other users.

- Added `enhancedNotebookInteractionEnabled` to global settings and system prompt context
- Updated `replace_in_file` tool to use a dynamic instruction function that appends notebook rules based on context
- Wired up state management to pass the setting value to the prompt builder

refactor: unify notebook output sanitization across two code paths

Previously, context menu commands (Add to Cline, Explain, etc.) wiped ALL
notebook outputs, while file mentions preserved text and only truncated images.
Additionally, when outputs weren't cleared, massive base64-encoded image data
was sent directly to the LLM, flooding context with garbage.

Changes:
- Create shared notebook-utils.ts with sanitization logic
- Update extract-text.ts to use shared utility
- Update commandUtils.ts to sanitize instead of clearing outputs

Both paths now truncate base64 image data with "[IMAGE DATA TRUNCATED]"
while preserving useful text outputs like print statements and errors.

feat(improve): unify prompt sending behavior for improveWithCline

Refactor improveWithCline to build a single prompt and handle sending uniformly: for notebooks, populate existing task if available and send immediately; otherwise, create new task. This unifies behavior across selected text and notebook contexts, removing dependency on sendAddToInputEvent and simplifying logic. Minor formatting tweaks in extension.ts for notebook context string.

refactor: remove notebook_cell_json from proto, move notebook context to dedicated commands

The contributor's original implementation added notebook_cell_json to the
CommandContext proto, which was then populated in getContextForCommand() for
any notebook file when enhancedNotebookInteractionEnabled was set.

This couples notebook-specific functionality to the general command proto,
which feels heavy for a niche feature. Protos should stay clean and general.

Changes:
- Remove notebook_cell_json field from CommandContext proto
- Export findMatchingNotebookCell() from commandUtils.ts
- Update Jupyter commands (JupyterGenerateCell, JupyterExplainCell,
  JupyterImproveCell) in extension.ts to fetch cell JSON directly and
  bundle it into the notebookContext parameter
- Update command files to use only notebookContext parameter
- Remove notebook-specific handling from getContextForCommand()

Result: Notebook context only flows through dedicated Jupyter commands.
Regular commands (Add to Cline, Fix, etc.) work the same for all file types.
The proto stays clean and general-purpose.

Note: This removes the behavior where regular commands would get notebook
context when used on .ipynb files with enhancedNotebookInteractionEnabled.
That feature is now exclusive to the dedicated Jupyter menu commands.

feat: improve notebook handling for empty notebooks

- Add semicolon to import statement for consistency
- Prevent errors by checking cell count before accessing notebook cells
- Add fallback in getContextForCommand for active notebook editor when no text editor is available
- Ensures robustness when dealing with empty or cell-less notebooks in the VSCode extension

refactor: extract common notebook context logic for Jupyter commands

Extracted duplicated code into a helper function `getNotebookCommandContext` to handle active notebook checks, context retrieval, and cell JSON fetching. This reduces duplication in `JupyterGenerateCell` and `JupyterExplainCell` commands, improving code maintainability and readability. Minor import semicolon fix for consistency.

fix: block notebook edits when enhanced interaction disabled

Prevent crashes when enhancedNotebookInteractionEnabled is false by blocking .ipynb file edits in WriteToFileToolHandler. Added validation to return an error message instructing the user to enable the setting, and set didRejectTool to stop the operation. Reading notebooks remains unaffected.

fix(mentions): reorder parameters in parseMentions signature

Reordered the parameters in the parseMentions function to move the default parameter to the end of the argument list. This change ensures consistency in the function signature and correctly aligns arguments at the call site in the Task class.

This update was done to fix failing tests.
Created proper diff views for vscode nd removed unnecessary logs

feat: make replace_in_file prompt dynamic based on open files

Add editorTabs to SystemPromptContext to expose open/visible files.

Populate editorTabs in Task using HostProvider.

Conditionally include notebook-specific instructions in replace_in_file tool only when .ipynb files are open or visible.

title: feat(cli): add mcp add shortcuts for stdio/http servers
body:
### Related Issue

Issue: #XXXX

### Description

This PR adds first-class MCP shortcuts to the CLI so users can add servers without hand-editing `cline_mcp_settings.json`.

Problem being solved:
- The CLI docs previously pointed users to manual JSON editing for MCP setup.
- This is slower, error-prone, and inconsistent with how users discover MCP in the UI.
- A very common mistake is entering a URL as the second argument without `--type http`, which used to silently produce a broken stdio config.

What this change introduces:
- New command group: `cline mcp`.
- New command: `cline mcp add <name> [targetOrCommand...]`.
- Stdio shortcut flow: `cline mcp add kanban -- kanban mcp`.
- Remote HTTP shortcut flow: `cline mcp add linear https://mcp.linear.app/mcp --type http`.
- Supported transport parsing for `stdio` (default), `http` (mapped to `streamableHttp`), and `sse`.
- Duplicate-name protection with a clear error.
- Settings file bootstrapping and schema validation before writing.

Important implementation details and decisions:
- MCP add logic was extracted into a dedicated utility module (`cli/src/utils/mcp.ts`) to keep `cli/src/index.ts` focused on command registration and user-facing output.
- Stdio configs now write `type: "stdio"` for consistency with current MCP examples and type-based remote entries.
- The command writes through the same CLI config resolution path (`--config` and default `~/.cline`) to avoid introducing an alternate storage path.
- Validation uses the existing `ServerConfigSchema` so the command cannot write malformed entries that diverge from runtime expectations.

Debugging and gotchas encountered during implementation:
- Commander argument parsing around `--` required careful shape selection to preserve arbitrary stdio command args.
- The initial implementation accepted a single URL argument in default stdio mode and wrote it as a command. This was corrected to fail fast with a prescriptive message to use `--type http`.
- There is an intentional asymmetry in stored config across transports (`type` for remote and now also stdio), and this PR normalizes around `type` to reduce user confusion.

Documentation updates:
- CLI MCP config docs now include direct command examples for stdio and remote HTTP setup.
- The note about MCP management was updated to clarify that interactive slash-command MCP management is still not present in the CLI TUI, while `cline mcp add` is now available.

### Test Procedure

Validation performed:

1. Static type validation:
   - `cd cli && npm run typecheck`

2. Unit tests for command parsing and MCP config behavior:
   - `cd cli && npm run test:run -- src/index.test.ts src/utils/mcp.test.ts`

3. Behavioral coverage added:
   - Confirms stdio add parsing with pass-through command args.
   - Confirms `--type http` parsing.
   - Confirms stdio writes `type: "stdio"`.
   - Confirms remote HTTP writes `type: "streamableHttp"`.
   - Confirms URL without `--type http` throws a clear corrective error.

Risk assessment and checks:
- Existing task/auth/history command behavior is untouched.
- MCP write path and schema are reused from existing code, reducing divergence risk.
- New logic is isolated behind a new command and utility, so impact is limited to explicit `cline mcp add` usage.

### Type of Change

-   [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
-   [x] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
-   [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected)
-   [x] ♻️ Refactor Changes
-   [ ] 💅 Cosmetic Changes
-   [x] 📚 Documentation update
-   [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes

### Pre-flight Checklist

-   [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs)
-   [ ] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`)
-   [ ] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Screenshots

N/A (CLI and config behavior change)

### Additional Notes

None.
author: saoudrizwan

reviews.body:

Adds first-class MCP server “add” shortcuts to the Cline CLI so users can create/update `cline_mcp_settings.json` via commands instead of manual JSON editing, aligning CLI setup ergonomics with existing MCP support in the core runtime.

**Changes:**
- Introduces `cline mcp add <name> [targetOrCommand...]` with transport parsing for `stdio` (default), `http`→`streamableHttp`, and `sse`.
- Adds MCP settings write utility (`cli/src/utils/mcp.ts`) plus unit tests for parsing and config output.

title: fix(prompt): resolve native tool placeholder interpolation
body:
### Related Issue

**Issue:** #XXXX

### Description

This is a focused follow-up to feedback on #9738.

Native tool schema descriptions were leaving `{{CWD}}` and `{{MULTI_ROOT_HINT}}` unresolved in generated native tool definitions (including Vertex Gemini 3), which made prompt/tool behavior hard to verify from snapshots and leaked raw template markers into provider payloads.

This PR fixes that by:
- Resolving `{{CWD}}` to `context.cwd` (fallback `process.cwd()`) during native tool schema conversion.
- Resolving `{{MULTI_ROOT_HINT}}` based on `context.isMultiRootEnabled`.
- Centralizing `MULTI_ROOT_HINT` in a shared system-prompt constant so tool-use prompt rendering and native schema conversion use the same source of truth.
- Adding regression coverage in `spec.test.ts` to verify OpenAI/Anthropic/Gemini native converters all replace these placeholders.

### Test Procedure

I validated with:

1. `npm run protos`
2. `npm run test:unit -- src/core/prompts/system-prompt/__tests__/integration.test.ts --update-snapshots`
3. `npm run test:unit -- src/core/prompts/system-prompt/__tests__/spec.test.ts`

Validation points:
- New unit coverage asserts no `{{CWD}}` / `{{MULTI_ROOT_HINT}}` leakage for native converters.
- Updated native tools snapshots now contain resolved CWD values (`/test/project` in test context).
- Final unit run passed after the constant-centralization refinement.

Scope is intentionally limited to native tool description interpolation and shared hint-constant consistency.

title: feat(cli): add kanban command aliases
body:
### Related Issue

Issue: N/A

### Description

This adds a small CLI entry point for Kanban so users can run either `cline kanban` or `cline --kanban` and get the exact behavior of `npx kanban --agent cline`.

The implementation introduces a shared runner that spawns `npx` with inherited stdio and exits with the child process exit code. It also handles Windows by using `npx.cmd` on `win32`.

For `--kanban`, the root command now short circuits early before normal task flow. If a prompt is passed together with `--kanban`, the command warns and exits, matching existing CLI guard patterns for mutually incompatible inputs.

I also added parsing coverage in `cli/src/index.test.ts` for:
- the new `kanban` subcommand
- the new `--kanban` root flag
- command structure assertions including the new command

While running tests, importing `cli/src/index.ts` in Vitest was still executing `program.parse()` and causing runtime side effects in tests. To keep command parsing tests deterministic, `program.parse()` is now skipped when `process.env.VITEST === \"true\"`.

### Test Procedure

I validated the change with:

```bash
npx biome check cli/src/index.ts cli/src/index.test.ts --diagnostic-level=error
npm --prefix cli run typecheck
npm --prefix cli run test:run
```

I specifically verified:
- command parsing accepts `cline kanban`
- command parsing accepts `cline --kanban`
- existing CLI tests continue to pass

title: fix(gemini): cap Flash output tokens at 8192 across providers
body:
### Description

The purpose of this PR is to stop spiraling of Gemini flash models because with no limits on output tokens the model just keeps outputting forever. This PR adds a consistent output-token cap for Gemini Flash models to prevent overly large completions and keep behavior predictable across providers.

Changes included:
- Cap Gemini Flash output tokens to `8192` in direct Gemini requests by setting `maxOutputTokens` in the Gemini provider.
- Cap OpenRouter Gemini Flash requests to `8192` by sending `max_tokens` for Gemini Flash model IDs.
- Clamp dynamic OpenRouter/Cline model metadata for Gemini Flash to `8192` so runtime and model info stay aligned.
- Add unit tests for OpenRouter stream payload behavior.

### Test Procedure

I validated the change with:

- `npm run protos`
- `npm run test:unit -- src/core/api/transform/__tests__/openrouter-stream.test.ts src/core/api/providers/__tests__/openrouter.test.ts`
- `npm run test:unit -- src/core/api/transform/__tests__/openrouter-stream.test.ts`
- `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false --project tsconfig.unit-test.json`
- `npx biome check src/core/api/providers/gemini.ts src/core/api/transform/openrouter-stream.ts src/core/controller/models/refreshClineModels.ts src/core/controller/models/refreshOpenRouterModels.ts src/core/api/transform/__tests__/openrouter-stream.test.ts`

What I specifically checked:
- OpenRouter includes `max_tokens=8192` for Gemini Flash.
- OpenRouter preserves lower max token values when model metadata is below `8192`.
- OpenRouter does not send `max_tokens` for non-Gemini models.

### Type of Change

-   [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
-   [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
-   [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected)
-   [ ] ♻️ Refactor Changes
-   [ ] 💅 Cosmetic Changes
-   [ ] 📚 Documentation update
-   [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes

### Pre-flight Checklist

-   [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs)
-   [ ] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`)
-   [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Screenshots

N/A (no UI changes)

### Additional Notes

I used `8192` as the cap (instead of `8092`/`8196`) to align with existing token-limit conventions in this codebase.
author: arafatkatze

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR enforces a consistent 8,192 output-token cap for Gemini “Flash” models across direct Gemini requests and OpenRouter-backed requests, aiming to prevent unexpectedly large completions and keep runtime model metadata aligned with request behavior.

**Changes:**
- Clamp Gemini Flash model metadata (`maxTokens`) to 8,192 when refreshing OpenRouter and Cline model lists.
- Send `max_tokens`=min(modelMax, 8192) for OpenRouter Gemini Flash streaming requests.
- Set `maxOutputTokens` for Gemini provider requests with Flash capped at 8,192, plus add unit coverage for OpenRouter stream payload.

title: fix(telemetry): restore cache token and cost metrics in captureTokenUsage
body:
### Related Issue

**Issue:** ENG-1600

### Description

The `captureTokenUsage` telemetry method was only recording `inputTokens` and `outputTokens`, silently dropping `cacheWriteTokens`, `cacheReadTokens`, and `totalCost` that were already available at both call sites in the task loop. This meant the `task/tokens` event and associated OTel metrics had no cache or cost data.

This PR adds an optional `options` parameter to `captureTokenUsage()` and forwards the cache/cost fields from both the streaming `onUsageChunk` callback and the `getApiStreamUsage` fallback path in `src/core/task/index.ts`.

**Changes:**
- `TelemetryService.captureTokenUsage()` — new optional `options` param with `cacheWriteTokens`, `cacheReadTokens`, `totalCost`; records `cline.tokens.cache_write`, `cline.tokens.cache_read` counters/histograms and `cline.tokens.cost` histogram when provided
- `src/core/task/index.ts` — `queueUsageChunkSideEffects` expanded to accept and forward `chunkOptions`; both `onUsageChunk` and `getApiStreamUsage` fallback call sites now pass cache/cost fields through

### Test Procedure

- Added 3 unit tests in `TelemetryService.metrics.test.ts`:
  1. Verifies cache and cost metrics are emitted when options are provided
  2. Verifies cache/cost metrics are skipped when options fields are undefined
  3. Verifies options are included in event properties
- Ran full unit test suite (`npm run test:unit`) — 1186 passing, 0 failures
- Ran `npm run compile` — clean build with lint passing
- The change is additive-only (new optional parameter), so all existing callers are unaffected

### Type of Change

-   [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
-   [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
-   [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected)
-   [ ] ♻️ Refactor Changes
-   [ ] 💅 Cosmetic Changes
-   [ ] 📚 Documentation update
-   [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes

### Pre-flight Checklist

-   [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs)
-   [x] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`)
-   [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Screenshots

N/A — backend-only telemetry change with no UI impact.

### Additional Notes

The `options` parameter is intentionally optional so all existing callers continue to work unchanged. Only the two call sites in the task loop were updated to pass the extra fields.
author: alex-lum

Add optional `options` parameter to `captureTokenUsage()` to record
cache write/read token counters and cost histograms that were
previously missing from telemetry.

- Extend `captureTokenUsage` with `cacheWriteTokens`, `cacheReadTokens`,
  and `totalCost` fields via an options object
- Record `cline.tokens.cache_write`, `cline.tokens.cache_read` counters/
  histograms and `cline.tokens.cost` histogram when provided
- Forward cache/cost data from both streaming `onUsageChunk` and
  `getApiStreamUsage` fallback call sites in the task loop
- Add 3 test cases covering options forwarding, undefined skipping,
  and event property inclusion
Merge branch 'main' into alex/eng-1600-new-tasktokens-event-not-reporting-cache-tokens

refactor(telemetry): extract shared TokenUsage type and add value assertions

Address PR review feedback:
- Extract shared TokenUsage interface used by both captureTokenUsage and
  captureConversationTurnEvent, preventing future drift
- Add numeric value assertions for cache/cost counters and histograms
  so regressions recording wrong values are caught
Merge branch 'main' into alex/eng-1600-new-tasktokens-event-not-reporting-cache-tokens

reviews.body:
__Must fix:__ `applyTaskOptions()` currently writes a session override for `actModeThinkingBudgetTokens` / `planModeThinkingBudgetTokens` even when `--thinking` is not provided. Because `thinkingBudget` defaults to `0`, this can unintentionally override a user’s saved positive thinking budget for the current CLI session. Please only call `setSessionOverride(...)` for the thinking budget when `--thinking` is explicitly passed, and otherwise leave the existing configured value unchanged.

This is the thing Greptile pointed out, and it's what Cline w/ GPT-5.4 said too when I asked for what's 100% essential to fix while keeping the PR minimal.  Otherwise good to go.

title: fix(cli): remount TUI only on width resize
body:
### Related Issue

Issue: N/A

### Description

This fixes a follow-up CLI TUI behavior issue after the startup-prompt replay fix.

Current behavior before this change:
- Any terminal resize event triggered the full recovery path in `useTerminalSize`
- The recovery path clears the terminal and forces a remount via `resizeKey`
- This was needed for width changes, but it also ran on height-only changes

Problem with that behavior:
- Width changes alter wrapping, so Ink's tracked output height can go stale and the remount is useful
- Height-only changes do not change wrapping width, so forcing a remount is unnecessary and causes visible task restart-like churn

What this PR changes:
- `useTerminalSize` now tracks previous `columns`
- It sets a pending recovery flag only when width actually changes
- Height-only resize events still update `rows` reactively, but do not trigger clear + remount
- Debounce behavior remains the same for width changes

I also expanded the regression test to reflect runtime behavior more accurately:
- Added helpers to control `process.stdout.columns` and `rows` in tests
- Updated the existing resize test to explicitly verify width change triggers the clear sequence
- Added a new test that verifies height-only resize does not trigger the clear sequence or remount path

### Test Procedure

Ran:
- `npm -C cli run test:run -- src/components/App.resize-initial-prompt.test.tsx`
- `npm -C cli run typecheck`
- `npx --yes biome check --diagnostic-level=error --files-ignore-unknown=true cli/src/hooks/useTerminalSize.ts cli/src/components/App.resize-initial-prompt.test.tsx`

Manual check:
1. Start `cline "prompt"`
2. Resize terminal height only
3. Confirm no full reload/remount behavior
4. Resize terminal width
5. Confirm recovery path still runs and rendering remains correct

### Type of Change

- [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
- [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected)
- [ ] ♻️ Refactor Changes
- [ ] 💅 Cosmetic Changes
- [ ] 📚 Documentation update
- [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes

### Pre-flight Checklist

- [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs)
- [ ] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`)
- [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Screenshots

N/A

### Additional Notes

This keeps the width-resize protection that addresses Ink rendering artifacts while removing unnecessary remounts on height-only resizes.
author: saoudrizwan

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Adjusts the CLI Ink TUI resize recovery logic so that the expensive “clear + full remount” path only runs when terminal *width* changes, avoiding unnecessary churn on height-only resizes while preserving the wrapping/artifact mitigation for width changes.

**Changes:**
- Track previous `columns` and gate the debounced recovery/remount to width changes only.
- Keep `rows` updates reactive on resize without triggering a full recovery on height-only changes.
- Expand the regression test to assert clear/remount behavior for width changes and no clear/remount for height-only changes.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| cli/src/hooks/useTerminalSize.ts | Adds width-change detection and a pending refresh flag so recovery/remount happens only on column changes. |
| cli/src/components/App.resize-initial-prompt.test.tsx | Updates resize tests with helpers to control terminal dimensions and validates clear sequence only occurs on width changes. |

title: fix(cli): prevent startup prompt replay on resize remount
body:
### Related Issue

Issue: N/A

### Description

This fixes a CLI TUI regression where `cline \"prompt\"` restarted the task after terminal resize.

Root cause:
- `useTerminalSize` increments `resizeKey` after a real `process.stdout` resize event.
- `App` remounts keyed content on resize.
- `ChatView` runs mount-only startup auto-submit effect.
- `initialPrompt` and `initialImages` were still being passed on remount, so `controller.initTask(...)` replayed.

Why interactive typed prompts did not restart:
- typed prompts are not persistent startup props after first submit.
- only CLI startup props were being re-sent on remount.

Fix approach:
- make startup prompt and images one-shot in `App` via local pending state.
- clear pending startup values after first mount so later resize remounts do not replay.

Added regression coverage:
- new test `App.resize-initial-prompt.test.tsx` emits a real `process.stdout` `resize` event.
- verifies startup `initTask` runs once and is not called again after resize debounce/remount.

### Test Procedure

Ran:
- `npm -C cli run typecheck`
- `npm -C cli run test:run -- src/components/App.test.tsx src/components/App.resize-initial-prompt.test.tsx`
- `npx --yes biome check --diagnostic-level=error --files-ignore-unknown=true cli/src/components/App.tsx cli/src/components/App.resize-initial-prompt.test.tsx`

Manual behavior validation target for reviewers:
1. run `cline \"hello\"`
2. let task start
3. resize terminal
4. confirm UI reflows only and task does not restart

### Type of Change

- [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
- [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected)
- [ ] ♻️ Refactor Changes
- [ ] 💅 Cosmetic Changes
- [ ] 📚 Documentation update
- [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes

### Pre-flight Checklist

- [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs)
- [ ] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`)
- [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Screenshots

N/A

### Additional Notes

The previous attempted fix focused on remount behavior in `ChatView`, but this patch fixes the source of replay by consuming startup inputs one time at the `App` boundary where props are threaded into `ChatView`.
author: saoudrizwan

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Fixes a CLI Ink TUI regression where resizing the terminal caused the keyed remount to replay the startup `initialPrompt`/`initialImages`, restarting the task.

**Changes:**
- Make `initialPrompt` and `initialImages` “one-shot” by consuming them into local pending state in `App` and clearing after first mount.
- Thread the pending startup values into `ChatView` instead of the raw props so resize remounts don’t re-trigger startup submission.
- Add a regression test that emits a real `process.stdout` `resize` event and asserts `initTask` is only called once.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| cli/src/components/App.tsx | Introduces pending startup state and clears it after first mount; passes pending values to `ChatView` to prevent replay on resize remount. |
| cli/src/components/App.resize-initial-prompt.test.tsx | Adds regression coverage ensuring a real resize event doesn’t cause startup prompt replay / duplicate `initTask` calls. |

title: feat(cli): add --continue for current directory
body:
### Related Issue

**Issue:** #XXXX

### Description

This adds a minimal `cline --continue` flow for the CLI.

The goal here was to support one narrow behavior: when the user runs `cline --continue` from a directory, the CLI should reopen the most recent task previously started from that same directory. I deliberately kept this scoped to the default command only. This PR does not add `cline task --continue`, does not treat an extra prompt as a follow-up message, and does not broaden the existing `--taskId` resume flow.

The implementation leans on the task metadata that already exists in history. Task history entries already capture `cwdOnTaskInitialization`, with `shadowGitConfigWorkTree` available as a fallback for older tasks. I added a tiny helper that filters history by the current workspace path, sorts by timestamp, and returns the newest match. Once that task is found, the CLI hands off to the existing resume path instead of creating a second resume mechanism. That keeps the actual task restoration behavior consistent with how `--taskId` already works.

I also added a few guardrails so this stays intentionally small and predictable. `--continue` rejects a prompt, rejects piped stdin, and rejects being combined with `--taskId`. If there is no historical task for the current directory, the CLI exits with a clear message telling the user to start a new task or browse history.

The debugging journey here was mostly about keeping the scope tight. An earlier pass grew into broader support for prompt follow-ups and `cline task --continue`, which made the diff noisier than necessary and started to blur the original intent. I reset that work and rebuilt the change from scratch with a narrower target so the final patch only touches the default command behavior plus the small history lookup helper and docs.

### Test Procedure

I tested the history resolution logic directly with a focused unit test file that covers:

- selecting the newest task for a matching workspace
- falling back to `shadowGitConfigWorkTree` for older entries
- returning `null` when there is no workspace match

I also ran CLI typechecking to make sure the new flag handling and resume handoff compile cleanly:

```bash
cd cli
npx vitest run src/utils/task-history.test.ts
npm run typecheck
```

The main regression risk here was accidentally broadening resume behavior beyond the requested feature. I checked that this PR does not alter `cline task`, does not add prompt-follow-up semantics to `--continue`, and continues to route actual resumption through the existing `resumeTask()` path once a task ID has been resolved.

### Type of Change

-   [ ] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
-   [x] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
-   [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected)
-   [ ] ♻️ Refactor Changes
-   [ ] 💅 Cosmetic Changes
-   [x] 📚 Documentation update
-   [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes

### Pre-flight Checklist

-   [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs)
-   [ ] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`)
-   [ ] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Screenshots

Not applicable. This changes CLI behavior and documentation only.

### Additional Notes

This PR intentionally keeps the UX narrow:

- `cline --continue` is supported
- `cline task --continue` is not added
- `cline --continue "prompt"` is rejected
- `stdin | cline --continue` is rejected

That was a deliberate choice to match the requested behavior and avoid layering multiple meanings onto the flag in the first implementation.
author: saoudrizwan

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Adds a minimal `cline --continue` flow to the CLI to resume the most recent task associated with the current working directory, leveraging existing task history metadata and the existing resume path.

**Changes:**
- Added `--continue` flag to the default `cline` command and routed it through the existing `resumeTask()` implementation.
- Introduced a small history helper to locate the newest task matching the current workspace directory (with legacy fallback).
- Updated CLI documentation/manpage to describe and demonstrate the new flag.

**Issue:** #XXXX

### Description

Cline's Gemini converter (`toolSpecFunctionDeclarations` in `spec.ts`) was **not including parameter descriptions** in the tool schemas sent to Gemini. Every other provider (Anthropic, OpenAI) in Cline already received parameter descriptions, and every competing agent (Codex, OpenCode, Gemini CLI) sends them too.

This meant when Cline sent tools like `replace_in_file` to Gemini, the model only saw parameter names and types — with no documentation about what format `diff` expects, what `task_progress` is for, or that `path` should be relative to CWD. The model had to guess entirely from names and the system prompt.

**The fix:** Add `description` from `param.instruction` to the Gemini parameter schema, matching what the Anthropic/OpenAI converters already do.

**Cross-agent comparison:**

| Agent | Parameter descriptions in tool schema? |
|-------|---------------------------------------|
| Codex | Yes |
| OpenCode | Yes |
| Gemini CLI | Yes |
| Cline (before) | **No** — only type, no description |
| Cline (after) | Yes |

### Test Procedure

- All 1166 unit tests passing
- Added new `spec.test.ts` with 5 tests verifying:
  - Parameter descriptions are included in Gemini tool schemas
  - Descriptions are present for all parameters (required and optional)
  - Function-type instructions (dynamic descriptions) resolve correctly
  - Empty instructions don't produce empty description fields
  - Gemini and Anthropic converters produce identical description text for the same tool
- Gemini tools snapshot updated to reflect the new descriptions

### Type of Change

-   [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
-   [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
-   [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected)
-   [ ] ♻️ Refactor Changes
-   [ ] 💅 Cosmetic Changes

-   [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Screenshots

N/A — backend-only fix affecting tool schema generation.

### Additional Notes

This is a structural correctness fix, not a prompt tweak. The model's primary interface with tools is the function declaration schema — missing parameter descriptions meant Gemini was effectively calling tools with no documentation.

The Gemini converter was the only provider that didn't include

and OpenAI converters both resolve param.instruction into each
parameter's description field. This was missing for Google/Gemini,
meaning the model only saw parameter names and types with no

title: fix(tools): prevent crash when list_files/list_code_definition_names receives a file path
body:
### Related Issue

**Issue:** #XXXX

### Description

Fixes a crash when the model passes a **file path** (instead of a directory path) to `list_files` or `list_code_definition_names`. This happens regularly — models sometimes call `list_code_definition_names` on a specific file they want to inspect, which causes `globby` to throw because its `cwd` option must be a directory.

**Changes:**

- **`list-files.ts`** — Adds an `isDirectory` guard before calling `globby`. Returns `[[], false]` for non-directory paths instead of crashing. Also fixes a latent bug where the unresolved `dirPath` was passed as `cwd` instead of the already-resolved `absolutePath`.
- **`tree-sitter/index.ts`** — Adds an `isDirectory` check with a helpful error message: *"The provided path is a file, not a directory. To view this file use read_file instead."* Previously it only checked `fileExistsAtPath`, which returns true for files too.
- **`list_code_definition_names.ts`** — Clarifies the tool parameter description to say "a directory (not a file)" and suggests `read_file` as the alternative.
- **Tests** — Added for both `listFiles` and `parseSourceCodeForDefinitionsTopLevel` covering the file-path and directory-path cases.

### Test Procedure

- All 1165 unit tests passing
- New tests verify both the file-path guard (returns empty/error) and the normal directory-path case (still works)
- Snapshots updated for tool description changes across all variants
- Validated at 60.2% on Terminal-Bench (no regression)

fix(tools): prevent crash when list_files/list_code_definition_names receives a file path

listFiles() passed unvalidated paths as globby's `cwd`, crashing with
"The cwd option must be a path to a directory" when the model provided a
