title: fix: trigger auto-compaction on OpenRouter context overflow errors
body: ### Related Issue Issue: #9592 ### Description This PR fixes a regression where OpenRouter context-window overflows were not always recognized as context-overflow errors by Cline's retry logic, so automatic compaction did not trigger reliably. Initial hypothesis was that `max_tokens` should only be set for Anthropic models. While investigating, I traced the error-handling flow end to end and found the more direct break in the retry path: - `task/index.ts` already has the right behavior for context overflow: if `checkContextWindowExceededError(...)` returns true, it auto-compacts and retries. - The OpenRouter provider wraps stream errors into a plain `Error` string in some paths. - In the direct `chunk.error` path, the thrown error message used `error.code` + `error.message`, but the structured `status` field was not preserved. - In the `finish_reason = "error"` path, the provider can include a stringified JSON object in the message. Because of that wrapping, by the time the task retry logic sees the error, it may not have consistent structured status metadata unless we preserve it. This PR makes that behavior explicit and robust: 1. OpenRouter provider now preserves status/code on wrapped errors before throwing. - `src/core/api/providers/openrouter.ts` 2. Context overflow detection still prefers structured status first, and also supports JSON-encoded `"status"`/`"code"` inside message text for the mid-stream path. - `src/core/context/context-management/context-error-handling.ts` 3. Added regression tests to lock in both sides: - detector-level tests for structured status and JSON-encoded status - provider-level test that verifies OpenRouter wrapped errors carry `status: 400` and are recognized by `checkContextWindowExceededError(...)` Debugging journey summary: - Reproduced the issue report assumptions from #9592 and followed the first-chunk failure flow. - Confirmed `task/index.ts` compaction logic is already present and correctly wired. - Verified the mismatch occurred at provider wrapping boundaries, not in compaction orchestration. - Added tests around the exact error shapes we see in practice so this does not regress when OpenRouter changes response formatting between stream paths. Alternatives considered: - Only matching broader regex text patterns in context error detection. - Rejected as primary fix because this is brittle and less explicit than preserving structured status at the source. - Restricting `max_tokens` setting to Anthropic-only in OpenRouter transform. - This may still be a useful separate decision, but it does not address the specific failure mode where context overflow should trigger compact-and-retry and currently can fail to detect due to wrapped error shape. ### Test Procedure 1. Ran focused unit tests for the new and changed coverage: ```bash npm run test:unit -- src/core/context/context-management/__tests__/context-error-handling.test.ts src/core/api/providers/__tests__/openrouter.test.ts ``` 2. Verified expected outcomes in tests: - Structured `status: 400` + context-length message is detected as context-window overflow. - JSON-encoded status/message inside error text is detected. - Non-context 400 error text is not falsely classified as context overflow. - OpenRouter handler preserves `status` on wrapped stream errors and detection returns true. 3. Spot-validated with a local script using the issue payload shape (`status: 400` + context-length message) and confirmed detector returns true. Potential impact reviewed: - This only changes error wrapping metadata and context overflow detection logic for matching errors. - Non-context 400 errors remain non-compaction errors unless they match context-window patterns. - Existing retry behavior in `task/index.ts` is unchanged; this PR ensures that behavior receives the correct signal. ### Type of Change - [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality) - [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected) - [ ] ♻️ Refactor Changes - [ ] 💅 Cosmetic Changes - [ ] 📚 Documentation update - [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes ### Pre-flight Checklist - [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [ ] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - [ ] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Screenshots N/A (backend/error-handling change). ### Additional Notes No API contract changes. This is an internal reliability fix for context-overflow recovery signaling. ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Fixes OpenRouter context overflow error handling by preserving error status and enhancing detection logic, ensuring reliable auto-compaction triggering. > > - **Behavior**: > - Fixes OpenRouter context overflow error handling by preserving `status` and `code` in wrapped errors in `openrouter.ts`. > - Enhances `checkContextWindowExceededError()` in `context-error-handling.ts` to detect JSON-encoded status in error messages. > - **Tests**: > - Adds tests in `openrouter.test.ts` to verify error wrapping and context-compaction detection. > - Adds tests in `context-error-handling.test.ts` for structured and JSON-encoded status detection. > - **Misc**: > - Confirms existing retry logic in `task/index.ts` is correctly wired for context overflow. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for 9458d4472b405b2647ad2d54ee9092e6dcacb21d. You can [customize](https://app.ellipsis.dev/cline/settings/summaries) this summary. It will automatically update as commits are pushed.

title: fix: prevent Chinese filename escaping in diff view
body: ## Related Issue Closes #9604 ## Description When viewing changes for files with non-ASCII filenames (e.g. Chinese characters like `研究内容-大纲.md`), the diff view displays percent-encoded filenames and fails to open properly. **Root cause:** `vscode.Uri.from()` percent-encodes the `path` component, turning `研究内容-大纲.md` into `%E7%A0%94%E7%A9%B6%E5%86%85%E5%AE%B9-%E5%A4%A7%E7%BA%B2.md`. **Fix:** Switch to `vscode.Uri.parse()` + `.with()` for constructing the diff view URI. This is consistent with how other diff URIs are already created in `openMultiFileDiff.ts` (line 19) and `VscodeCommentReviewController.ts` (line 92), which use the same pattern: ```typescript // Before (encodes non-ASCII characters): vscode.Uri.from({ scheme: DIFF_VIEW_URI_SCHEME, path: fileName, query: base64Content, }) // After (preserves non-ASCII characters, consistent with rest of codebase): vscode.Uri.parse(`${DIFF_VIEW_URI_SCHEME}:${fileName}`).with({ query: base64Content, }) ``` ## Test Procedure 1. Create or open a file with Chinese characters in the filename (e.g. `研究内容-大纲.md`) 2. Have Cline make changes to the file 3. Click "View Changes" — the diff view should display the correct filename and open successfully ## Type of Change - [x] Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) ## Pre-flight Checklist - [x] I have read the [contributing guidelines](../CONTRIBUTING.md) - [x] My changes follow the project's coding style - [x] I have tested my changes - [x] My changes do not generate any new warnings ## Additional Notes This is a 1-line change (net -2 lines). The content provider (`provideTextDocumentContent`) only reads `uri.query` (base64-encoded content), not `uri.path`, so the change in URI construction does not affect content delivery. ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Fixes non-ASCII filename encoding in diff view by using `vscode.Uri.parse()` in `VscodeDiffViewProvider`. > > - **Behavior**: > - Fixes issue with non-ASCII filenames (e.g., Chinese) being percent-encoded in diff view. > - Uses `vscode.Uri.parse()` with `.with()` instead of `vscode.Uri.from()` in `VscodeDiffViewProvider`. > - **Consistency**: > - Aligns URI construction with existing patterns in `openMultiFileDiff.ts` and `VscodeCommentReviewController.ts`. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for 7e1288cd629f5f8f82461d992a915cb6726929af. You can [customize](https://app.ellipsis.dev/cline/settings/summaries) this summary. It will automatically update as commits are pushed.

commits.message:
fix: prevent Chinese filename escaping in diff view

Use Uri.parse() instead of Uri.from() for the diff view URI to prevent
non-ASCII characters (e.g. Chinese) in filenames from being
percent-encoded. This is consistent with how other diff URIs are created
in openMultiFileDiff.ts and VscodeCommentReviewController.ts.

Uri.from() encodes the path component, turning Chinese characters into
percent-encoded sequences like %E7%A0%94..., which causes the diff view
to display escaped filenames and fail to open properly.

fix: encode URI-reserved delimiters in filename before Uri.parse

Encode %, #, and ? in the filename before passing to Uri.parse() to
prevent them from being interpreted as URI delimiters. This handles
edge cases where filenames contain these characters (valid on macOS/Linux)
while preserving non-ASCII characters like Chinese.

OK, I have meditated on this and asked another Windows developer for input. Here's what I think we should do:

BASIC version (this is very close to what you have...):

- Let people use file extensions 🆕 
- On macOS and Linux, execute the hooks as we do today
- On Windows, we should support .cmd 🆕 and .ps1 files and look up cmd.exe, powershell.exe respectively to run them
- Ignore .cmd and .ps1 files on non-Windows platforms (sorry, cross-platform PowerShell users)
- Only pay attention to .cmd, .ps1 files on Windows.

Benefit of the basic version: It is easy to implement and quick to explain.

Approving, if you could just 🥺 support file extensions and add .cmd files?

DELUXE version:

- BASIC version, plus...
- There's a metadata file where you can point to interpreters on Windows. For example you could say you want .js files to run in node; .py files to run in Python.
- The same metadata file can turn hooks on and off by platform.

Benefit of the deluxe version:
- Mixed Windows-other OS teams are going to have to check in two files for each hook: The hook, and a .ps1 wrapper around it which looks up the interpreter and runs the hook. This is tedious if you have a lot of hooks, so we could make it a first class feature.
- The hardy solo Windows developer on a team of macOS/Linux developers can turn broken hooks off until she goes and fixes their broken shell scripts or whatever to run in git bash, etc.

title: fix: clear all OCA secrets on auth refresh failure to prevent re-auth loop
body: ## Summary Fixes #9567 — Oracle Code Assist (OCA) authentication breaks daily after token expiry and cannot recover without manual SQLite deletion. ## Root Cause `OcaAuthProvider.clearAuth()` only cleared 2 of the 4 OCA secrets: - ✅ `ocaApiKey` — cleared - ✅ `ocaRefreshToken` — cleared - ❌ `ocaAccessToken` — **not cleared** (legacy secret from older Cline versions) - ❌ `ocaTokenSet` — **not cleared** (legacy secret from older Cline versions) When OCA token refresh fails with `400 invalid_grant` or `401`, the code correctly calls `kickstartInteractiveLoginAsFallback()` → `clearAuth()`. But the legacy secrets `ocaAccessToken` and `ocaTokenSet` (written by older Cline versions) remained in VS Code's secret storage. These stale secrets caused every subsequent re-auth attempt to fail in a loop. Restarting VS Code did not help since the secrets are persisted to disk. The reporter's workaround — deleting rows matching `secret://%oca%` from the SQLite DB — confirms that clearing all 4 secrets resolves the issue. ## Changes - **`src/shared/storage/state-keys.ts`**: Added `ocaAccessToken` and `ocaTokenSet` to `SecretKeys` so they are properly typed and can be managed via `StateManager.setSecret()` - **`src/services/auth/oca/providers/OcaAuthProvider.ts`**: Updated `clearAuth()` to clear all 4 OCA secrets - **`proto/cline/state.proto`**: Auto-regenerated by pre-commit hook ## Testing Notes This fix is safe: - If `ocaAccessToken`/`ocaTokenSet` don't exist in storage, `setSecret(key, undefined)` is a no-op - If they do exist (legacy users), they will now be properly cleared on auth failure - Normal auth flow is unaffected since `clearAuth()` is only called on failure/logout Requires an OCA environment and a token expiry scenario to fully verify. Tagging @nihar-oracle (Oracle, original OCA auth author) for review. ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Fixes OCA re-authentication loop by clearing all relevant secrets on auth refresh failure in `OcaAuthProvider`. > > - **Behavior**: > - Fixes OCA re-auth loop by clearing all 4 secrets in `OcaAuthProvider.clearAuth()`. > - Handles `400 invalid_grant` and `401` errors by ensuring no stale secrets remain. > - **Files**: > - `OcaAuthProvider.ts`: Updates `clearAuth()` to clear `ocaAccessToken` and `ocaTokenSet`. > - `state-keys.ts`: Adds `ocaAccessToken` and `ocaTokenSet` to `SecretKeys`. > - `state.proto`: Adds `oca_access_token` and `oca_token_set` fields. > - **Testing**: > - Safe for environments without legacy secrets; no-op if secrets don't exist. > - Requires OCA environment and token expiry scenario for full verification. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for f95686374e77199665d1aeb66cf8ca58e484c9bb. You can [customize](https://app.ellipsis.dev/cline/settings/summaries) this summary. It will automatically update as commits are pushed.

commits.message:
fix: clear all OCA secrets on auth refresh failure to prevent re-auth loop

When OCA token refresh fails with 400 invalid_grant or 401, legacy secrets
ocaAccessToken and ocaTokenSet (from older Cline versions) were left in VS
Code's secret storage. clearAuth() only cleared ocaApiKey and ocaRefreshToken,
causing every subsequent re-auth attempt to fail in a loop requiring manual
SQLite deletion to recover.

Fix:
- Add ocaAccessToken and ocaTokenSet to SecretKeys in state-keys.ts
- Update OcaAuthProvider.clearAuth() to clear all 4 OCA secrets

Fixes #9567

title: fix: resolve "Could not find the file context" error in Explain Changes
body: ## Summary Fixes intermittent `"Error: Could not find the file context"` error when replying to a comment thread in the Explain Changes diff view (issue #9382). ## Root Cause Two code sites were doing a strict `absolutePath`-only lookup when matching a file path from the VS Code comment controller against the `changedFiles` array: **`explainChangesShared.ts` — `handleCommentReply()`:** ```typescript // Before (broken): const file = changedFiles.find((f) => f.absolutePath === filePath) // After (fixed): const file = changedFiles.find((f) => f.absolutePath === filePath || f.relativePath === filePath) ``` **`explainChanges.ts` — `onCommentStart` callback:** ```typescript // Before (broken): const matchingFile = changedFiles.find((f) => f.absolutePath === filePath) // After (fixed): const matchingFile = changedFiles.find((f) => f.absolutePath === filePath || f.relativePath === filePath) ``` The VS Code comment controller may return paths in different formats depending on context (relative vs. absolute, different path separators on Windows, etc.), causing the strict match to fail intermittently. ## Fix Added `relativePath` as a fallback in both lookup sites, making them consistent with the already-correct logic in `streamAIExplanationComments()` which already handled both cases. ## Testing No existing tests for this module. The fix aligns the two broken lookup sites with the pattern already used correctly in `streamAIExplanationComments` in the same file. Fixes #9382 ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Fixes "Could not find the file context" error in Explain Changes by adding `relativePath` fallback in file path lookup logic. > > - **Behavior**: > - Fixes intermittent "Error: Could not find the file context" in Explain Changes comment replies by modifying file path lookup logic. > - Updates `handleCommentReply()` in `explainChangesShared.ts` and `onCommentStart` callback in `explainChanges.ts` to use `relativePath` as a fallback when `absolutePath` fails. > - Aligns with existing logic in `streamAIExplanationComments()` which already handles both path formats. > - **Misc**: > - Changes `parseInt` to `Number.parseInt` in `explainChangesShared.ts` for consistency. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for 0542dbfe26c75874b3743bd84dd1505103067bd8. You can [customize](https://app.ellipsis.dev/cline/settings/summaries) this summary. It will automatically update as commits are pushed.

commits.message:
fix: resolve 'Could not find the file context' error in Explain Changes

Both handleCommentReply() in explainChangesShared.ts and the onCommentStart
callback in explainChanges.ts were using a strict absolutePath-only match
when looking up files in changedFiles. If the VS Code comment controller
returns a path in a different format (relative vs absolute, different
separators on Windows), the lookup would silently fail and show
'Error: Could not find the file context'.

Add relativePath as a fallback in both lookup sites, making them
consistent with the already-correct logic in streamAIExplanationComments.

Fixes #9382
Refactor to use parseInt instead of Number.parseInt

title: fix: use JSON_SCHEMA for yaml.load to prevent unsafe deserialization
body: ### Related Issue **Issue:** Aikido security report — unsafe `yaml.load` can lead to remote code execution ### Description Both `yaml.load()` call sites in the codebase use the default schema, which (while safe in js-yaml v4) doesn't explicitly restrict parsing to JSON-compatible types. Since `.clinerules` files and skills can come from untrusted sources (shared repos, community skills), this adds an explicit `{ schema: yaml.JSON_SCHEMA }` to both calls. `JSON_SCHEMA` only allows strings, numbers, booleans, nulls, arrays, and objects — all custom YAML tags (like `!!js/function`, which is the classic RCE vector) are rejected. **Files changed:** - `src/core/context/instructions/user-instructions/frontmatter.ts` — parses YAML frontmatter from `.clinerules` files - `evals/analysis/src/classifier.ts` — loads failure pattern config **Tests added:** - Verifies `!!js/function` and `!!python/object` tags are rejected (fail-open with parseError) - Verifies JSON-compatible YAML values still parse correctly - Verifies classifier throws on YAML with custom tags ### Test Procedure Ran existing unit tests to confirm no regressions: - `frontmatter.test.ts`: all 6 tests pass (3 existing + 3 new) - `classifier.test.ts`: 17/19 pass (new security test passes; 2 pre-existing failures in Provider Bug Detection are unrelated regex pattern mismatches confirmed by running on main) ### Type of Change - [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality) - [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected) - [ ] ♻️ Refactor Changes - [ ] 💅 Cosmetic Changes - [ ] 📚 Documentation update - [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes ### Pre-flight Checklist - [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [x] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - [x] I have created a changeset using `npm run changeset` (required for user-facing changes) - [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Screenshots N/A — backend-only change with no UI impact. ### Additional Notes While js-yaml v4 defaults to a safe schema (unlike v3 which used `DEFAULT_FULL_SCHEMA`), adding `JSON_SCHEMA` explicitly is a defense-in-depth measure. It makes the intent unambiguous, silences security scanners, and protects against future regressions if the dependency were ever downgraded. ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Fixes unsafe YAML deserialization by using `JSON_SCHEMA` in `yaml.load()` to prevent remote code execution. > > - **Security Fix**: > - Use `yaml.JSON_SCHEMA` in `yaml.load()` to prevent unsafe deserialization in `frontmatter.ts` and `classifier.ts`. > - Rejects custom YAML tags like `!!js/function` and `!!python/object`. > - **Tests**: > - Added tests in `frontmatter.test.ts` and `classifier.test.ts` to verify rejection of unsafe tags and correct parsing of JSON-compatible YAML. > - **Behavior**: > - `parseYamlFrontmatter()` and `FailureClassifier` now explicitly use `JSON_SCHEMA` for YAML parsing. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for ec4dfd67c71f2205ea2ac80b866a51ca308cc9fd. title: fix: fetch model info from API in CLI headless auth for Cline and Vercel providers
body: ### Related Issue **Issue:** #7998 ### Description When the CLI runs in headless mode (e.g., `cline auth -p cline -k -m ` in a Docker container), the Cline and Vercel providers silently get wrong model metadata — specifically `maxTokens: 8192` instead of the model's real limit (64K+ for modern Claude models). This causes `write_to_file` to fail on large files because the API truncates the response. #### Root cause `applyProviderConfig()` in `cli/src/utils/provider-config.ts` was calling `controller.readOpenRouterModels()`, which only reads from a **disk cache file**. That cache is populated by `refreshOpenRouterModels()`, which is called when: - The VS Code extension initializes - The interactive ModelPicker component mounts In headless CLI environments (Docker containers, CI, terminal-bench), neither of these runs before `applyProviderConfig`. The disk cache doesn't exist, so `readOpenRouterModels()` returns `undefined`, model info is never set in state, and both `ClineHandler` and `VercelAIGatewayHandler` fall back to `openRouterDefaultModelInfo` with `maxTokens: 8192`. Additionally, `vercel-ai-gateway` was never handled at all in this code path — the model info fetch only ran for `cline` and `openrouter` providers. #### What this PR changes Two things in `applyProviderConfig()`: 1. **Cline/OpenRouter**: Replace `controller.readOpenRouterModels()` (disk-cache-only) with `refreshOpenRouterModels(controller)` (fetches from OpenRouter API if cache is empty, falls back to disk cache if API fails). 2. **Vercel AI Gateway**: Add model info fetching using `refreshVercelAiGatewayModels(controller)`, setting the correct `actModeVercelAiGatewayModelInfo` / `planModeVercelAiGatewayModelInfo` state keys. Both `refresh*` functions have their own in-memory caching and deduplication, so repeated calls are cheap. #### Why this only affected headless mode In the VS Code extension, `refreshOpenRouterModels()` runs on startup and the ModelPicker triggers it again before model selection. By the time a task starts, the disk cache always exists. The headless `auth` command skips both of these, so the cache is empty on first run. ### Test Procedure - `npx tsc --noEmit` passes cleanly — no type errors. - The `refresh*` functions are well-tested in the existing codebase and used extensively by the VS Code extension and CLI ModelPicker. - The change is minimal: swap one function call (disk-only → API-with-fallback) and add one new provider branch. - **What could break:** If both the API fetch and disk cache fail (e.g., no network in a completely offline environment), the model info won't be set and the handler will still fall back to `openRouterDefaultModelInfo`. This is the same behavior as before, so no regression. - Companion PRs #9544 and #9545 address the same `maxTokens` issue from other angles (hardcoded values in `openrouter-stream.ts` and stale static definitions in `api.ts`). ### Type of Change - [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) ### Pre-flight Checklist - [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [x] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Screenshots N/A — backend-only change to CLI provider configuration. No UI changes. ### Additional Notes This is the third of three PRs addressing #7998 (`write_to_file` failures on non-Anthropic providers): | PR | What it fixes | Providers affected | |----|---------------|-------------------| | #9544 | Hardcoded `maxTokens: 8192` switch in `openrouter-stream.ts` | OpenRouter | | #9545 | Stale static `maxTokens: 8192` in `api.ts` model definitions | Anthropic direct, Bedrock, Vertex, SAP AI Core | | **This PR** | CLI headless auth not fetching model info from API | Cline, Vercel AI Gateway | ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Fixes CLI headless mode bug by fetching model info from API for Cline and Vercel providers in `applyProviderConfig()`. > > - **Behavior**: > - In `applyProviderConfig()`, replace `controller.readOpenRouterModels()` with `refreshOpenRouterModels(controller)` for Cline/OpenRouter providers to fetch model info from API. > - Add model info fetching for Vercel AI Gateway using `refreshVercelAiGatewayModels(controller)`. > - **Caching**: > - Both `refreshOpenRouterModels` and `refreshVercelAiGatewayModels` use in-memory caching and deduplication. > - **Fallback**: > - If API fetch and disk cache fail, fallback to `openRouterDefaultModelInfo` remains unchanged. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for db8014800ce20501a5213ad6c7cbe56b1ed8de14. You can [customize](https://app.ellipsis.dev/cline/settings/summaries) this summary. It will automatically update as commits are pushed.

fix: fetch model info from API in CLI headless auth for Cline and Vercel providers

The CLI's applyProviderConfig() was reading model info from a disk
cache (controller.readOpenRouterModels) instead of fetching from
the provider API. In headless/Docker environments (e.g., terminal-bench)
the cache doesn't exist, so model info was never set. Both handlers
then fell back to openRouterDefaultModelInfo with maxTokens: 8192,
causing write_to_file truncation on large outputs.

Changes:
- Replace controller.readOpenRouterModels() (disk cache) with
  refreshOpenRouterModels() (fetches from API, with cache fallback)
- Add vercel-ai-gateway to the model info fetch path using
  refreshVercelAiGatewayModels()

Relates to #7998

title: fix: update stale maxTokens values for Claude 3.7+ models across Anthropic, Bedrock, Vertex, and SAP AI Core
body: ### Related Issue **Issue:** #7998 ### Description The static `maxTokens` values for Claude models in `src/shared/api.ts` have been stuck at `8192` since the Claude 3.5 era. Anthropic has since increased output limits significantly for every model from Claude 3.7 Sonnet onward, but these definitions were never updated. This matters because `maxTokens` is sent as `max_tokens` to the API — it's the ceiling on how many output tokens the model can generate per response. When a model tries to write a large file via `write_to_file` and the content exceeds 8192 tokens, the response gets silently truncated. The tool call JSON is incomplete, the `content` parameter is lost, and the user sees: ``` Cline tried to use write_to_file without value for required parameter 'content'. Retrying... ``` The retry also fails (same ceiling), and the agent dies after 3-4 API calls. #### Which providers are affected? These static definitions are the source of truth for providers that don't fetch model info dynamically: | Provider | Uses `api.ts` static values? | Affected? | |----------|------------------------------|-----------| | **Anthropic direct** | Yes (`anthropicModels`) | **Yes — fixed in this PR** | | **AWS Bedrock** | Yes (`bedrockModels`) | **Yes — fixed in this PR** | | **Google Vertex AI** | Yes (`vertexModels`) | **Yes — fixed in this PR** | | **SAP AI Core** | Yes (`sapAiCoreModels`) | **Yes — fixed in this PR** | | OpenRouter | No — fetches dynamically | Not affected (fixed in #9544) | | Vercel AI Gateway | No — fetches dynamically | Not affected (API reports correct values) | | Cline provider | No — uses OpenRouter stream | Not affected (fixed in #9544) | #### What changed? Updated 41 `maxTokens` entries across four model definition sections: | Model | Old Value | New Value | Source | |-------|-----------|-----------|--------| | Claude Opus 4.6 | 8,192 | **128,000** | [Anthropic docs](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/about-claude/models/overview) | | Claude 3.7 Sonnet | 8,192 | **128,000** | [AWS Bedrock docs](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/model-parameters-anthropic-claude-37.html) | | Claude Sonnet 4.6 | 8,192 | **64,000** | [Anthropic docs](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/about-claude/models/overview) | | Claude Sonnet 4.5 | 8,192 | **64,000** | [Anthropic docs](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/about-claude/models/overview) | | Claude Sonnet 4 | 8,192 | **64,000** | [Google Vertex AI docs](https://docs.cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/generative-ai/docs/partner-models/claude/sonnet-4) | | Claude Haiku 4.5 | 8,192 | **64,000** | [Anthropic docs](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/about-claude/models/overview) | | Claude Opus 4.5 | 8,192 | **64,000** | [Google Vertex AI docs](https://docs.cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/generative-ai/docs/partner-models/claude/opus-4-5) | | Claude Opus 4.1 | 8,192 | **32,000** | [Google Vertex AI docs](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/generative-ai/docs/partner-models/claude/opus-4-1) | | Claude Opus 4 | 8,192 | **32,000** | [model.wiki](https://model.wiki/providers/anthropic/models/claude-opus-4-20250514/) | Claude 3.5 Sonnet and Claude 3.5 Haiku remain at 8,192 (correct). All `:1m` variants, Bedrock `v1:0` variants, and Vertex `@date` variants are updated to match their base model. #### Important nuance: `max_tokens` semantics differ by API - **Anthropic native API** (used by Anthropic direct, Bedrock, Vertex): `max_tokens` is the **total** budget for thinking + output when extended thinking is enabled. E.g. `max_tokens: 64000` with `budget_tokens: 10000` leaves 54,000 for output. - **OpenAI-compatible APIs** (OpenRouter, Vercel): `max_tokens` is output only; thinking budget is separate via `reasoning.max_tokens`. This means the values in this PR serve double duty — they're both the output ceiling for non-thinking requests and the total budget for thinking requests. The values match what Anthropic documents as the max for each model. ### Test Procedure - Verified values against Anthropic's official documentation, AWS Bedrock docs, Google Vertex AI docs, and the Vercel AI Gateway API (`https://ai-gateway.vercel.sh/v1/models`). - Cross-referenced with OpenRouter's dynamic model info (which already reports the correct values). - `npm run compile` passes cleanly — no type errors. - The companion PR #9544 was tested with runtime instrumentation on OpenRouter, proving that the old 8192 ceiling caused truncation (`finish_reason: "length"`, `completion_tokens: 8192`) and that raising the limit resolved it (`finish_reason: "tool_calls"`, `completion_tokens: 9824`). The same truncation mechanism applies to all providers using these static definitions. **What could break:** The model will now be allowed to generate longer outputs, which could increase latency and cost for very long responses. However, the model only generates what it needs — `max_tokens` is a ceiling, not a target. The old 8192 value was artificially preventing the model from completing valid tool calls. ### Type of Change - [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) ### Pre-flight Checklist - [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [x] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Screenshots N/A — data-only change to model definitions. No UI changes. ### Additional Notes - Companion to #9544, which fixes the same issue for OpenRouter by removing a hardcoded switch that overrode the (correct) dynamic value. - Vercel AI Gateway was independently verified to report correct values via its public API, so it does not need a fix. - A reference document with all sources is available at `docs/claude-max-output-tokens-reference-2026-02-24.md` in the repo. ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Updated `maxTokens` values for Claude 3.7+ models in `src/shared/api.ts` to handle larger outputs across multiple providers. > > - **Behavior**: > - Updated `maxTokens` values for Claude models in `src/shared/api.ts` to reflect increased limits from Claude 3.7+ models. > - Affects `anthropicModels`, `bedrockModels`, `vertexModels`, and `sapAiCoreModels`. > - Ensures models can handle larger outputs without truncation. > - **Models**: > - `maxTokens` for `claude-opus-4-6` updated to 128,000. > - `maxTokens` for `claude-sonnet-4-6` and `claude-sonnet-4-5` updated to 64,000. > - `maxTokens` for `claude-opus-4-1` and `claude-opus-4` updated to 32,000. > - **Misc**: > - Verified against official documentation and dynamic model info from OpenRouter. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for 6ffe11fdd9d496e52e61f10691727657b49bc8eb. You can [customize](https://app.ellipsis.dev/cline/settings/summaries) this summary. It will automatically update as commits are pushed.

commits.message:
fix: update stale maxTokens values for Claude 3.7+ models

Every Claude model from 3.7 Sonnet onward had maxTokens set to 8192
in the static model definitions. These values were correct for Claude
3.5 and earlier, but Anthropic has significantly increased output
limits for newer models:

- Claude Opus 4.6: 128K (was 8192, 15.6x too low)
- Claude 3.7 Sonnet: 128K (was 8192, 15.6x too low)
- Claude Sonnet 4.6/4.5/4, Haiku 4.5, Opus 4.5: 64K (was 8192)
- Claude Opus 4, Opus 4.1: 32K (was 8192)

These static definitions are the source of truth for Anthropic direct,
Bedrock, Vertex, and SAP AI Core providers. With the old values, any
write_to_file call exceeding 8192 output tokens would be silently
truncated, producing a missing 'content' parameter error.

Values verified against Anthropic docs, AWS Bedrock docs, Google
Vertex AI docs, and the Vercel AI Gateway API.

Relates to #7998

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

fix: update openRouterDefaultModelInfo.maxTokens to 64K

This fallback ModelInfo (representing claude-sonnet-4.5) is used
when dynamic model info isn't available — notably by the Cline and
Vercel providers in the CLI when the model cache is empty (e.g.,
fresh Docker containers in terminal-bench).

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

title: fix: use model.info.maxTokens for OpenRouter instead of hardcoded 8192
body: ### Related Issue **Issue:** #7998 ### Description `write_to_file` fails with `missing required parameter 'content'` on OpenRouter (and the Cline provider, which shares the same code path) when the model tries to write a large file. The root cause is a hardcoded `max_tokens = 8192` in the OpenRouter stream transform that silently overrides the correct dynamic value from `model.info.maxTokens`. **What happens:** 1. Model generates a `write_to_file` tool call with large `content` (e.g. a 500-line file) 2. Output hits the 8192-token ceiling → provider returns `finish_reason: "length"` 3. `StreamResponseHandler` tries to finalize the incomplete tool call JSON 4. `JSON.parse` fails → regex fallback extracts `path` (short, complete) but not `content` (truncated mid-string) 5. `WriteToFileToolHandler` rejects: `missing required parameter 'content'` 6. Retry hits the same ceiling → agent dies after 3-4 API calls **Why this exists:** The switch statement was written when 8192 was the actual max output for Claude models. Modern Claude models support much higher limits (128K for Opus 4.6, 64K for Sonnet 4.6/4.5). OpenRouter's API already reports the correct value via `top_provider.max_completion_tokens`, and `model.info.maxTokens` reflects this — but the hardcoded switch was overriding it. **The fix:** Replace the 30-line hardcoded switch with `const maxTokens = model.info.maxTokens || undefined` — matching what the Vercel AI Gateway transform already does. ### Test Procedure Debugged with runtime instrumentation. Before/after comparison: **Before fix** (hardcoded `max_tokens: 8192`): - `max_tokens` sent to OpenRouter: **8192** - `completion_tokens` returned: **8192** (hit ceiling exactly) - `finish_reason`: **"length"** (truncated) - `write_to_file` result: **missing content parameter** ❌ **After fix** (`max_tokens: 128000` from `model.info`): - `max_tokens` sent to OpenRouter: **128000** - `completion_tokens` returned: **9824** (model needed ~20% more than old ceiling allowed) - `finish_reason`: **"tool_calls"** (completed normally) - `write_to_file` result: **succeeded**, 35,070 chars written ✅ Tested by prompting Cline with OpenRouter + Claude Sonnet 4.6 to write a large single-file Python implementation (~600 lines). Previously failed deterministically; now succeeds. **What could break:** Models not in the switch statement previously got `maxTokens = undefined`, which let OpenRouter use its own default. After this change, they get `model.info.maxTokens`, which should be equivalent or better since model info is populated from OpenRouter's API response. For models where `model.info.maxTokens` is 0 or undefined, the `|| undefined` fallback preserves the old behavior. ### Type of Change - [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) ### Pre-flight Checklist - [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [x] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Screenshots N/A — backend-only change. No UI changes. ### Additional Notes - The Cline provider (`src/core/api/providers/cline.ts`) also benefits from this fix since it calls `createOpenRouterStream` internally. - The Vercel AI Gateway transform already uses `model.info?.maxTokens || undefined` and does not have this bug. - A separate follow-up may be needed to update the static `maxTokens: 8192` values in `src/shared/api.ts` for `anthropicModels`, `bedrockModels`, and `vertexModels`, which affect the Anthropic direct, Bedrock, and Vertex providers. ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Fixes token limit issue in `createOpenRouterStream` by using `model.info.maxTokens` instead of a hardcoded value. > > - **Behavior**: > - Replaces hardcoded `maxTokens = 8192` with `model.info.maxTokens || undefined` in `createOpenRouterStream` in `openrouter-stream.ts`. > - Fixes issue where large `write_to_file` calls failed due to token limit being exceeded. > - **Impact**: > - Affects OpenRouter and Cline providers using `createOpenRouterStream`. > - Ensures models use correct token limits from `model.info`. > - **Misc**: > - Removes 30-line switch statement for `maxTokens` in `openrouter-stream.ts`. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for edb3e068eff0a06d03f83708459f0ddeda26fa41. You can [customize](https://app.ellipsis.dev/cline/settings/summaries) this summary. It will automatically update as commits are pushed.

commits.message:
fix: use model.info.maxTokens for OpenRouter instead of hardcoded 8192

The OpenRouter stream transform had a 30-line switch statement that
hardcoded max_tokens=8192 for every Claude model. This was written when
8192 was the actual max output for Claude, but modern Claude models
support much higher limits (e.g. 128K for Sonnet 4.6, 64K for others).

OpenRouter's API already reports the correct max_completion_tokens per
model, and model.info.maxTokens reflects this (128000 for Sonnet 4.6).
The hardcoded switch was silently overriding the dynamic value.

This caused write_to_file failures on OpenRouter (and the Cline
provider, which shares this code path) whenever the tool call content
exceeded 8192 output tokens. The response was truncated
(finish_reason: "length"), producing incomplete JSON that lost the
content parameter.

Runtime evidence:
- Before: max_tokens=8192 sent, completion_tokens=8192 (ceiling),
  finish_reason="length", write_to_file content missing
- After: max_tokens=128000 sent, completion_tokens=9824 (needed more
  than 8192), finish_reason="tool_calls", write_to_file succeeded

Fixes #7998

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

title: Add Additional Markdown Formatting in CLI
body: ### Related Issue **Issue:** #XXXX ### Description The CLI currently partially supports markdown formatting when Cline gives a response. This change uses the marked library to handle more markdown formatting cases including heading, lists, blockquotes etc. ### Test Procedure Ask Cline to explain something to you or specifically ask for a markdown formatted response. Here's the one I used ``` Without using any tools, respond with a message that demonstrates all of these markdown features in a single response: 1. A level 1 heading (# Heading) 2. A level 2 heading (## Heading) │ 3. A level 3 heading (### Heading) 4. A regular paragraph with **bold text**, *italic text*, and `inline code` 5. An unordered list with 3 items 6. An ordered list with 3 items 7. A fenced code block with some example JavaScript code 8. A blockquote 9. A link like [Example](https://example.com) 10. Nested list items (a list inside a list item) Make sure each feature is clearly labeled so I can verify the rendering. ``` ### Type of Change - [ ] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality) - [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected) - [ ] ♻️ Refactor Changes - [x] 💅 Cosmetic Changes - [ ] 📚 Documentation update - [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes ### Pre-flight Checklist - [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [x] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - [ ] I have created a changeset using `npm run changeset` (required for user-facing changes) - [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Screenshots **Before** **After** ### Additional Notes ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Enhance CLI markdown rendering using `marked` library for comprehensive markdown support in `ChatMessage.tsx`. > > - **Behavior**: > - Enhanced markdown rendering in CLI using `marked` library in `ChatMessage.tsx`. > - Supports headings, lists, blockquotes, code blocks, links, and more. > - **Dependencies**: > - Added `marked` and `@types/marked` to `package.json`. > - **Functions**: > - Introduced `renderTokens()` and `renderToken()` in `ChatMessage.tsx` for rendering markdown tokens. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for 1572f0a4b945c82f7faac6cb5a56f5137b27492c. title: feat: add thinking to MiniMax M2.5 and add the M2.5-highspeed model to MiniMax provider
body: ### Related Issue **Issue:** #9391 ### Description Adds MiniMax M2.5 to the native MiniMax provider so users can use it directly with their own MiniMax API key. Previously, M2.5 was only available as a free model through the Cline/OpenRouter provider but wasn't listed in the MiniMax provider's model dropdown. Changes: - Added `MiniMax-M2.5` to `minimaxModels` in `src/shared/api.ts` with 192K context window, 128K max output tokens, prompt caching support, reasoning/thinking support, and pricing - Updated `minimaxDefaultModelId` from `MiniMax-M2.1` to `MiniMax-M2.5` - Added `minimax/minimax-m2.5` to the prompt caching switch case in `src/core/api/transform/openrouter-stream.ts` for OpenRouter users No changes needed to the MiniMax handler (`src/core/api/providers/minimax.ts`) since it already handles thinking/reasoning blocks via the Anthropic SDK. The CLI ModelPicker, model-filters, and system prompt variants all automatically pick up the new model. ### Test Procedure - Verified TypeScript compilation passes with no new errors in changed files - The existing `MinimaxHandler` uses the Anthropic SDK and already handles thinking/reasoning stream events, so M2.5 will work through the same code path as M2.1 - The model entry follows the exact same pattern as existing MiniMax models (M2.1, M2.1-lightning, M2) - CLI ModelPicker imports `minimaxModels`/`minimaxDefaultModelId` from shared api.ts, so it automatically picks up the new model - Model filter in `model-filters.ts` uses `includes("minimax-m2")` which matches `minimax-m2.5` ### Type of Change - [ ] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [x] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality) - [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected) - [ ] ♻️ Refactor Changes - [ ] 💅 Cosmetic Changes - [ ] 📚 Documentation update - [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes ### Pre-flight Checklist - [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [x] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - [ ] I have created a changeset using `npm run changeset` (required for user-facing changes) - [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Screenshots N/A - Backend-only change adding a model entry to the provider's model list. ### Additional Notes Reference: [MiniMax Anthropic API docs](https://platform.minimax.io/docs/api-reference/text-anthropic-api) ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Add MiniMax M2.5 model to MiniMax provider, updating default model and ensuring compatibility with existing systems. > > - **Behavior**: > - Add `MiniMax-M2.5` to `minimaxModels` in `api.ts` with 192K context window, 128K max tokens, prompt caching, reasoning support, and pricing. > - Update `minimaxDefaultModelId` to `MiniMax-M2.5`. > - Add `minimax/minimax-m2.5` to prompt caching in `openrouter-stream.ts`. > - **Compatibility**: > - No changes to `MinimaxHandler` needed; existing Anthropic SDK handles reasoning. > - CLI ModelPicker and model-filters automatically recognize new model. > - **Testing**: > - Verified TypeScript compilation with no new errors. > - Model entry follows existing MiniMax model patterns. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for 85524ae1bc7aa4ff44a06524f5bfd708b0d8b39f. title: Remove voice mode UI and disable dictation
body:
### Related Issue

**Issue:** #XXXX

### Description

This PR fully removes dictation/voice-to-text from Cline rather than just hiding the UI.

Scope:
- Removed dictation UI/state wiring from extension/webview state contracts.
- Removed dictation runtime stack:
  - `src/core/controller/dictation/*`
  - `src/services/dictation/*`
  - `src/shared/audioProgramConstants.ts`
  - `src/shared/DictationSettings.ts`
- Removed dictation proto service and state hooks:
  - deleted `proto/cline/dictation.proto`
  - removed `dictation_settings` fields from `Settings` and `UpdateSettingsRequest`
  - added `reserved 23` in `UpdateSettingsRequest` to preserve field history
- Removed account transcription method and dictation telemetry capture/event constants.
- Removed deprecated dictation docs page.

### Test Procedure

- Ran `npm run protos` to regenerate protobus/grpc wiring after proto changes.
- Ran `npx tsc --noEmit` at repo root.
- Ran `cd webview-ui && npx tsc --noEmit`.
- Ran `npm run lint` (including proto lint).

### Type of Change

-   [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
-   [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
-   [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected)
-   [x] ♻️ Refactor Changes
-   [ ] 💅 Cosmetic Changes
-   [ ] 📚 Documentation update
-   [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes

### Pre-flight Checklist

-   [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs)
-   [ ] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`)
-   [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Screenshots

N/A.

### Additional Notes

This intentionally removes the dictation feature stack entirely from this repo branch.

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR prevents internal placeholder tools (notably `focus_chain`) from being included in the provider-native tools payload, avoiding downstream tool-schema validation failures (e.g., AWS Bedrock rejecting empty tool descriptions).

**Changes:**
- Filtered native tool export to exclude tools with blank descriptions.
- Added assertions in integration/unit tests ensuring `focus_chain` is not present in native tools output.
- Updated native-tools snapshots across affected model/provider combinations to reflect the removal.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 7 out of 7 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| src/core/prompts/system-prompt/registry/ClineToolSet.ts | Filters out blank-description tools from the native tools list; minor label access change. |
| src/core/prompts/system-prompt/__tests__/integration.test.ts | Adds checks ensuring `focus_chain` is absent from generated native tools across snapshot tests. |

title: fix(gemini): add Gemini 3.1 Pro to GCP and Gemini
body: ### Related Issue Issue: N/A (reported by user in r/CLine: Gemini 3.1 not available via Google provider) ### Description This fixes a model availability regression where users could not select Gemini 3.1 Pro from the Google provider picker, even though the model was available upstream and worked when manually patched. The root cause was that static provider model definitions still used gemini-3-pro-preview as the only Gemini 3 Pro entry for Google/Gemini and Vertex model catalogs. In practice, this meant 3.1 was missing from provider-managed model lists even while related parts of the product already referenced 3.1 IDs. Technical approach: - Added gemini-3.1-pro-preview as a first-class model entry in shared model catalogs. - Kept gemini-3-pro-preview as a legacy-compatible entry so existing saved settings/configs continue to resolve cleanly. - Set Gemini provider default to gemini-3.1-pro-preview. - Kept Vertex default on gemini-3-pro-preview to avoid behavior change for Vertex users right now. - Updated surrounding model-ID references where stale 3.0/old Pro IDs would otherwise create inconsistent UX. - Updated user-facing copy that advertised the old default. Debugging journey and decisions: - The initial user report mentioned they had to patch built assets, which usually points to source-level model registry drift. - Tracing provider resolution showed both Gemini and Vertex handlers depend on shared static maps, so fixing only one callsite would have been incomplete. - There was a tradeoff between fully switching defaults everywhere versus preserving compatibility. We chose compatibility for Vertex default while still exposing 3.1 in both providers. - Another subtle issue was temperature logic for Gemini 3 model IDs in OpenRouter/Vercel transforms; this used 3.0-specific prefixes. The logic was generalized to Gemini 3 family IDs so 3.1 inherits expected request defaults. Non-obvious gotchas: - Existing users may have persisted model IDs. Removing old IDs would silently break restore paths. Keeping gemini-3-pro-preview as a supported alias avoids this. - Provider catalogs, onboarding/recommended models, docs, and transformation logic can drift independently. This fix intentionally aligns them so one release does not reintroduce confusion. ### Test Procedure What I tested: - Type safety and compile validation: - npx tsc --noEmit - npm run check-types - Unit tests: - npm run test:unit -- src/core/api/providers/__tests__/vercel-ai-gateway.test.ts - This command executed the unit suite in this repo context and passed. What could break and how it was checked: - Model resolution fallback paths: validated by unit coverage around model ID retention/fallback behavior. - Config compatibility with older saved IDs: maintained by preserving gemini-3-pro-preview entries rather than replacing them. - Request parameter defaults for Gemini 3 family through OpenRouter/Vercel paths: validated by code-path updates and successful type/test runs. Why this is ready: - The provider model source-of-truth now includes both new and legacy IDs. - Defaults are set intentionally per provider. - Build and tests pass after the changes. ### Type of Change - [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality) - [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected) - [ ] ♻️ Refactor Changes - [ ] 💅 Cosmetic Changes - [x] 📚 Documentation update - [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes ### Pre-flight Checklist - [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [ ] Tests are passing (npm test) and code is formatted and linted (npm run format && npm run lint) - [ ] I have created a changeset using npm run changeset (required for user-facing changes) - [x] I have reviewed contributor guidelines ### Screenshots No UI visual change requiring screenshots. ### Additional Notes If maintainers prefer a changeset entry for this user-facing model picker fix, I can add one in a follow-up commit on this branch. ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Adds `gemini-3.1-pro-preview` to Google provider, updates defaults and temperature logic, and maintains backward compatibility. > > - **Behavior**: > - Adds `gemini-3.1-pro-preview` as a first-class model entry in `geminiModels` and `vertexModels`. > - Sets `gemini-3.1-pro-preview` as the default for Google provider, keeps `gemini-3-pro-preview` for Vertex. > - Updates temperature logic for Gemini 3 models in `openrouter-stream.ts` and `vercel-ai-gateway-stream.ts` to use family IDs. > - **Documentation**: > - Updates `google-gemini.mdx` to reflect `gemini-3.1-pro-preview` as the default model. > - **Tests**: > - Updates `vercel-ai-gateway.test.ts` to use `gemini-3.1-pro-preview` in test cases. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for bc4f76667922de69037ea6eaa2cf0e25c4d2ca37. You can [customize](https://app.ellipsis.dev/cline/settings/summaries) this summary. It will automatically update as commits are pushed.

## Pull request overview

This PR adds support for configuring skills and making modelId optional in subagent configurations. It also refactors token usage tracking to use structured state objects and updates host retrieval to use `HostRegistryInfo` instead of `HostProvider`.

**Changes:**
- Add optional `skills` field to agent configuration schemas to allow subagents to be restricted to specific skills
- Make `modelId` optional in agent configurations for more flexibility
- Refactor token usage tracking from individual variables to structured `SubagentUsageState` and `SubagentRequestUsageState` objects for better state management
- Replace `HostProvider.env.getHostVersion()` async call with `HostRegistryInfo.get()` cached access

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| src/core/task/tools/subagent/AgentConfigLoader.ts | Adds `skills` parsing functions and makes `modelId` optional in both schemas |
| src/core/task/tools/subagent/SubagentBuilder.ts | Adds `getConfiguredSkills()` method to expose configured skills |
| src/core/task/tools/subagent/SubagentRunner.ts | Implements skill filtering logic based on agent configuration, refactors token usage tracking, and replaces host provider call with registry access |
| src/core/task/tools/subagent/__tests__/SubagentRunner.test.ts | Adds test for token tracking refactoring to ensure prior request totals are passed to compaction check |

While not a bug since both calculations produce the same result, this adds unnecessary computational overhead. Consider removing this duplicate calculation.

commits.message:
refactor: consolidate subagent request usage tracking into state object

Replace scattered per-request token tracking variables with a structured
`SubagentUsageState` interface containing `currentRequest` and
`lastRequest` states. This improves code organization by grouping related
token metrics (input, output, cache write/read, total tokens, cost) into
a cohesive `SubagentRequestUsageState` object, reducing variable sprawl
and making the usage lifecycle (current → last) more explicit.

feat(subagent): add support for skills and optional modelId in agent config

- Update `AgentBaseConfigSchema` and `AgentConfigFrontmatterSchema` to include an optional `skills` field and make `modelId` optional.
- Implement `parseSkills` and `normalizeSkillName` in `AgentConfigLoader` to handle skill parsing from YAML frontmatter.
- Update `SubagentBuilder` to provide access to configured skills.
- Modify `SubagentRunner` to filter available skills based on the agent's configuration, falling back to all available skills if none are specified.
- Update host retrieval to use `HostRegistryInfo` instead of `HostProvider`.

This allows subagents to be restricted to specific skills and provides more flexibility in model configuration.

title: Remove all traces of changeset-converter.yml GitHub Action and npm run changeset [ENG-1543]
body: ### Related Issue **Issue:** [ENG-1543] ### Description We are removing the changeset part of the PR contribution process. @arafatkatze and I have discussed it and determined that we don't need developers to run `npm run changeset`. As far as we understand, the original intention was to support releases by automating changelog generation for the `cline/CHANGELOG.md` file. It seems that we have a better, simpler way to do that now in our internal release process. This PR removes all mention of `npm run changeset` and the `changeset-convertor.yml` GitHub Action. ### Test Procedure ### Manual Testing 1. Pull this branch locally. 2. Confirm Changesets tooling is removed: - `package.json` no longer includes `@changesets/cli` - `npm run changeset` script is removed - `.github/workflows/changeset-converter.yml` is removed - PR template no longer asks contributors to run `npm run changeset` 3. Verify no Changesets references remain in lockfile: - Search `package-lock.json` for `@changesets` (should return none). 4. Run project validation: - `npm run compile` - `npm run lint` - `npm run test` 5. Sanity check docs/process updates: - `CONTRIBUTING.md` and `.github/pull_request_template.md` reflect that contributors do __not__ create changelog-entry files. ### Expected Result - All commands pass. - No CI/workflow or contributor guidance still depends on Changesets tooling. ### Type of Change - [ ] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality) - [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected) - [ ] ♻️ Refactor Changes - [ ] 💅 Cosmetic Changes - [x] 📚 Documentation update - [x] 🏃 Workflow Changes ### Pre-flight Checklist - [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [x] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - REMOVED: [ ] I have created a changeset using `npm run changeset` (required for user-facing changes) - [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Screenshots n/a ### Additional Notes n/a ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > This PR removes changeset tooling and updates documentation to simplify the release process by eliminating the need for contributors to create changesets. > > - **Removal of Changeset Tooling**: > - Removed `@changesets/cli` from `package.json` and `package-lock.json`. > - Deleted `.github/workflows/changeset-converter.yml` and `.github/scripts/overwrite_changeset_changelog.py`. > - Removed `.changeset/README.md` and `.changie.yaml`. > - **Documentation Updates**: > - Updated `CONTRIBUTING.md` and `locales/ko/CONTRIBUTING.md` to reflect that contributors do not need to create changelog-entry files. > - Modified `.github/pull_request_template.md` to remove changeset creation step. > - Updated `.clinerules/general.md` and `.clinerules/workflows/*` to remove references to changesets. > - **Miscellaneous**: > - title: ci: add automatic retries for smoke test jobs
body: ### Related Issue **Issue:** #XXXX ### Description Smoke tests can fail intermittently due to flaky API calls or transient infrastructure issues. This adds automatic retry logic (up to 3 attempts with a 10-second delay between retries) to the smoke test step in the CI workflow, reducing false negatives that block PRs. ### Test Procedure This is a CI workflow change. The retry logic will be exercised the next time smoke tests run on a PR that touches the relevant paths. The bash retry loop was verified for correctness by inspection. ### Type of Change - [ ] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality) - [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected) - [ ] ♻️ Refactor Changes - [ ] 💅 Cosmetic Changes - [ ] 📚 Documentation update - [x] 🏃 Workflow Changes ### Pre-flight Checklist - [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [x] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - [x] I have created a changeset using `npm run changeset` (required for user-facing changes) - [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Screenshots N/A — CI workflow change only. ### Additional Notes The retry wraps only the test execution step. Auth is performed once before the retry loop. Each attempt is grouped in GitHub Actions logs for readability, with `::warning::` annotations on retry and `::error::` if all 3 attempts fail. ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Adds retry logic to smoke test CI jobs to handle flaky failures by retrying up to 3 times. > > - **CI Workflow**: > - Adds retry logic to `cline-evals-regression.yml` for smoke tests, retrying up to 3 times with a 10-second delay. > - Logs each attempt with `::group::` and `::endgroup::` for readability in GitHub Actions. > - Uses `::warning::` for retries and `::error::` if all attempts fail. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for 961fba9e366f0f7fd35ee9a382417df333607376. title: feat: add AgentConfigLoader for file-based agent configs (ENG-1547)
body: ### Related Issue **Issue:** #XXXX ### Description Add AgentConfigLoader singleton to manage agent configurations loaded from YAML files in the agents directory `~/Documents/Agents` (where Skills and Hooks are configured). Supports hot-reloading via file watcher, validates config schema with Zod, and integrates with extension lifecycle (StateManager initialization and tearDown disposal). Overview of the agent configuration yaml file content: ```yaml --- name: name-of-the-subagent description: Reviews code for quality and best practices tools: Names of the default Cline tool that this subagent has access to, seperated by ",". Default Cline tools: "ask_followup_question", "attempt_completion", "execute_command", "replace_in_file", "read_file", "write_to_file", "search_files", "list_files", "list_code_definition_names", "browser_action", "use_mcp_tool", "access_mcp_resource", "load_mcp_documentation", "new_task", "plan_mode_respond", "act_mode_respond", "web_fetch", "web_search", "condense", "summarize_task", "report_bug", "new_rule", "apply_patch", "generate_explanation", "use_skill", "use_subagents", modelId: model id for the current selected provider --- System prompt for the subagent ``` Also update PromptRegistry to load synchronous and simplify variant lookup - Convert async load() to synchronous, called in constructor as both loadVariants and loadComponents are not async functions - Remove health check logic and loaded state tracking - Extract getVariant() method with proper generic fallback - Add getComponents() accessor and simplify component loading - Convert variant/component loaders from async to synchronous - Remove unnecessary await calls throughout the class ### Test Procedure - Create a `subagent.yml` file at `~/Documents/Agents/subagent.yml` - Create a `code-reviewer.yaml` file at `~/Documents/Agents/subagent.yaml` - Verify main agents can use all the tools assigned available to the model variant. - Verify subagents can only execute allowed tools Test Prompt in cline repo: do we clean up logs files generated by Terminal command? ```yaml --- name: subagent description: Be a helpful coding assistant that can use tools to answer user's question and complete coding tasks. tools: execute_command, write_to_file, replace_in_file modelId: moonshotai/kimi-k2.5 --- Be a helpful coding assistant that can use tools to answer user's question and complete coding tasks. ``` Test Prompt in cline repo: use code reviewer subagent to summarize the last commit ```yaml --- name: code-reviewer description: Reviews code for quality and best practices tools: execute_command, read_file modelId: anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6 --- You are a code reviewer. When invoked, analyze the code using git commands and provide specific, actionable feedback on quality, security, and best practices. ``` https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/42d7f835-3fa0-4cc7-9fb7-164db711da1b ### Type of Change - [ ] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality) - [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected) - [x] ♻️ Refactor Changes - [ ] 💅 Cosmetic Changes - [ ] 📚 Documentation update - [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes ### Pre-flight Checklist - [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [x] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - [ ] I have created a changeset using `npm run changeset` (required for user-facing changes) - [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Screenshots ### Additional Notes ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Introduces `AgentConfigLoader` for YAML-based agent configurations, updates `PromptRegistry` and `ToolExecutor` for dynamic tool management, and adds tests for new functionalities. > > - **AgentConfigLoader**: > - Introduces `AgentConfigLoader` to manage agent configurations from YAML files in `AgentConfigLoader.ts`. > - Supports hot-reloading via file watcher and validates config schema with Zod. > - Integrates with `StateManager` for initialization and disposal. > - **PromptRegistry & PromptBuilder**: > - Updates `PromptRegistry` to load synchronously and manage tool specs in `PromptRegistry.ts`. > - Refactors `PromptBuilder` to handle tool prompts and placeholders in `PromptBuilder.ts`. > - **Tool Management**: > - Refactors `ToolExecutor` and `ToolExecutorCoordinator` to register tools dynamically using `toolUseNames` in `ToolExecutor.ts` and `ToolExecutorCoordinator.ts`. > - Adds `UseSubagentsToolHandler` to handle subagent tool execution in `SubagentToolHandler.ts`. > - **Testing**: > - Adds tests for `AgentConfigLoader`, `SubagentBuilder`, and `SubagentRunner` in `AgentConfigLoader.test.ts`, `SubagentBuilder.test.ts`, and `SubagentRunner.test.ts`. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for 3932635d973c636b834eaf5882983ddcc9fc8030. reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR refactors the tool handler registration system to centralize tool handler instantiation and improve tool access control for subagents. The changes consolidate scattered tool registration logic into a single source of truth and convert the PromptRegistry to use synchronous loading.

**Changes:**
- Centralized all tool handler instantiation in `ToolExecutorCoordinator.toolHandlersMap` with factory functions for each tool
- Added `registerByName` method to allow runtime filtering of tools based on allowed tool lists
- Converted PromptRegistry's `load()`, `loadVariants()`, and `loadComponents()` from async to synchronous since no async operations are performed
- Extracted `getVariant()` method in PromptRegistry with proper generic fallback logic
- Updated SubagentRunner to use the coordinator pattern and filter tools to only those in `SUBAGENT_ALLOWED_TOOLS`

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 4 out of 5 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| src/core/task/tools/ToolExecutorCoordinator.ts | Added centralized toolHandlersMap and registerByName method for filtered tool registration |
| src/core/task/ToolExecutor.ts | Simplified tool registration to use registerByName instead of manual registration for each tool |
| src/core/task/tools/subagent/SubagentRunner.ts | Uses coordinator pattern with filtered tool registration and reuses baseConfig.api instead of creating new API handler |
| src/core/prompts/system-prompt/registry/PromptRegistry.ts | Converted async load methods to synchronous, extracted getVariant method, removed unused health check logic |
| package-lock.json | Added peer:true flag to @babel/core (npm lock file format change) |
</details>

commits.message:
refactor: centralize tool handler registration and filter by allowed tools

- Create centralized toolHandlersMap for all tool handler instantiation
- Add registerToolHandlers method to register only allowed tools from config
- Filter subagent tools to only include allowed tools from allowedTools config
- Remove scattered tool handler registration logic in favor of single source of truth
- Improve maintainability by consolidating tool handler creation in one place

This refactoring ensures subagents only have access to explicitly allowed tools
and makes the tool registration process more maintainable and consistent.

It also improves separation of concerns by having the coordinator manage all tool handler registration, while the executor focuses on orchestration. The allowedTools parameter enables runtime filtering of available tools for different contexts.

Also update PromptRegistry to load synchronous and simplify variant lookup

- Convert async load() to synchronous, called in constructor as both loadVariants and loadComponents are not async functions
- Remove health check logic and loaded state tracking
- Extract getVariant() method with proper generic fallback
- Add getComponents() accessor and simplify component loading
- Convert variant/component loaders from async to synchronous
- Remove unnecessary await calls throughout the codebase
- Add PromptRegistry tests for variant resolution and components
fix test

Add AgentConfigLoader singleton to manage agent configurations loaded from
YAML files in the agents directory. Supports hot-reloading via file watcher,
validates config schema with Zod, and integrates with extension lifecycle
(StateManager initialization and tearDown disposal).

feat(tools): implement dynamic tool registration for subagents

Updates the tool system to support dynamically registered subagents as individual tools.

- Modifies `ClineToolSet` to generate specific tool definitions for configured subagents via `AgentConfigLoader`.
- Updates `parseAssistantMessageV2` to use `getToolUseNames()` instead of a static list, enabling the parser to recognize dynamic tool tags.
- Replaces the generic `USE_SUBAGENTS` tool with specific subagent instances when available in the system prompt context.
Merge branch 'main' into bee/tools-allowed

update config path and refine tool descriptions

- Relocate the subagent configuration directory from `~/.cline/data/agents` to `~/Documents/Cline/Agents` to improve user accessibility.
- Update subagent tool descriptions and parameter instructions in the system prompt to be more descriptive and helpful for the model.

fix: await AgentConfigLoader initialization before StateManager completes

Ensure agent configs are fully loaded during StateManager initialization
by awaiting the `ready()` promise. Previously, `AgentConfigLoader` was
instantiated without waiting for the initial load to complete, causing
potential race conditions where configs might not be available when
needed.

- Add `initialLoadPromise` field to track the async initial load
- Expose a `ready()` method to allow callers to await initialization
- Await `AgentConfigLoader.getInstance().ready()` in StateManager

title: fix: prevent reasoning delta crash on usage-only stream chunks
body: ### Related Issue **Issue:** #9427 ### Description This PR fixes a streaming crash seen in CLI and VSCode extension sessions: `Cannot use 'in' operator to search for 'reasoning' in undefined`. Root cause: Some provider handlers checked reasoning fields with the JavaScript `in` operator before validating that `delta` existed. Usage-only stream chunks can omit `choices[0].delta`, which made those checks throw. What changed: - Guarded reasoning and reasoning-details checks with `delta && ...` in: - `openrouter.ts` - `cline.ts` - `vercel-ai-gateway.ts` - `fireworks.ts` - Added regression tests that stream a usage-only chunk (`choices: [{}]` with no `delta`) for: - `VercelAIGatewayHandler` - `OpenRouterHandler` - `ClineHandler` - `FireworksHandler` - Added a patch changeset. ### Test Procedure 1. Run targeted unit tests for impacted handlers: - `npm run test:unit -- --grep "VercelAIGatewayHandler|OpenRouterHandler|ClineHandler|FireworksHandler"` 2. Verify all targeted tests pass and include usage-only chunk coverage for each provider listed above. 3. Confirm no crash occurs when processing chunks that contain usage but no `delta`. ### Type of Change - [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality) - [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected) - [ ] ♻️ Refactor Changes - [ ] 💅 Cosmetic Changes - [ ] 📚 Documentation update - [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes ### Pre-flight Checklist - [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [x] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - [x] I have created a changeset using `npm run changeset` (required for user-facing changes) - [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Screenshots N/A (backend/provider streaming fix). ### Additional Notes This change specifically prevents the `in`-operator crash path on usage-only streamed chunks. ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Fix crash by guarding reasoning checks in provider handlers and add regression tests for usage-only chunks. > > - **Behavior**: > - Fix crash by adding `delta && ...` guards for reasoning checks in `openrouter.ts`, `cline.ts`, `vercel-ai-gateway.ts`, and `fireworks.ts`. > - Handles usage-only stream chunks without `choices[0].delta`. > - **Tests**: > - Add regression tests for usage-only chunks in `cline.test.ts`, `fireworks.test.ts`, `openrouter.test.ts`, and `vercel-ai-gateway.test.ts`. > - **Misc**: > - Add a patch changeset for the fix. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for 2dc64c39dccf720391b3c65fc8cf37e1e5f7ee9d. title: feat: preconnect websocket to reduce response latency

This introduces a responsesWsReadyPromise to track the connection state and prevent duplicate connection attempts while the initial connection is in flight. ### Test Procedure ### Type of Change - [ ] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [x] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality) - [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected) - [ ] ♻️ Refactor Changes - [ ] 💅 Cosmetic Changes - [ ] 📚 Documentation update - [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes ### Pre-flight Checklist - [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [x] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - [ ] I have created a changeset using `npm run changeset` (required for user-facing changes) - [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Screenshots ### Additional Notes ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Preconnects WebSocket in `OpenAiNativeHandler` to reduce response latency by managing connection state with a promise. > > - **Behavior**: > - Introduces `responsesWsReadyPromise` in `OpenAiNativeHandler` to manage WebSocket connection state. > - Adds `preconnectResponsesWebsocket()` to initiate WebSocket connection early, reducing handshake latency. > - Updates `ensureResponsesWebsocket()` to handle connection promise logic, preventing duplicate connections. > - **Functions**: > - Modifies `createResponseStream()` to call `preconnectResponsesWebsocket()` when using WebSocket mode. > - Updates `closeResponsesWebsocket()` to reset `responsesWsReadyPromise`. > - **Misc**: > - Adds error handling for WebSocket preconnection failures, logging errors and closing connections. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for 465d8191ec1bbbdd32bcf31ab349760790a70e18.
