reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This pull request introduces a WebSocket preconnection optimization to reduce latency when making OpenAI API calls in WebSocket mode. The change initiates the WebSocket connection early in the request flow, allowing the handshake to complete before the actual API call is made. **Changes:** - Added `responsesWsReadyPromise` field to track in-flight WebSocket connection attempts - Introduced `preconnectResponsesWebsocket()` method to initiate early connection - Modified `ensureResponsesWebsocket()` to return shared promise for concurrent callers - Updated cleanup logic in `closeResponsesWebsocket()` to clear the ready promise --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

commits.message:
feat: preconnect websocket to reduce response latency

Warm up the OpenAI WebSocket connection early in WebSocket mode to avoid handshake latency on the first response.create call. This introduces a responsesWsReadyPromise to track the connection state and prevent duplicate connection attempts while the initial connection is in flight.

commits.message:
fix: restrict OpenAI tool ID transformation to native provider

Update `convertToOpenAiMessages` and `transformToolCallId` to only apply tool ID transformations when the provider is explicitly set to `openai-native`. This prevents unintended ID modifications for other providers (like OpenRouter or local LLMs) that use the OpenAI format but may have different tool ID requirements or already provide compatible IDs.

title: Adding voice mode to Cline
body: ### Testing: Please use Staging to test this in the debugger ### Related Issue **Issue:** #6208 (self-referential, or create a separate issue if needed) ### Description This PR introduces a voice dictation feature to Cline, allowing users to transcribe speech to text directly in the chat input. The implementation follows Cline's established architecture patterns using protobuf-based RPC communication between the webview and extension host. **Key features added:** - Speech-to-text transcription using Cline's transcription service (requires Cline account) - Cross-platform audio recording using FFmpeg - Real-time recording status indicators with duration tracking - Maximum 5-minute recording limit with auto-stop - Comprehensive error handling with user-friendly messages **Technical approach:** - Follows Cline's protobuf development pattern with new `DictationService` RPC endpoints - Integrates with existing authentication and account services - Uses FFmpeg for cross-platform audio capture (macOS, Linux, Windows) - Implements proper state management through the existing StateManager system - Includes telemetry tracking for usage analytics ### Test Procedure **Manual testing performed:** 1. **Basic functionality:** - Tested recording start/stop/cancel operations - Verified transcription accuracy in English and multiple languages - Confirmed 5-minute auto-stop functionality works correctly 2. **Error handling:** - Tested without FFmpeg installed - proper error message displayed - Tested without Cline account authentication - appropriate auth prompt shown - Tested with insufficient credits - clear error message displayed - Tested network disconnection scenarios - connection errors handled gracefully 3. **Cross-platform testing:** - Verified FFmpeg detection and installation prompts on different platforms 4. **Integration testing:** - Confirmed settings persistence across sessions - Verified telemetry events are captured correctly **Areas that need testing by reviewers:** - Works in Both Personal Account and Org Account - Pricing shows up in the activity - Pricing shows up in the dashboard - You get charged nothing for cancelling a currently running run - Installs FFMpeg cleanly ### Type of Change - [ ] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [X] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality) - [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected) - [ ] ♻️ Refactor Changes - [ ] 💅 Cosmetic Changes - [ ] 📚 Documentation update - [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes ### Pre-flight Checklist - [X] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [ ] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - **Note: Linting issues need to be addressed** - [X] I have created a changeset using `npm run changeset` (required for user-facing changes) - [X] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Screenshots **Voice Recording UI States:** - Idle state: Microphone icon in chat input - Recording state: Red stop button with duration counter and cancel option - Processing state: Loading spinner during transcription - Error state: Error icon with tooltip showing error message **Settings Panel:** - New "Dictation" section in Feature Settings - Language dropdown with 50+ supported languages - Enable/disable toggle for the feature *(Screenshots would be added here showing the actual UI states)* ### Additional Notes **Dependencies:** - Requires FFmpeg for audio recording (installation prompts provided for each platform) - Requires active Cline account with transcription credits - Uses Cline's transcription service API (billed at $0.006 per minute) ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Adds voice dictation feature to Cline with audio recording, transcription services, and UI integration. > > - **Behavior**: > - Adds voice dictation feature to Cline, enabling speech-to-text transcription in chat input. > - Uses FFmpeg for cross-platform audio recording with a 5-minute limit. > - Implements real-time recording status and error handling. > - **Services**: > - Introduces `AudioRecordingService` for handling audio capture and `VoiceTranscriptionService` for transcription. > - Adds `transcribeAudio()` to `ClineAccountService` for transcription API calls. > - **UI Components**: > - Updates `ChatTextArea.tsx` to include `VoiceRecorder` component for recording control. > - Adds `VoiceRecorder.tsx` for managing recording and transcription states. > - Updates `FeatureSettingsSection.tsx` to include dictation settings. > - **Configuration**: > - Adds `DictationSettings` to manage feature flags and user preferences. > - Updates `audioProgramConstants.ts` for FFmpeg configuration across platforms. > - **Telemetry**: > - Extends `TelemetryService` to track dictation events like recording start/stop and transcription success/error. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for 9014acdb8df98523a17ed5752fdfef4f0b73d023. author: arafatkatze

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR adds support for fetching recommended and free Cline model cards from a backend API endpoint, replacing the previously hard-coded lists with dynamic data. The changes introduce a new RPC endpoint `refreshClineRecommendedModelsRpc` that fetches model metadata from the Cline API, with proper caching (1-hour TTL), disk fallback, and error handling. The frontend integrates this new endpoint into the OpenRouterModelPicker component, maintaining backward compatibility with static fallback arrays.

**Changes:**
- Add proto service definition and message types for Cline recommended models
- Implement backend model fetching with cache management and disk persistence
- Wire the API-backed model cards into the frontend model picker with fallback support
- Update model filtering logic to preserve explicitly allowed free models from the API

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 7 out of 7 changed files in this pull request and generated 5 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| proto/cline/models.proto | Adds RPC method `refreshClineRecommendedModelsRpc` and message types `ClineRecommendedModel` and `ClineRecommendedModelsResponse` |
| src/core/controller/models/refreshClineRecommendedModels.ts | Core logic for fetching recommended models from API with 1-hour cache, pending promise deduplication, and disk fallback |
| src/core/controller/models/refreshClineRecommendedModelsRpc.ts | RPC handler that wraps the core fetch logic and converts to protobuf types |
| src/core/storage/disk.ts | Adds `clineRecommendedModels` to GlobalFileNames and refactors getSavedClineMessages for better readability |
| src/shared/utils/model-filters.ts | Updates filterOpenRouterModelIds to accept allowedFreeModelIds parameter for preserving API-specified free models |
| webview-ui/src/components/settings/utils/providerUtils.ts | Updates filterOpenRouterModelIds (duplicate implementation)

<sub>7 files reviewed, 3 comments</sub>

<sub>[Edit Code Review Agent Settings](https://app.greptile.com/review/github) | [Greptile](https://greptile.com?utm_source=greptile_expert&utm_medium=github&utm_campaign=code_reviews)</sub>

Running this through `gpt-5.3-codex` to get a "deep code review" for the most important details to address for production readiness, here's what I'm seeing:

```
Highest-priority code review feedback:

1. __Retry behavior bug in UI fetch gate (High):__ `hasFetchedClineRecommendedModelsRef` is set __before__ the RPC succeeds. If first fetch fails (network hiccup), the session never retries and stays on fallback cards. Set the “fetched” flag only after success, or track `hasAttempted` + backoff/retry trigger.
2. __Model ID canonicalization gap (High):__ filtering/zero-pricing relies on exact `allowedFreeModelIds` matches, while OpenRouter IDs may vary by suffix/alias (`:free`, provider normalization). `FREE_CLINE_MODEL_ALIASES` exists but is empty, suggesting known mismatch risk. Add canonical ID normalization + tested alias mapping to prevent hidden/free-model regressions.
3. __Duplicate filtering logic drift risk (High-Med):__ `filterOpenRouterModelIds` now exists in both `src/shared/utils/model-filters.ts` and `webview-ui/.../providerUtils.ts`. This is a future divergence trap. Consolidate to one shared implementation and import it in webview.
4. __Cache file write robustness (Medium):__ `refreshClineRecommendedModels.ts` uses plain `fs.writeFile`; elsewhere you already use `atomicWriteFile` patterns for corruption safety. Prefer atomic write for the cache JSON too.
5. __Response normalization strictness (Medium):__ normalization currently rejects payloads unless both `recommended` and `free` are arrays. For safer server rollout compatibility, tolerate missing fields with defaults (`[]`) rather than failing the whole refresh.
6. __Observability and failure semantics (Medium):__ on fetch + cache miss you return empty arrays (good for UX fallback), but this is indistinguishable from “legitimately empty” data. Consider returning metadata (`source: api|cache|empty`, `error?:...`) or at least structured logs/telemetry so production incidents are diagnosable.
7. __Missin
Added a few comments while I keep reviewing

Implements a new OpenAI Responses API “websocket mode” path (gated behind a feature flag + new `ApiFormat`), adds message timestamps to support safe `previous_response_id` chaining, and expands telemetry to record task completion payloads including TTFT/duration.

fix: openai native provider token usage mapping

- Add `store` parameter support to OpenAI native provider options to allow persisting completions.
- Fix incorrect mapping of `cached_tokens` and `reasoning_tokens` in usage statistics.
- Include `thoughtsTokenCount` in the final usage report to track reasoning model performance and costs.

feat: add websocket support for OpenAI Responses API

This commit introduces WebSocket support for the OpenAI Native provider's Responses API, providing an alternative to the standard HTTP streaming.

- Implement `createResponseStreamWebsocket` in `OpenAiNativeHandler` with a fallback to HTTP on failure.
- Refactor `OpenAiNativeHandler` to modularize tool mapping and parameter construction for the Responses API.
- Update `OcaHandler` to explicitly disable `previousResponseId` when using the Responses API and add validation for model information.

disablePreviousResponseId

feat: add timestamp to conversation messages for response chaining

Add `ts` field to `ClineStorageMessage` to track when messages were
created. Use this timestamp to enforce a 23-hour validity window when
chaining OpenAI responses via `previousResponseId`, since the API only
retains responses for 24 hours. Also fix non-null assertion operators
in tests to use optional chaining for safer access.

add OpenAI Responses Websocket Mode ApiFormat support

- Add `OPENAI_RESPONSES_WEBSOCKET_MODE` to the `ApiFormat` enum in proto definitions.
- Update `OpenAiNativeHandler` to use the new API format for determining when to use websocket mode, replacing previous environment-based logic.
- Refactor tool mapping for OpenAI Responses to support strict mode and correctly handle null parameters.
- Ensure the `store` option is disabled when `previous_response_id` is present in websocket mode.
- Bump version to 2.4.1 and update package dependencies.

title: cli yolo mode should not persist yolo setting to disk ever
body: - added a method to StateManager, setSessionOverride, which overrides state settings while the statemanager lives in memory ### Related Issue **Issue:** CLINE-1357 ### Description Cline CLI was persisting yolo setting to disk, causing cline to auto-switch to act mode. This PR adds a method to StateManager, setSessionOverride, which overrides state settings while the statemanager lives in memory. This allows us to override yolo setting for the current session without ever persisting it to disk. ### Test Procedure 1. Set yolo mode to false in cline config 2. run `cline -y "run command"` and verify that yolo mode is enabled for that command without changing the config file 3. run `cline config` and verify that yolo mode is still set to false in the config file ### Type of Change - [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality) - [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected) - [ ] ♻️ Refactor Changes - [ ] 💅 Cosmetic Changes - [ ] 📚 Documentation update - [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes ### Pre-flight Checklist - [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [x] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - [ ] I have created a changeset using `npm run changeset` (required for user-facing changes) - [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Screenshots ### Additional Notes ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Adds `setSessionOverride` to `StateManager` to ensure CLI `yolo` mode is session-only and not persisted to disk. > > - **Behavior**: > - Adds `setSessionOverride` method to `StateManager` to handle session-specific settings without persisting to disk. > - Modifies `applyTaskOptions` in `index.ts` to use `setSessionOverride` for `yoloModeToggled`, ensuring `yolo` mode is session-only. > - **State Management**: > - Introduces `sessionOverrideCache` in `StateManager` to store session-specific settings. > - Updates `getGlobalSettingsKey` in `StateManager` to prioritize `sessionOverrideCache` over other caches. > - **Misc**: > - Removes `restoreYoloState` function and related logic from `index.ts`. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for c39067cc2f3befd47390f45904a2fbd0daaf187c. title: fix(ollama): abort streaming requests when task is cancelled
body: ### Related Issue **Issue:** #7468 ### Description When a user clicks cancel during an Ollama streaming request, Cline breaks out of the stream loop but never tells the Ollama client to actually abort the HTTP request. The Ollama server continues generating in the background, keeping the GPU busy and blocking subsequent requests until completion. This PR calls the Ollama SDK's built-in `abort()` method when the user cancels a task, which uses an AbortController internally to close the HTTP connection immediately. **Changes:** - Add optional `abort?(): void` method to `ApiHandler` interface - Implement `abort()` in `OllamaHandler` that calls `this.client?.abort()` - Call `this.api.abort?.()` in Task when aborting the stream ### Test Procedure 1. Start a chat with Ollama provider 2. Let the model begin generating a response 3. Click the cancel button 4. Observe GPU usage drops immediately (vs continuing to generate in the background) 5. Start a new request - it should proceed immediately without waiting for the previous generation to complete Verified that non-Ollama providers are unaffected since the `abort()` method is optional. ### Type of Change - [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality) - [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected) - [ ] ♻️ Refactor Changes - [ ] 💅 Cosmetic Changes - [ ] 📚 Documentation update - [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes ### Pre-flight Checklist - [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [x] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - [x] I have created a changeset using `npm run changeset` (required for user-facing changes) - [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Screenshots N/A - Backend change. The user-visible effect is that GPU usage stops immediately when cancelling an Ollama request. ### Additional Notes Note: The Ollama server itself has a known limitation where it may continue processing briefly even after the client aborts ([ollama/ollama#2876](https://github.com/ollama/ollama/issues/2876)). However, calling `abort()` immediately closes the HTTP connection, which frees up the client for new requests and reduces wasted GPU cycles. ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Adds `abort()` method to `OllamaHandler` and calls it on task cancellation to immediately stop HTTP requests. > > - **Behavior**: > - Calls `abort()` on Ollama client when task is cancelled to stop HTTP request immediately. > - Ensures GPU resources are freed up immediately after cancellation. > - **Interfaces**: > - Adds optional `abort?(): void` method to `ApiHandler` interface. > - **Implementations**: > - Implements `abort()` in `OllamaHandler` to call `this.client?.abort()`. > - Calls `this.api.abort?.()` in `Task` class when aborting the stream. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for aeb0209f2768da5b4c56f10a71a473f4e61b9e37. You can [customize](https://app.ellipsis.dev/cline/settings/summaries) this summary. It will automatically update as commits are pushed.
author: robinnewhouse

commits.message:
fix(ollama): abort streaming requests when task is cancelled

Previously, clicking cancel would break out of the stream loop but
leave the HTTP connection open. Ollama would continue generating in
the background, keeping the GPU busy and blocking subsequent requests
until completion.

Now we call the Ollama SDK's abort() method to immediately close the
connection.

Fixes #7468

title: fix(release-eng): Pin VSCode nightly build to node version 22
body: ### Related Issue **Issue:** n/a ### Description #### What was broken Nightly extension publishing started failing during packaging (`vsce package`). Two separate issues were involved: 1. __Original breakage:__ when nightly renames the package (`claude-dev` → `cline-nightly`), npm’s workspace self-link still pointed to the old name, causing dependency-check failure. 2. __After that fix merged:__ nightly still failed because the workflow used `node-version: lts/*`, which resolved to Node 24/npm 11 and introduced a different `npm list` failure (`ELSPROBLEMS`) not seen in the Node 22 test environment. #### What this PR changes 1. __Nightly packaging script hardening (`scripts/publish-nightly.mjs`)__ - Reconciles workspace self-link during nightly rename. - Restores original state after packaging. - Adds safety validation so only expected symlinks are modified. 2. __Nightly workflow stabilization (`.github/workflows/publish-nightly.yml`)__ - Changes Node setup from `lts/*` to `22` to match the rest of CI/test tooling and avoid npm behavior drift. #### Why this is necessary Without this, nightly publish can fail even when product code is healthy: - first due to temporary package-name/link mismatch, - then due to inconsistent Node/npm behavior in the release job. This PR makes nightly publishing deterministic and aligned with the already-passing CI environment. #### Why we’re confident this works - We verified the script path locally end-to-end with dry run packaging. - CI logs confirm the original missing/extraneous self-link error disappeared after script fix. - Remaining CI failure was isolated to Node 24/npm 11 behavior in nightly job; pinning to Node 22 removes that drift and matches successful test workflows. ### Test Procedure Merge this change and run the GitHub Action to observe the behavior. ### Type of Change - [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality) - [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected) - [ ] ♻️ Refactor Changes - [ ] 💅 Cosmetic Changes - [ ] 📚 Documentation update - [x] 🏃 Workflow Changes ### Pre-flight Checklist - [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [x] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - [ ] I have created a changeset using `npm run changeset` (required for user-facing changes) - [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Screenshots n/a ### Additional Notes n/a ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Pins Node version to 22 in nightly workflow and hardens packaging script to fix build issues. > > - **Nightly Workflow Stabilization**: > - Changes Node setup in `.github/workflows/publish-nightly.yml` from `lts/*` to `22` to prevent `npm list` failures with Node 24/npm 11. > - **Packaging Script Hardening**: > - Updates `scripts/publish-nightly.mjs` to reconcile workspace self-link during package rename and restore original state post-packaging. > - Adds validation to ensure only expected symlinks are modified. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for 768d625c41f148237d1ab9176ac1e8de96f31635. title: fix(release-eng): Fix nightly extension publish failure caused by workspace self-link mismatch
body: ### Related Issue **Issue:** n/a ### Description Our nightly VS Code extension publish started failing during packaging. The root cause was that we temporarily rename the extension package from `claude-dev` to `cline-nightly`, but npm workspace linking still pointed at the old name. That mismatch made `vsce` fail its dependency check and blocked the nightly release pipeline. This PR fixes that by aligning the workspace self-link during nightly packaging, then restoring it afterward. In short: we make the temporary nightly rename and npm’s workspace link agree with each other while packaging runs. #### Why this broke A recent workspace change (`3a14a88f4`, adding `"."` to root workspaces) introduced a root self-link in `node_modules` for the package name. Nightly packaging changes the package name, so dependency detection started seeing inconsistent state and failing. #### What this PR changes In `scripts/publish-nightly.mjs`: - Reconcile workspace self-link before `vsce package` (rename existing self-link or create temporary nightly one if missing). - Restore/remove that link in cleanup (`finally`, `exit`, `SIGINT`, `SIGTERM`). - Add safety checks so filesystem operations only proceed if links are expected symlinks pointing to the repo root. - Fail closed with clear errors if unexpected paths are detected. ### Test Procedure Nightly packaging dry-run now succeeds end-to-end and restores state correctly. __Test command used:__ ```bash npm run publish:marketplace:nightly -- --dry-run ``` Observed outcome: - `vsce` packages successfully (`dist/cline-nightly.vsix` created) - no dependency-mismatch failure - workspace link restored (`cline-nightly -> claude-dev`) - `package.json` restored at end of run ### Type of Change - [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality) - [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected) - [ ] ♻️ Refactor Changes - [ ] 💅 Cosmetic Changes - [ ] 📚 Documentation update - [x] 🏃 Workflow Changes ### Pre-flight Checklist - [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [x] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - [ ] I have created a changeset using `npm run changeset` (required for user-facing changes) - [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Screenshots n/a ### Additional Notes n/a ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Fixes nightly publish failure by aligning workspace self-link with temporary package name in `publish-nightly.mjs`. > > - **Behavior**: > - Fixes nightly publish failure by aligning workspace self-link with temporary package name in `publish-nightly.mjs`. > - Restores original workspace link after packaging. > - Adds safety checks for symlink operations. > - **Functions**: > - Adds `reconcileWorkspaceSelfLinkForNightly()` and `restoreWorkspaceSelfLink()` to manage workspace link consistency. > - **Misc**: > - Handles cleanup on process exit, `SIGINT`, and `SIGTERM` to ensure state restoration. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for cc8e13ee0fc1f59b0cad67d889d59d6afb9354b0. title: fix: flaky Cancel behavior by preventing duplicate cancel actions (CLINE-1380)
body: ### Related Issue **Issue:** CLINE-1380 part of https://github.com/cline/cline/issues/9383 ### Description This PR fixes chat cancel behavior where users sometimes had to click Cancel multiple times, and repeated clicks could accidentally transition into Resume/restart behavior. - Updated action button click handling to avoid immediate re-enabling on cancel and keep a single in-flight action until state updates. - Added a handler-level cancel lock (cancelInFlightRef) so duplicate cancel requests are ignored while the first cancel is still processing. - Routed Escape key cancel through the same guarded action path as button clicks. - Hardened cancel execution flow: - Attempt cancelBackgroundCommand when applicable. - Always call cancelTask afterward to ensure task cancellation is applied even if background-command state is stale. - Keep background cancel errors non-fatal and continue with task cancel. ### Test Procedure 1. buttonConfig.test.ts passed (21/21) 2. Manual testing: Cancel an on-going task and makes sure it only requires a single click ### Type of Change - [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality) - [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected) - [ ] ♻️ Refactor Changes - [ ] 💅 Cosmetic Changes - [ ] 📚 Documentation update - [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes ### Pre-flight Checklist - [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [x] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - [ ] I have created a changeset using `npm run changeset` (required for user-facing changes) - [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Screenshots ### Additional Notes ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Fixes duplicate cancel actions in chat by introducing a lock mechanism in `useMessageHandlers.ts` and updating `ActionButtons.tsx` to ensure single processing of cancel actions. > > - **Behavior**: > - Prevents duplicate cancel actions by introducing `cancelInFlightRef` in `useMessageHandlers.ts` to lock cancel requests while one is processing. > - Updates `handleActionClick` in `ActionButtons.tsx` to ensure only one cancel action is processed at a time. > - Routes Escape key cancel through the same guarded path as button clicks in `ActionButtons.tsx`. > - **Error Handling**: > - Keeps background cancel errors non-fatal and continues with task cancel in `useMessageHandlers.ts`. > - **Misc**: > - Minor refactoring in `ActionButtons.tsx` to streamline action handling logic. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for b90f706797b41ec927bda75f0caf81cc9c3d2727. You can [customize](https://app.ellipsis.dev/cline/settings/summaries) this summary. It will automatically update as commits are pushed.

title: refactor: replace non-null assertions with checks in PatchParser
body: ### Related Issue **Issue:** #XXXX ### Description Addresses some forbidden non-null assertions usages alerted by our linter. Replace all forbidden non-null assertions (`!`) in PatchParser.ts with safe alternatives using optional chaining (`?.`) and nullish coalescing (`?? ""`/`?? 0`). Refactors `PatchParser` to remove forbidden non-null assertions by using optional chaining / nullish coalescing and updates the Levenshtein implementation to use a flat distance matrix; also expands `PatchParser` test coverage for new error/edge cases.

**Changes:**

- Refactor Levenshtein distance matrix from 2D to flat array to avoid index-based assertions.
- Add/adjust tests for duplicate operations, invalid add lines, empty add content, warnings cleanup, and several edge cases.

**src/core/task/tools/utils/PatchParser.ts:125**
* `parseUpdateFile` can enter an infinite loop if the patch ends unexpectedly (i.e., `this.index` reaches `this.lines.length`) because the `while` condition uses optional chaining and doesn’t include a `hasMoreLines()` guard. When `currentLine` is `undefined` and `index === 0`, the loop body doesn’t advance `this.index` or throw, and `peek(...)` returns `endPatchIndex` unchanged.

Add an explicit `this.hasMoreLines()` condition to the `while` loop and/or throw a `DiffError` for unexpected EOF so malformed patches fail fast instead of hanging.

title: Add support for DB backed Welcome Banner
body: ### Related Issue closes https://linear.app/cline-bot/issue/PF-453/integrate-dynamic-welcome-banners-into-the-banner-service-in-the ### Description This PR adds support for DB backed Welcome Banner. This is behind a feature flag: remote-welcome-banners. The feature flag can toggle between showing the DB backed welcome banners vs the previously hardcoded welcome banners. The rationale for this is that, we can release this change, with the feature flag off; then in the next version where we need to show new welcome banners, we can simply add the banners via command center, and update the hardcoded banners to the latest ones, and toggle feature flag to hide the hard coded banners and show remote banners instead. If there are any issues, simply toggle back to hardcoded ones. We only need to do this for the next release to ensure everything is working properly with backup. When the flag is off: when flag is enabled, we show DB backed Banner and hide the hardcoded ones: When clicking on an action of the banner, the banner will close. https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4e088a76-777f-4921-b51f-6da1882db42e ### Test Procedure ### Type of Change - reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR fixes a CI failure by configuring the npm workspace to include the root package, enabling changesets to properly version both the `claude-dev` VSCode extension and the `cline` CLI package. The repository uses a monorepo structure where the root contains the VSCode extension (`claude-dev` package) and a `cli/` subdirectory contains the CLI tool (`cline` package). Without the root workspace entry, changesets could not recognize and version the `claude-dev` package, causing CI failures in the changeset version bump workflow. **Changes:** - Added "." to the workspaces array in package.json to include the root package as a workspace - Updated package-lock.json to reflect the new workspace structure with a proper link entry for `claude-dev` ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 1 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | package.json | Added "." to workspaces array to include root package in workspace configuration | | package-lock.json | Added node_modules/claude-dev link entry to reflect the root workspace in the lockfile metadata | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. title: fix(models): keep Sonnet 4.5 as default for now
body: ### Related Issue Issue: #9377 ### Description This PR rolls back the default model promotion from Claude Sonnet 4.6 to Claude Sonnet 4.5, while keeping Sonnet 4.6 fully supported and still visible in model selection surfaces. What changed in behavior: - Provider defaults now point back to Sonnet 4.5 for Anthropic, Claude Code, Bedrock, and OpenRouter. - Claude Code shorthand aliases (`sonnet`, `sonnet[1m]`) now map to Sonnet 4.5 again. - The fallback recommendation message shown after repeated mistakes points back to Claude 4.5 Sonnet. - Onboarding and recommended model surfaces are rebalanced so Sonnet 4.5 is the default-style choice, with Sonnet 4.6 still present as a newer option. Reasoning and scope: - Recent work intentionally moved defaults to 4.6 and removed free-promo positioning. - Product direction changed again and we now want to keep 4.5 as the default for stability. - I kept this PR focused on default and recommendation semantics only, without removing 4.6 model support or 4.6 marketing/banner visibility. Non-obvious details: - OpenRouter default model info text was also reverted to 4.5-specific copy so the fallback default metadata matches the actual default model ID. - The onboarding frontier list now includes both 4.5 and 4.6, with 4.5 marked as the best/default-like entry and 4.6 retained as new. - A changeset is included since this is user-facing behavior. ### Test Procedure 1. Static checks - `npx biome check --diagnostic-level=error cli/src/constants/featured-models.ts src/core/task/index.ts src/shared/api.ts src/shared/cline/onboarding.ts webview-ui/src/components/chat/chat-view/components/layout/WelcomeSection.tsx webview-ui/src/components/onboarding/data-steps.ts webview-ui/src/components/settings/OpenRouterModelPicker.tsx` 2. Unit tests - `npm run -s test:unit -- src/core/api/providers/__tests__/claude-code.test.ts` - Result: `1014 passing, 4 pending` 3. Manual diff verification - Confirmed defaults reverted in `src/shared/api.ts` for Anthropic, Claude Code, Bedrock, and OpenRouter. - Confirmed onboarding/recommended UI surfaces now prioritize Sonnet 4.5 while retaining Sonnet 4.6 support. ### Type of Change - [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality) - [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected) - [ ] ♻️ Refactor Changes - [ ] 💅 Cosmetic Changes - [ ] 📚 Documentation update - [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes ### Pre-flight Checklist - [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [ ] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - [x] I have created a changeset using `npm run changeset` (required for user-facing changes) - [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Screenshots No visual screenshots included. Changes are model defaults and recommendation/onboarding content updates. ### Additional Notes This PR intentionally does not alter Sonnet 4.6 support or general availability. It only reverts default-selection behavior and associated recommendation framing back to Sonnet 4.5. ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Reverts default model from Claude Sonnet 4.6 to Claude Sonnet 4.5 across various components, maintaining Sonnet 4.6 availability. > > - **Behavior**: > - Reverts default model from `Claude Sonnet 4.6` to `Claude Sonnet 4.5` in `src/shared/api.ts` for Anthropic, Claude Code, Bedrock, and OpenRouter. > - Updates `WelcomeSection.tsx` to set `Claude Sonnet 4.5` as the default model in banner actions. > - Adjusts onboarding models in `onboarding.ts` to prioritize `Claude Sonnet 4.5`. > - **UI Components**: > - Updates `OpenRouterModelPicker.tsx` to feature `Claude Sonnet 4.5` as the recommended model. > - Modifies `data-steps.ts` to reflect `Claude Sonnet 4.5` in user type selections. > - **Misc**: > - Adds changeset `fair-drinks-tickle.md` to document the reversion of the default model. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for b48eacd6ca22f258f29346d511b19162eb3e7a03. title: fix(models): reinstate minimax m2.5 free promo surfaces
body: ### Related Issue Issue: N/A Follow-up context: #9361 and #9377 ### Description This PR restores MiniMax M2.5 free-promo surfaces after we removed them in #9361. After that removal, we confirmed MiniMax M2.5 is still intended to be free. This PR brings it back in the same high-visibility places users rely on for discovery and free-model behavior, and also restores it in onboarding free selection. What was restored 1. Free pricing behavior in Cline provider - `src/core/api/providers/cline.ts` - Added `minimax/minimax-m2.5` back to `CLINE_FREE_MODELS` so usage cost is overridden to zero for this model. 2. Featured model surfaces - `webview-ui/src/components/settings/OpenRouterModelPicker.tsx` - Added MiniMax M2.5 back to `recommendedModels`. - Added MiniMax M2.5 back to `freeModels`. - `cli/src/constants/featured-models.ts` - Added MiniMax M2.5 back to free featured models. 3. Banner promo surface - `src/shared/cline/banner.ts` - Added a MiniMax M2.5 free promo banner with CTA routing to the `free` tab. 4. What’s New modal promo surface - `webview-ui/src/components/common/WhatsNewModal.tsx` - Added MiniMax M2.5 free promo line item and link to the free tab. 5. Onboarding free models - `src/shared/cline/onboarding.ts` - Added MiniMax M2.5 into the free onboarding group (similar class of surfacing as other free models). How I identified scope - Used commit history and blame on the removal commit: - `36c68a6ab` removed the original MiniMax promo surfaces. - Cross-checked earlier commits where MiniMax was introduced/promoted: - `7896e6d89`, `955ae2f62`, and `ecde79cf0`. - Restored only the MiniMax-specific pieces while preserving the current Sonnet 4.6 default/not-free direction from #9377. Non-obvious decision - I did not revert Sonnet-related free behavior while restoring MiniMax. - This keeps current product intent intact: Sonnet 4.6 remains non-free/default as merged in #9377, while MiniMax M2.5 is restored as free. ### Test Procedure 1. title: feat(models): make sonnet 4.6 default and remove free promo positioning
body: ### Related Issue Issue: N/A Follow-up context: #9356 ### Description This PR is a follow-up to the Sonnet 4.6 promo rollout. The previous change intentionally combined two things at once: - temporary free promo positioning for Sonnet 4.6 - keeping 4.5-era defaults in place for risk control during the promo window Now that we are past the promo decision point, this PR shifts to the steady-state behavior we want: - Sonnet 4.6 is the default model across providers - Sonnet 4.6 is no longer treated as free in pricing or free model surfaces - user-facing messaging now frames Sonnet 4.6 as the latest model, not a limited free offer Technical approach 1) Default model wiring - Updated provider defaults in `src/shared/api.ts`: - `anthropicDefaultModelId` to `claude-sonnet-4-6` - `claudeCodeDefaultModelId` to `claude-sonnet-4-6` - `bedrockDefaultModelId` to `anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-6` - `openRouterDefaultModelId` to `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6` - Updated Claude Code alias behavior so `sonnet` and `sonnet[1m]` resolve to Sonnet 4.6 variants. - Updated the OpenRouter default model description copy from 4.5 language to 4.6 language. 2) Remove free pricing behavior for Sonnet 4.6 - In `src/core/api/providers/cline.ts`, removed Sonnet 4.6 IDs from `CLINE_FREE_MODELS` so usage cost is no longer overridden to 0 for Sonnet 4.6. 3) Remove free tab/listing placement for Sonnet 4.6 - In `webview-ui/src/components/settings/OpenRouterModelPicker.tsx`, removed Sonnet 4.6 from `freeModels` and removed alias-based Sonnet free override. - In `cli/src/constants/featured-models.ts`, removed Sonnet 4.6 from free featured models and promoted it to first recommended slot. - In `src/shared/cline/onboarding.ts`, removed the Sonnet 4.6 free onboarding entry while keeping it in frontier. 4) Update promo copy to latest-model copy - `src/shared/cline/banner.ts`: changed from free-limited messaging and free-tab CTA to latest-model messaging with recommended-tab CTA. - `webview-ui/src/components/common/WhatsNewModal.tsx`: removed 24-hour free wording and pointed CTA to recommended tab. - `webview-ui/src/components/chat/chat-view/components/layout/WelcomeSection.tsx`: fallback set-model target now 4.6. - `src/core/task/index.ts`: recommendation text now references Claude 4.6 Sonnet. Debugging and gotchas - While patching `src/shared/api.ts`, I briefly introduced a duplicate `openRouterDefaultModelId` line. I caught it immediately with a targeted grep and removed the duplicate. - Biome flagged a formatting issue in `WhatsNewModal.tsx` after the copy update. I ran formatter on that file and re-ran checks. - The targeted unit test command still triggers the broader unit suite in this repo. I kept the same command and verified full pass output. Alternatives considered - Keeping Claude Code `sonnet` alias on 4.5 while changing only explicit defaults: - Rejected because alias/default mismatch can confuse behavior and onboarding expectations. - Keeping Sonnet 4.6 in both recommended and free tabs: - Rejected because this PR intentionally ends free positioning and free pricing for Sonnet 4.6. ### Test Procedure Validation steps run locally: 1) Static checks - `git diff --name-only | rg '\\.(ts|tsx)$' | xargs npx biome check --diagnostic-level=error --no-errors-on-unmatched` - Result: pass 2) Unit tests - `npm run -s test:unit -- src/core/api/providers/__tests__/bedrock.test.ts src/core/api/providers/__tests__/claude-code.test.ts` - In this repo this command runs the broader unit suite - Result: `1014 passing, 4 pending` 3) Spot checks of behavior-critical strings and mappings - Verified no Sonnet 4.6 free wording remains in banner or whats-new surfaces - Verified default IDs now point to Sonnet 4.6 in Anthropic, Claude Code, Bedrock, and OpenRouter - Verified Sonnet 4.6 removed from free featured lists and free pricing override paths ### Type of Change - [ ] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [x] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality) - [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected) - [ ] ♻️ Refactor Changes - [x] 💅 Cosmetic Changes - [ ] 📚 Documentation update - [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes ### Pre-flight Checklist - [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [ ] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - [x] I have created a changeset using `npm run changeset` (required for user-facing changes) - [ ] I have reviewed contributor guidelines ### Screenshots No UI screenshots included for this PR. ### Additional Notes Changeset included: - `.changeset/blue-maps-fix.md` ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Set Sonnet 4.6 as the default model and remove its free promotional status across various components. > > - **Behavior**: > - Set `claude-sonnet-4-6` as default model in `src/shared/api.ts` for Anthropic, Claude Code, Bedrock, and OpenRouter. > - Remove Sonnet 4.6 from `CLINE_FREE_MODELS` in `src/core/api/providers/cline.ts`. > - Update `OpenRouterModelPicker.tsx` to remove Sonnet 4.6 from free models. > - **UI Changes**: > - Update `BannerCardData` in `src/shared/cline/banner.ts` to reflect Sonnet 4.6 as the latest model, not free. > - Modify `WhatsNewModal.tsx` to remove free offer wording for Sonnet 4.6. > - Adjust `WelcomeSection.tsx` to set Sonnet 4.6 as default model. > - **Misc**: > - Update `featured-models.ts` to promote Sonnet 4.6 to recommended slot. > - Remove Sonnet 4.6 from free onboarding in `onboarding.ts`. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for c2887d01edeff6b82921a5b42ef76d63b7748b05. You can [customize](https://app.ellipsis.dev/cline/settings/summaries) this summary. It will automatically update as commits are pushed.

## Pull request overview

This PR transitions Sonnet 4.6 from a temporary “free promo” position to the steady-state default across providers, and updates UI/onboarding/banner copy and model lists to reflect Sonnet 4.6 as the standard recommended model rather than a free offer.

**Changes:**
- Update default model IDs/aliases across Anthropic, Claude Code, Bedrock, and OpenRouter to Sonnet 4.6.
- Remove Sonnet 4.6 from “free” pricing overrides and free model surfacing (model pickers, featured lists, onboarding).
- Refresh user-facing messaging (banner, what’s new, welcome/recommendation text) to “latest model” framing and recommended-tab CTAs.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 10 out of 10 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

<details>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| webview-ui/src/components/settings/OpenRouterModelPicker.tsx | Removes Sonnet 4.6 from free tab and adjusts how Cline “free model” pricing override IDs are derived. |
| webview-ui/src/components/common/WhatsNewModal.tsx | Updates CTA tab and removes free-window language for Sonnet 4.6. |
| webview-ui/src/components/chat/chat-view/components/layout/WelcomeSection.tsx | Updates fallback SetModel target to Sonnet 4.6. |
| src/shared/cline/onboarding.ts | Removes Sonnet 4.6 from onboarding “free” group while keeping it in frontier. |
| src/shared/cline/banner.ts | Updates banner copy/CTA from “free limited” to “latest model” and points to recommended tab. |
| src/shared/api.ts | Switches provider defaults and Claude Code aliases to Sonnet 4.6; updates OpenRouter default description. |
| src/core/task/index.ts | Updates recommendation text to reference Claude 4.6 Sonnet. |
| src/core/api/providers/cline.ts | Removes Sonnet 4.6 from Cline free-cost override list. |
| cli/src/constants/featured-models.ts | Removes Sonnet 4.6 from free featured list; promotes it in recommended list. |
| .changeset/blue-maps-fix.

title: Alex/inf 413 bug no telemetry for versions greater than 20
body: ### Description Fixes CLI telemetry builds missing OTEL exporter settings by ensuring reusable publish workflows inherit secrets. This allows `npm-main` / `npm-nightly` to pass `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_*` into the build so `cli/esbuild.mts` embeds the OTLP endpoint. (Temporary verification job can be removed after confirming secrets are set.) ### Test Procedure - Triggered **Publish CLI (Trusted)** [nightly workflow on a branch](https://github.com/cline/cline/actions/runs/22122059053/job/63944131184) with only workflow changes. - Verified `publish-nightly / Verify OTEL secrets are inherited` logs show all three vars as `set`. - No CLI publishing was required to validate the fix. ### Type of Change - [x] 🏃 Workflow Changes - [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) ### Pre-flight Checklist - [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [ ] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - [ ] I have created a changeset using `npm run changeset` (required for user-facing changes) - [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Screenshots ### Additional Notes - I removed the `verify secrets` step from the workflow, but could be worth adding as a check ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Fixes missing OTEL exporter settings in CLI builds by inheriting secrets in `publish-cli-trusted.yaml`. > > - **Workflow Changes**: > - In `publish-cli-trusted.yaml`, added `secrets: inherit` to `publish-main` and `publish-nightly` jobs to ensure OTEL exporter settings are passed. > - **Verification**: > - Verified that `publish-nightly` logs show OTEL secrets as `set`. > - Temporary verification job added for confirmation, to be removed later. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for 57dfb543409b3796c85708fe48a0615fbf3b3e4a. title: fix: disable click-to-set auto-condense threshold and hardcode default
body:
### Related Issue

**Issues:** #5616, #5842, #8236

### Description

The context window progress bar has a click handler that sets `autoCondenseThreshold` based on where you click — intended as a future feature to let users control when auto-compaction fires. However, this handler was wired up to the `<Progress>` component before the feature was ready for release: there's no visual affordance (the `AutoCondenseMarker` only renders when `useAutoCondense` is enabled), no confirmation dialog, and no undo. The result is that any accidental click on the progress bar silently persists a threshold value into VS Code `globalState`, which survives across sessions and extension updates.

With a threshold of e.g. 0.05 (from clicking near the left edge), compaction fires at `floor(200000 * 0.05) = 10,000` tokens — after just one exchange — instead of the intended `floor(200000 * 0.75) = 150,000` tokens. This causes ~20 context resets across a 64-exchange task, burning tokens on summarization and degrading task quality.

**Changes:**
- **Disable click and keyboard handlers** on the context window progress bar that set `autoCondenseThreshold`. The handlers and components are commented out with TODO notes for future release when the feature has proper UX (confirmation, visual feedback, undo) and 1m context models are more reliable.
- **Hardcode threshold to 0.75 default** in the task loop, ignoring the stored `globalState` value. This heals existing users whose threshold was corrupted by accidental clicks, since `globalState` is not reset on extension update.
- **Add unit tests** for `shouldCompactContextWindow` covering the threshold math, the bug case, edge cases (undefined/zero fallback, cache tokens, maxAllowedSize cap).

### Test Procedure

- Added instrumentation to `shouldCompactContextWindow()` and the auto-condense decision point in `task/index.ts`
- **Before fix:** logs showed `thresholdPercentage: 0.06`, `roundedThreshold: 12000`, `result: true` at 23K tokens
- **After disabling click handler:** confirmed no new threshold changes occur on progress bar clicks
- **After hardcoding 0.75:** logs showed `thresholdPercentage: 0.75`, `roundedThreshold: 150000`, `result: false` at 33K tokens
- Verified `AutoCondenseMarker` and progress bar still render normally
- All 8 new unit tests pass

### Type of Change

-   [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)

### Pre-flight Checklist

-   [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs)
-   [x] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`)
-   [x] I have created a changeset using `npm run changeset` (required for user-facing changes)
-   [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Screenshots

N/A — no visual changes. The progress bar and auto-condense marker render identically; only the click interaction is removed.

### Additional Notes

The click-to-set-threshold feature was implemented but prematurely wired into the live UI before the feature was ready for release. All components (`AutoCondenseMarker`, `ContextWindowSummary`, keyboard/click handlers) are preserved as commented-out code with TODO comments for future re-enablement when 1m context models are more reliable and the interaction has proper confirmation/undo affordances.

fix: disable click-to-set auto-condense threshold and hardcode default

Clicking anywhere on the context window progress bar silently set
autoCondenseThreshold to a value based on click position (e.g. 0.05),
persisting in globalState. This caused compaction to fire at ~10K tokens
instead of the intended ~150K, resulting in ~20 context resets per task.

- Comment out click and keyboard handlers on progress bar (keep components
  for future release with proper UX)
- Hardcode threshold to 0.75 default, ignoring corrupted stored values

title: fix: smarter retry for write_to_file missing content parameter
body:
## Problem

When the models output is truncated (due to output token limits or high context usage), `write_to_file` tool calls arrive with an empty `content` parameter. The current error handler returns a generic "Missing value for required parameter content. Please retry with complete response." — which the model interprets literally and retries identically, creating an **infinite retry loop** that wastes tokens and blocks progress.

This is likely caused by an upstream issue where providers (e.g., Vercel-proxied endpoints) drop incomplete content on mid-stream context window errors instead of returning partial results.

This is a widely reported issue affecting 12+ users across multiple providers (Claude, Gemini, DeepSeek) and environments (VS Code, JetBrains).

**Related issues:** #7998, #7863, #8051, #8739, #7965

## Solution

Scope kept to **runtime error-path behavior** only (no global prompt updates in this PR).

Replaced the generic "missing parameter" error with a write_to_file-specific error that:
- Explains **why** it happened: output too large or token limits reached
- Shows **context window usage** (e.g., "Context window is 60% full") using the shared `getLastApiReqTotalTokens()` utility
- Provides **actionable suggestions**: break into smaller steps, prefer replace_in_file for existing files
- Includes the standard tool use instructions reminder

## How It Breaks the Retry Loop

**Before:** Model fails → gets generic "retry" → tries identically → fails → infinite loop

**After:** Model fails → gets specific guidance about WHY (output too large, context X% full) and HOW to fix (use replace_in_file instead) → loop breaks on first retry

## Changes

- **`src/core/prompts/responses.ts`** — `writeToFileMissingContentError(relPath, contextUsagePercent?)` with clear guidance and optional context window awareness
- **`src/core/task/tools/handlers/WriteToFileToolHandler.ts`** — Uses specialized error with shared `getLastApiReqTotalTokens()` utility instead of generic `sayAndCreateMissingParamError`. Removed private `getContextUsagePercent()` method.
- **`src/core/prompts/__tests__/writeToFileMissingContentError.test.ts`** — 11 unit tests covering error message content and context window awareness

## Test Procedure

```bash
npm run test:unit -- --grep "writeToFileMissingContentError"
```

All 11 tests passing.

## Type of Change

- [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)

## Pre-flight Checklist

- [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore
- [x] Tests are passing
- [x] I have created a changeset using `npm run changeset`
- [x] I have reviewed contributor guidelines
author: ClineXDiego

reviews.body: Looks good!

commits.message:
fix: smarter retry for write_to_file missing content parameter (#7998)

Replace generic 'missing parameter' error with progressive guidance when
write_to_file fails due to empty content parameter. This breaks the
infinite retry loop where the model repeatedly attempts the same
write_to_file call that exceeds output token limits.

Changes:
- Add writeToFileMissingContentError() to formatResponse with 3 tiers:
  1st failure: suggestions (use skeleton + replace_in_file)
  2nd failure: strong directive (stop retrying write_to_file)
  3rd+ failure: CRITICAL stop, forces alternative strategies
- Add context window awareness: warns model when >50% context used
- Add getContextUsagePercent() helper to WriteToFileToolHandler
- Add 22 unit tests covering progressive escalation and context awareness

Fixes #7998
add changeset for write_to_file retry fix

refactor: simplify write_to_file error handling per review

- Simplify writeToFileMissingContentError to single-tier error following
  existing diffError pattern (no progressive escalation)
- Use shared getLastApiReqTotalTokens() for context window awareness
- Remove private getContextUsagePercent() method from handler
- Add proactive skeleton + replace_in_file guidance to write_to_file
  tool description for all variants
- Simplify tests to match new API (11 tests)
test: update system prompt snapshots
chore: revert write_to_file prompt guidance

feat: restore progressive 3-tier guidance for write_to_file missing content

Restore the progressive escalation that was removed in dd3c12d4e:
- Tier 1 (1st failure): Gentle suggestions (skeleton + replace_in_file)
- Tier 2 (2nd failure): Strong directive, 'Do NOT attempt full write again'
- Tier 3 (3rd+ failure): CRITICAL stop, forces alternative strategies
- Context window warning when >50% full
- Dynamic UI message: 'Retrying...' vs 'multiple times — different approach'
- 21 tests covering all tiers and context awareness

nit: extract context window warning threshold to named constant

Also replace emoji with plain text in warning message for consistency.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

## Pull request overview

This PR completes the rollout/migration from the previously-integrated “Sonnet 5” naming to **Claude Sonnet 4.6**, wiring the 4.6 IDs across providers, model registries, and UI surfaces while keeping **Sonnet 4.5** as the stable default.

**Changes:**

title: fix(telemetry): refresh OTEL org attributes on identify
body: ### Related Issue **Issue:** #XXXX ### Description The OpenTelemetry telemetry provider was not consistently including `organization_id` (and related org attributes like `organization_name`, `member_id`, `member_roles`) in OTEL log output. **Root cause:** `OpenTelemetryTelemetryProvider.identifyUser()` only rebuilt and cached `userAttributes` when the distinct ID changed (i.e., on first login or user switch). After an extension restart or org switch — where the user ID stays the same but the active org may differ — `userAttributes` remained empty or stale. Since all subsequent OTEL log events spread `...this.userAttributes` into their attributes, org context was silently missing. **Fix:** Decouple "refreshing cached user/org attributes" from "emitting the `user_identified` log event." Now `identifyUser()` always rebuilds `userAttributes` from the provided `ClineAccountUserInfo`, while the `user_identified` log and `setDistinctId()` call still only fire when the distinct ID actually changes. A private `buildUserAttributes()` helper was extracted for clarity. ### Test Procedure - Added 6 focused unit tests in `OpenTelemetryTelemetryProvider.test.ts` using an in-memory OTEL log exporter: 1. New user identification → emits `user_identified` with org attributes 2. Same distinct ID → no identify log, but org attributes present in subsequent logs 3. Org switch (same user ID) → attributes refreshed to new org 4. User with no active org → no org attributes leak 5. Org → no-org transition → stale org attributes cleared 6. Additional properties passthrough - Ran `npm test` (unit + integration). All new tests pass; 1 pre-existing failure in `LiteLlmHandler` (unrelated). ### Type of Change - [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality) - [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected) - [ ] ♻️ Refactor Changes - [ ] 💅 Cosmetic Changes - [ ] 📚 Documentation update - [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes ### Pre-flight Checklist - [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [x] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - [ ] I have created a changeset using `npm run changeset` (required for user-facing changes) - [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Screenshots N/A — backend-only change with no UI impact. ### Additional Notes - The `AuthService.ts` diff in this branch is from a separate commit; the core fix is in `OpenTelemetryTelemetryProvider.ts`. - To manually verify with console output: run the CLI with `CLINE_OTEL_TELEMETRY_ENABLED=true CLINE_OTEL_LOGS_EXPORTER=console`, authenticate, and check that OTEL log records include `organization_id` in their attributes. ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Fixes missing org attributes in OTEL logs by always refreshing user/org attributes in `identifyUser()` and adds tests for verification. > > - **Behavior**: > - `identifyUser()` in `OpenTelemetryTelemetryProvider.ts` now always refreshes `userAttributes` to ensure org context is included in logs. > - Extracted `buildUserAttributes()` to handle attribute construction. > - **Tests**: > - Added 6 unit tests in `OpenTelemetryTelemetryProvider.test.ts` to verify: > - Emission of `user_identified` log with org attributes on new user identification. > - Refresh of attributes on org switch without emitting `user_identified`. > - Handling of users with no active org and clearing stale attributes. > - Inclusion of additional properties in logs. > - **Misc**: > - Added logging in `AuthService.ts` to track user identification with org context. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for 1157c3badc50ce938db34de141e0a242eecbd2c6. You can [customize](https://app.ellipsis.dev/cline/settings/summaries) this summary. It will automatically update as commits are pushed.

title: fix(chat): keep reasoning visible before low-stakes tool groups
body:
### Related Issue

Issue: #8636

### Description

This PR started as a follow-up to the reasoning visibility regression and then expanded to cover the full thinking-row behavior after moving the loader out of the Virtuoso footer.

What was happening

1. Reasoning could disappear when low-stakes tools started immediately after reasoning.
2. After moving loader rendering from footer to row to avoid handoff flicker, loader visibility no longer matched all the broader waiting cases the old footer implementation covered.
3. The updated thinking label styling had a small visual regression where descenders (like the g in "Thinking") could clip in some states.

Technical approach and debugging journey

- First fix: keep standalone reasoning visible when low-stakes tool groups begin.
  - Root cause was in low-stakes grouping behavior. Reasoning could be routed into tool-group flow, but tool-group rendering intentionally excludes reasoning content, so it appeared to vanish.
  - The grouping logic now keeps that reasoning visible as its own row before the tool group, instead of letting it get swallowed.

- Loader architecture change: move thinking loader from Virtuoso footer to in-list row.
  - The footer-based approach had a mount/unmount handoff path when transitioning into streamed reasoning rows, which produced visible flicker in practice.
  - The loader now renders as a synthetic in-list reasoning row, so it stays in the same rendering pipeline as chat rows.

- Compatibility fix: restore old waiting coverage.
  - After the row migration, loader visibility initially became narrower than footer-era behavior.
  - Visibility logic was aligned back to the same waiting signal used before, with a small handoff guard to avoid transient disappearance while grouped rows catch up to raw stream events.

- Visual polish: prevent thinking text clipping.
  - The label line-height and non-interactive title styling were adjusted so brightness and vertical glyph rendering match prior behavior more closely.
  - This specifically avoids clipping descenders in the streaming/non-toggle state.

What this means for users

- Reasoning no longer disappears when low-stakes tool groups start right after reasoning.
- Thinking loader remains visible in the same broad request/waiting scenarios users already expected from the footer version.
- Transition behavior is smoother because loader and reasoning rows share in-list rendering rather than switching between footer and row.
- Thinking label appearance is closer to prior brightness and no longer clips text descenders.

### Test Procedure

I tested this in two layers.

1. Automated checks run repeatedly during iteration:
- `cd webview-ui && npx tsc --noEmit`
- `cd webview-ui && npm run test -- src/components/chat/chat-view/utils/messageUtils.test.ts src/components/chat/ThinkingRow.test.tsx`

2. Regression-focused behavior checks during development:
- reasoning then immediate low-stakes tool group start
- waiting states before content, during request progression, and while grouped tool activity is active
- transition from thinking loader to reasoning row without footer handoff flicker
- label appearance in loading and non-loading states

### Type of Change

-   [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
-   [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
-   [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected)
-   [ ] ♻️ Refactor Changes
-   [x] 💅 Cosmetic Changes
-   [ ] 📚 Documentation update
-   [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes

### Pre-flight Checklist

-   [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs)
-   [ ] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`)
-   [x] I have created a changeset using `npm run changeset` (required for user-facing changes)
-   [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Screenshots

Not included.

### Additional Notes

This PR includes iterative fixes that were driven by live UX feedback in the webview (loader visibility coverage, transition smoothness, and label rendering). The end state intentionally keeps behavior familiar while improving stability of the transition path.

title: fix(chat): prevent partial row churn during native tool arg streaming
body:
### Related Issue

Issue: N/A (small bug fix)

### Description

This PR fixes a chat flicker bug where both the streaming text blocks and the thinking footer rapidly re-rendered during act mode runs, most noticeably with `openai/gpt-5.2-codex`.

What we observed during investigation:
- In act mode, GPT 5.2 streamed tool-call argument deltas very rapidly.
- During that same period, partial `say("text", ..., partial=true)` messages were emitted at high frequency.
- Many of those partial text emissions were empty or effectively no-growth updates.
- The stream logs showed repeated `new_partial_true` events with changing timestamps, instead of stable `update_partial_true` updates on one active row.
- That row churn made both the text rows flicker and the waiting/thinking footer flicker.

Important context:
- Raw logs are intentionally not included here because they can contain sensitive request context.
- The findings above come from temporary instrumentation added during debugging and removed before finalizing the PR.

Root cause:
- In `processNativeToolCalls()`, we were finalizing the last partial text message too aggressively.
- That finalization happened even when there was no real assistant text content accumulated yet.
- Once the previous row was prematurely finalized, the next partial delta created a new row, repeating this loop rapidly.

Final fix in this PR:
- File: `src/core/task/index.ts`
- Change: only finalize the currently partial `say("text")` row when there is actual assistant text content, gated by `textBlocks.length > 0`.

### Mechanics of the fix

Before this change:
1. Tool arg delta arrives.
2. `processNativeToolCalls()` force-finalizes last partial text row, even with no assistant text content.
3. Next partial text update cannot attach to the old row.
4. A new partial row gets created.
5. Steps 1 to 4 repeat quickly, causing row churn, text flicker, and footer flicker.

After this change:
1. Tool arg delta arrives.
2. `textBlocks.length` is `0`, so we do not finalize the active partial text row.
3. Next partial text update continues on the same row.
4. Row continuity is preserved, so churn drops and both flickers stabilize.

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant M as GPT 5.2 stream
    participant T as processNativeToolCalls
    participant W as Webview rows

    rect rgb(255,245,245)
    note over M,W: Before
    M->>T: tool arg delta (no assistant text)
    T->>W: finalize partial text row
    M->>T: next partial delta
    T->>W: create new partial row
    M->>T: next partial delta
    T->>W: create new partial row again
    note over W: text rows flicker and footer flickers
    end

    rect rgb(245,255,245)
    note over M,W: After
    M->>T: tool arg delta (no assistant text)
    T->>T: textBlocks.length == 0
    T->>W: keep existing partial row open
    M->>T: next partial delta
    T->>W: update same partial row
    note over W: stable row continuity and stable footer
    end
```

Why this fix resolves the issue:
- It stops premature row closure during tool-call arg deltas that do not contain meaningful assistant text.
- That preserves partial-row continuity, so subsequent deltas update the same in-progress row instead of creating fresh rows.
- With less partial-row churn, both the streaming text block flicker and thinking-footer flicker drop significantly.

Scope:
- This PR intentionally keeps the fix narrow to native tool processing in `Task`.
- A handler-level dedupe experiment was explored during debugging and then reverted.

### Test Procedure

Automated checks run:
- `npx biome check src/core/task/index.ts --diagnostic-level=error`
- `npm run check-types`

Manual verification flow:
- Reproduce an act-mode run that streams tool-call arguments.
- Confirm partial text rows no longer get repeatedly recreated before assistant text is truly present.
- Confirm both text-block flicker and thinking-footer flicker are no longer rapidly toggling from row churn.

### Type of Change

-   [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
-   [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
-   [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected)
-   [ ] ♻️ Refactor Changes
-   [ ] 💅 Cosmetic Changes
-   [ ] 📚 Documentation update

title: fix(chat): restore reasoning traces and polish thinking UX
body: ### Related Issue **Issue:** #8636 ### Description This PR restores visible reasoning traces in chat and refines the thinking row UX so users can see model reasoning as it streams, then keep the chat clean after completion. Context: - We received repeated user feedback in #8636 that hiding reasoning behind only a "Thinking..." indicator made the model feel like a black box. - One concrete safety/trust example from that thread was users wanting to catch model behavior changes in real time (for example tool strategy changes), which is hard to do if reasoning is not visible. - The latest chat/tool-group refactors improved flicker/stability, but also left a path where reasoning could disappear from the rendered list. What this changes: - Restores standalone reasoning messages that were being dropped by the low-stakes tool grouping pipeline. - Keeps reasoning in chronological order when text arrives after reasoning. - Improves reasoning row presentation: - streamed reasoning is visible as "Thinking..." with shimmer - once complete, it becomes a collapsible "Thinking" row - completed and loading labels use consistent typography/color treatment - tightened spacing/alignment to reduce vertical jump during loading -> completed transition - removed left rule and left inset in the reasoning body for a cleaner layout - added a top fade indicator to match the bottom fade for scroll affordance - Hardens task-stream finalization behavior so pending partial reasoning gets completed reliably in UI state. Debugging journey and key findings: - First looked at recent commits touching reasoning/tool-group rendering (`#9298`, `#9300`, and surrounding changes). - Confirmed that `api_req_started` rows are intentionally filtered from visible rows, so visible reasoning depends on explicit `say("reasoning")` message flow. - Found a concrete drop bug in `groupLowStakesTools`: - `pendingReasoning` was cleared in `flushPending()` without being pushed. - This caused reasoning-only segments to vanish when not absorbed into a low-stakes tool group. - After fixing that, tests showed reasoning ordering needed one more adjustment: - when text was appended, pending reasoning had to flush first so reasoning appears before text. - We then iterated on pixel-level UX alignment (label naming, spacing, line-height, overflow clipping) based on manual QA feedback. Non-obvious decisions: - Kept the tool-group special case behavior intact (no expansion of reasoning inside grouped low-stakes tool summaries) while restoring reasoning visibility outside that path. - Avoided reintroducing the old `api_req_started` accordion behavior; reasoning visibility is implemented through existing reasoning row primitives. - Added a changeset because this is user-facing behavior in chat UX. ### Test Procedure What I tested and why: 1. Reasoning grouping logic - Ran targeted tests: - `cd webview-ui && npm run test -- src/components/chat/chat-view/utils/messageUtils.test.ts` - Added regression coverage for: - standalone reasoning preserved when no low-stakes tool group follows - standalone reasoning preserved before non-low-stakes tools - reasoning ordering remains correct relative to text 2. Thinking row UI behavior - Ran targeted tests: - `cd webview-ui && npm run test -- src/components/chat/ThinkingRow.test.tsx` - Verified behavior for: - streamed state rendering and shimmer class - toggle interaction for completed collapsible state 3. Type safety - Ran: - `cd webview-ui && npx tsc --noEmit` - `npm run check-types` (earlier in development while touching stream/task code) Potential risks checked: - Regression in tool-group handling due to reasoning flush changes - Duplicate reasoning completion behavior in stream finalization path - Visual jitter from button inherited sizing/overflow styles Why I am confident: - The root cause of missing reasoning was fixed at the exact drop point. - New targeted tests cover the regression path. - Stream finalization and rendering paths were validated together, not in isolation. ### Type of Change - [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality) - [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected) - [ ] ♻️ Refactor Changes - [x] 💅 Cosmetic Changes - [ ] 📚 Documentation update - [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes ### Pre-flight Checklist - [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [x] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - [x] I have created a changeset using `npm run changeset` (required for user-facing changes) - [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Screenshots Not included in this PR description, but the visible changes are in the chat reasoning row presentation and spacing. ### Additional Notes Files with the highest-impact logic changes: - `webview-ui/src/components/chat/chat-view/utils/messageUtils.ts` - `src/core/task/index.ts` - `webview-ui/src/components/chat/ThinkingRow.tsx` ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Restores reasoning trace visibility in chat and refines the thinking row UX, ensuring reasoning is visible during streaming and collapsible after completion. > > - **Behavior**: > - Restores reasoning trace visibility in chat, ensuring reasoning messages are not dropped by the tool grouping pipeline. > - Keeps reasoning in chronological order when text arrives after reasoning. > - Improves reasoning row presentation in `ThinkingRow.tsx`: > - Displays streamed reasoning as "Thinking..." with shimmer effect. > - Collapsible "Thinking" row after completion. > - Consistent typography and color treatment for labels. > - Adjusted spacing and alignment to reduce vertical jump during transitions. > - Removed left rule and inset for cleaner layout. > - Added top fade indicator for scroll affordance. > - **Bug Fixes**: > - Fixes reasoning drop bug in `groupLowStakesTools` in `messageUtils.ts` by ensuring `pendingReasoning` is pushed before being cleared. > - Adjusts reasoning ordering to ensure it appears before text when text is appended. > - **Tests**: > - Adds tests in `ThinkingRow.test.tsx` to verify streaming title styling and toggle interaction. > - Updates `messageUtils.test.ts` to cover reasoning preservation scenarios. > - **Misc**: > - Minor changes in `ChatRow.tsx`, `RequestStartRow.tsx`, and `MessagesArea.tsx` to support new reasoning behavior. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for b9302caa1a9fb50cf97c49730da8851f0f561185. You can [customize](https://app.ellipsis.dev/cline/settings/summaries) this summary. It will automatically update as commits are pushed.
