title: feat(subagents): tune research prompt output and command guidance
body: ### Related Issue Issue: N/A (prompt and UX tuning requested by maintainer feedback) ### Description @candieduniverse had some great ideas about our subagent prompts! And this PR incorporates those: This PR tunes subagent prompting so research runs are more useful and less verbose by default. Problem being solved: - Subagent output guidance was pushing heavy analysis format (line numbers plus a recommended-files section with per-file reasoning) even when the main agent only needed a concise handoff. - The command-use guidance did not clearly encourage in-command scripting patterns that can quickly narrow results during discovery. - The parent `use_subagents` description implied parallel fanout as the default pattern without clearly stating that one subagent is also a valid context-efficient strategy. What changed and why: - Updated the subagent execution suffix to default to concise handoff behavior: a `Relevant file paths` section with file paths only, unless deeper analysis is explicitly requested in the prompt. - Removed the default requirement for line numbers, summaries, and per-file explanations. - Reworded the subagent opener language to be less heavy while keeping the same exploration intent. - Added a single-line instruction that encourages being clever with command chaining or in-command scripting in `execute_command`, with pipes and filters for narrowing noisy output. - Added guidance in the `use_subagents` tool description that launching only one subagent is valid when multiple would waste context for small discovery tasks. - Updated docs to match the new behavior and expectations. Debugging and implementation notes: - Prompt integration snapshots failed immediately after changing prompt text, which was expected because the shared tool description and prompt suffix are rendered across many model families. - I regenerated snapshots in update mode so CI has a coherent baseline for the new wording. - No runtime logic or tool permissions were changed. This is prompt-content behavior plus docs alignment. Alternatives considered: - Keeping line numbers as a default and only dropping the recommended-files section. Rejected because the request was explicitly to avoid analysis-style output unless needed. - Adding more concrete command examples in the core prompt. Rejected in favor of a single non-prescriptive instruction so models keep flexibility. ### Test Procedure I tested prompt rendering and snapshot consistency directly: 1. `npx cross-env TS_NODE_PROJECT=./tsconfig.unit-test.json mocha src/core/prompts/system-prompt/__tests__/integration.test.ts` - Result: snapshot mismatches across prompt variants, expected due wording updates. 2. `npx cross-env TS_NODE_PROJECT=./tsconfig.unit-test.json mocha src/core/prompts/system-prompt/__tests__/integration.test.ts -- --update-snapshots` - Result: pass, snapshots updated. - Final summary from run: `930 passing`, `4 pending`. I also validated that changes are scoped to prompt/docs/snapshots and the branch is clean after commit. ### Type of Change - [ ] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [x] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality) - [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected) - [ ] ♻️ Refactor Changes - [x] 💅 Cosmetic Changes - [x] 📚 Documentation update - [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes ### Pre-flight Checklist - [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [ ] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - [ ] I have created a changeset using `npm run changeset` (required for user-facing changes) - [ ] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Screenshots N/A ### Additional Notes Because this prompt string is shared and snapshot-covered, snapshot updates span multiple model-family snapshot files even though behavior changes were localized to subagent prompt instructions. ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Refines subagent prompt behavior and guidance for efficiency, reducing verbosity and clarifying single subagent use in `SubagentRunner.ts` and documentation. > > - **Behavior**: > - Updated subagent execution suffix in `SubagentRunner.ts` to default to concise handoff with a `Relevant file paths` section, excluding line numbers and summaries unless requested. > - Removed default requirement for line numbers, summaries, and per-file explanations. > - Reworded subagent opener language to be less verbose. > - Added instruction for command chaining and in-command scripting in `execute_command`. > - Clarified `use_subagents` description to validate single subagent use for small tasks. > - **Documentation**: > - Updated `subagents.mdx` to reflect new behavior and expectations. > - Regenerated snapshots in `__snapshots__` to align with updated prompt text. > - **Testing**: > - Snapshot tests updated to reflect wording changes, ensuring consistency across prompt variants. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for d8298c0c1cdac62f0a4e72c99a885676117c9fc3. You can [customize](https://app.ellipsis.dev/cline/settings/summaries) this summary. It will automatically update as commits are pushed.
author: saoudrizwan

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR tunes subagent prompting to produce more concise, actionable output by default. The changes aim to reduce verbose analysis-heavy responses and encourage efficient command-line patterns for discovery work, while still allowing detailed output when explicitly requested. **Changes:** - Updated subagent system prompt suffix to default to concise file-path-only output instead of requiring detailed analysis with line numbers and per-file explanations - Modified the `use_subagents` tool description to clarify that launching a single subagent is valid for light discovery tasks - Added guidance encouraging clever command chaining and pipeline usage in `execute_command` to narrow results efficiently ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 27 out of 27 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | src/core/task/tools/subagent/SubagentRunner.ts | Rewrote SUBAGENT_SYSTEM_SUFFIX to shift from verbose analysis to concise file-path-focused output, removed default line number/summary requirements, and added command chaining guidance | | src/core/prompts/system-prompt/tools/subagent.ts | Extended tool description to clarify that single-subagent usage is valid when multiple would waste context | | docs/features/subagents.mdx | Updated documentation to reflect new default output format (file paths focus) and added note about command pipeline benefits | | src/core/prompts/system-prompt/__tests__/__snapshots__/* | Regenerated snapshots across all model family variants to reflect the updated tool description | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews.

title: Changeset version bump
body:
This PR was opened by the [Changesets release](https://github.com/changesets/action) GitHub action. When you're ready to do a release, you can merge this and publish to npm yourself or [setup this action to publish automatically](https://github.com/changesets/action#with-publishing). If you're not ready to do a release yet, that's fine, whenever you add more changesets to main, this PR will be updated.


# Releases
## cline@2.2.1

### Patch Changes

-   d3918dd: Canonicalize `attempt_completion` tool args by mapping `response` to `result` in the central tool executor to prevent intermittent missing-parameter retries with native parallel tool calling.
-   120754c: Allows users to enter custom aws region when selecting bedrock as a provider
-   5a75f08: Prevent Parent Container Scrolling In Dropdowns

title: feat: implement response chaining for Responses API
body: ### Related Issue **Issue:** #XXXX ### Description Implement response chaining by tracking and passing previous_response_id to continue conversations from the last assistant message. This enables the Responses API to maintain context across multiple turns. Key changes: - Search backwards through messages to find last assistant message with ID - Only send new messages after the chained response - Track function call metadata (call_id, name, id) across chunks - Include call_id in tool_call events for proper correlation - Clean up debug logging and remove commented code - Remove redundant "Ran out of tokens" log message This improves conversation continuity and ensures function calls are properly tracked with their associated IDs throughout the streaming response lifecycle. body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to Cline!

⚠️ Important: Before submitting this PR, please ensure you have:
- For feature requests: Created a discussion in our Feature Requests discussions board https://github.com/cline/cline/discussions/categories/feature-requests and received approval from core maintainers before implementation
- For all changes: Link the associated issue/discussion in the "Related Issue" section below

Limited exceptions:
Small bug fixes, typo corrections, minor wording improvements, or simple type fixes that don't change functionality may be submitted directly without prior discussion.

Why this requirement?
We deeply appreciate all community contributions - they are essential to Cline's success! To ensure the best use of everyone's time and maintain project direction, we use our Feature Requests discussions board to gauge community interest and validate feature ideas before implementation begins. This helps us focus development efforts on features that will benefit the most users.
-->

### Related Issue

<!-- Replace XXXX with the issue number that this PR addresses -->
**Issue:** N/A

### Description
Scrolling through a dropdown also scrolls through the parent container. That logic was written intentionally in [Tab.tsx](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/webview-ui/src/components/common/Tab.tsx#L27). This change applies `role="listbox"` and `role="option"` wherever it makes sense. I also added a change so that we check the target's closest element rather than just the target itself to account for scrolling on potentially nested elements that don't have that role e.g. in [ApiOptions.tsx](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/webview-ui/src/components/settings/ApiOptions.tsx#L336-L350) 

<!-- 
Help reviewers understand your changes by making this PR readable and well-organized:

- For larger changes, provide context about your approach and reasoning

Small PRs may need minimal description, but larger changes benefit from explaining where you're coming from. Much of this context can be in the linked issue above, so feel free to reference it rather than repeating everything here.
-->

### Test Procedure
- Open Cline settings and scroll through any dropdown while the parent container is scrollable (resizing your window to make it smaller can help with this) and ensure only the dropdown is scrollable

title: fix(subagents): retry initial stream bootstrap failures
body: ### Related Issue **Issue:** N/A ### Description This PR fixes a reliability gap in subagent runs where provider stream bootstrap errors could fail a subagent immediately, even when the same failure would often recover in the main agent loop. Problem - Subagent runs call `api.createMessage(...)` directly from their internal loop. - If stream creation fails before the first chunk, the subagent exits as failed. - In practice this showed up with Cline provider passthrough errors like `stream_initialization_failed` from the Vercel AI Gateway path. Why this happened - Provider-level `withRetry()` only retries rate limits by default. - This failure class is usually wrapped as stream initialization/network failure, not always surfaced as a clean `429`. - The main loop already has hardened first-chunk retry behavior, but subagents had a simpler loop and missed that protection. What changed - Added first-chunk retry logic to `SubagentRunner`: - `MAX_INITIAL_STREAM_ATTEMPTS = 3` - exponential backoff: `250ms`, `500ms`, `1000ms` - Added transient error classification for subagent initial stream failures: - status `429` - code `stream_initialization_failed` - message patterns for stream bootstrap/network timeouts and send failures - Routed subagent stream creation through this retry wrapper before normal chunk processing continues. - Added regression test that reproduces a first-attempt stream bootstrap failure and verifies recovery on the next attempt. Debugging journey - Reproduced and traced the error shape from the reported payload (`stream_initialization_failed`, Vercel gateway bootstrap failure). - Followed execution from `UseSubagentsToolHandler` to `SubagentRunner` and compared it against main loop request handling in `Task.attemptApiRequest`. - Confirmed subagent behavior was intentionally tolerant of per-subagent failures after a run starts, but did not have equivalent startup hardening before first chunk. - Verified that this is exactly where one failing branch in a subagent batch can end up marked failed while the main agent would have retried. Decisions and alternatives considered - Chosen: add a focused retry wrapper in subagent loop now. - Minimal risk, low surface area, directly addresses observed failure mode. - Considered: immediate cross-loop refactor to share one hardened agentic kernel. - Deferred to a follow-up because it is broader in scope and would mix reliability fix with architectural migration. Non-obvious gotchas - Subagents are headless and parallelized; they do not use the main loop's user-facing `api_req_failed` ask flow. - Matching main loop behavior exactly would require separating retry policy from UI/state side effects first. - Existing repository test state includes unrelated failures, so validation here is focused and targeted. ### Test Procedure How this was tested - Added unit test coverage for subagent bootstrap retry: - `retries initial stream failures before failing the subagent` - Ran targeted tests with transpile-only execution to validate runtime behavior: - `npx cross-env TS_NODE_PROJECT=./tsconfig.unit-test.json TS_NODE_TRANSPILE_ONLY=true mocha src/core/task/tools/subagent/__tests__/SubagentRunner.test.ts` - New subagent retry test passes. What was also checked - Existing subagent runner tests for native/non-native tool paths still pass in the same run. - Verified status aggregation behavior remains unchanged in `UseSubagentsToolHandler`. Known unrelated test constraints in current branch baseline - Full `npm run test:unit -- src/core/task/tools/subagent/__tests__/SubagentRunner.test.ts` is currently blocked by a pre-existing TypeScript error in `openai-native.ts`. - Full transpile-only unit run currently shows one unrelated existing failure in LiteLLM prompt-cache tests. Why this is safe - Change is localized to subagent startup stream handling. - No change to tool execution semantics, no change to subagent batch aggregation semantics. - Retries only apply before first chunk, so already-started runs are not behaviorally altered. ### Type of Change - [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality) - [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected) - [ ] ♻️ Refactor Changes - [ ] 💅 Cosmetic Changes - [ ] 📚 Documentation update - [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes ### Pre-flight Checklist - [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [ ] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - [ ] I have created a changeset using `npm run changeset` (required for user-facing changes) - [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Screenshots Not applicable. No UI surface changes. ### Additional Notes This is intentionally scoped as a reliability patch. A follow-up refactor can extract shared loop hardening from `Task` into reusable policy/kernels for both main and subagent loops. ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Adds retry logic for initial stream bootstrap failures in `SubagentRunner` to improve subagent reliability. > > - **Behavior**: > - Adds retry logic for initial stream bootstrap failures in `SubagentRunner` with `MAX_INITIAL_STREAM_ATTEMPTS = 3` and exponential backoff (`250ms`, `500ms`, `1000ms`). > - Classifies transient errors for retry: status `429`, code `stream_initialization_failed`, and specific message patterns. > - Routes subagent stream creation through retry wrapper before chunk processing. > - **Testing**: > - Adds regression test in `SubagentRunner.test.ts` for retrying initial stream failures. > - Ensures existing tests for subagent runner pass, verifying unchanged status aggregation behavior. > - **Misc**: > - No changes to tool execution or subagent batch aggregation semantics. > - Retries apply only before first chunk, not affecting already-started runs. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for 40e47e2c3e55e26a091460b8ab9ac30fcd4f226e. You can [customize](https://app.ellipsis.dev/cline/settings/summaries) this summary. It will automatically update as commits are pushed.

title: fix(deps): upgrade openai sdk to 6.21.0 for xhigh reasoning
body: ### Related Issue Issue: N/A (reported build failure in this thread) ### Description This change fixes a TypeScript build failure in the CLI caused by an SDK type mismatch around OpenAI reasoning effort values. Problem - The CLI build failed with `TS2322` in `openai-native.ts` because our internal normalized reasoning options include `xhigh`, while the currently installed `openai` SDK version in this branch exposed `ChatCompletionReasoningEffort` without `xhigh`. - The compile error occurred when assigning `requestedEffort` into a typed `reasoning` object for the Responses API path. Technical approach - Upgraded `openai` from `^6.9.0` to `^6.21.0`. - Updated lockfile entries accordingly. - Kept `openai-native.ts` behavior unchanged after dependency upgrade. Debugging journey - First, I reproduced and confirmed the failing type path in `openai-native.ts`. - I temporarily added a local fallback mapping for `xhigh` to `high` to unblock compilation, then validated builds. - After that, I verified the latest published `openai` package and checked type declarations directly in the packed artifact. - Confirmed `openai@6.21.0` includes `xhigh` in `ReasoningEffort` types. - Reverted local fallback code so we do not carry behavior changes that are no longer necessary. - Final state is dependency-only, with source logic unchanged from prior behavior. Gotchas discovered - A standalone `cd cli && npx tsc --noEmit` can fail in this repo when proto generated files are not present yet. Running `npm run cli:build` first (which runs protos) resolves that environment issue. - The lockfile delta is larger than a one-line version bump because npm rewrites the relevant dependency graph entries. Decisions and alternatives considered - Alternative considered: keep old SDK and patch code with custom narrowing or `xhigh -> high` mapping. - Decision: upgrade SDK instead. This keeps us aligned with current OpenAI type definitions and avoids unnecessary provider-specific behavior changes. ### Test Procedure 1. Ran `npm run cli:build` and confirmed success. 2. Ran `cd cli && npx tsc --noEmit` after proto generation and confirmed success. 3. Verified updated SDK type declarations include `xhigh` for reasoning effort. ### Type of Change - [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality) - [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected) - [ ] ♻️ Refactor Changes - [ ] 💅 Cosmetic Changes - [ ] 📚 Documentation update - [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes ### Pre-flight Checklist - [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [ ] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - [ ] I have created a changeset using `npm run changeset` (required for user-facing changes) - [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Screenshots Not applicable. No UI changes. ### Additional Notes No user-facing behavior change was intended beyond resolving the build-blocking type mismatch by aligning SDK types with currently supported reasoning effort values. ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Upgrade `openai` SDK to `6.21.0` to fix TypeScript build error due to `xhigh` reasoning effort type mismatch. > > - **Behavior**: > - Upgrades `openai` SDK from `^6.9.0` to `^6.21.0` in `package.json` to fix TypeScript build error `TS2322` in `openai-native.ts`. > - Resolves type mismatch for `xhigh` reasoning effort values in `ChatCompletionReasoningEffort`. > - **Technical Details**: > - Lockfile updated to reflect new `openai` version. > - No changes to `openai-native.ts` logic post-upgrade. > - **Debugging**: > - Verified `openai@6.21.0` includes `xhigh` in `ReasoningEffort` types. > - Removed temporary local fallback mapping for `xhigh` to `high`. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for 7abe59bebf70483043701f772080cce3c5dbd562. title: fix(chat): keep focus chain placeholder visible to prevent layout jump
body: ### Related Issue **Issue:** N/A ### Description This PR fixes a UX issue where the Focus Chain card area under the task header could disappear early (for example after cancelling the first request) before any checklist existed, then reappear later when a checklist was created. That behavior caused the main chat content below to shift vertically, which felt like the UI was jerking down unexpectedly. The change simplifies placeholder visibility in `ChatView` so it follows one rule: - Show the Focus Chain placeholder whenever Focus Chain is enabled and there is no existing checklist text yet. By removing extra guards tied to `resume_*` and `completion_result`, the header area remains stable until real checklist content exists, preventing the layout jump. ### Test Procedure 1. Start a task with Focus Chain enabled. 2. Confirm placeholder is shown immediately before checklist data exists. 3. Cancel the first request before checklist creation. 4. Confirm placeholder remains visible instead of disappearing. 5. Continue task until checklist appears. 6. Confirm placeholder is replaced by checklist without abrupt content jump. 7. Ran `pnpm -s tsc --noEmit`. ### Type of Change - [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality) - [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected) - [ ] ♻️ Refactor Changes - [ ] 💅 Cosmetic Changes - [ ] 📚 Documentation update - [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes ### Pre-flight Checklist - [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [ ] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - [ ] I have created a changeset using `npm run changeset` (required for user-facing changes) - [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Screenshots Not included. This is a small behavior fix in header placeholder visibility. ### Additional Notes None. ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Fixes Focus Chain placeholder visibility in `ChatView.tsx` to prevent layout jumps by simplifying visibility logic and removing unnecessary guards. > > - **Behavior**: > - Simplifies Focus Chain placeholder visibility in `ChatView.tsx` to show when Focus Chain is enabled and no checklist exists. > - Removes guards tied to `resume_*` and `completion_result` to prevent layout jumps. > - **Components**: > - Adds `showFocusChainPlaceholder` prop to `TaskSection` and `TaskHeader` to control placeholder visibility. > - Updates `FocusChain` to render placeholder when `showPlaceholderWhenEmpty` is true and no checklist text exists. > - **Misc**: > - Minor CSS adjustments in `MarkdownRow.tsx` and `ToolGroupRenderer.tsx` for consistent styling. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for eef56fca37fb1a1f769fd2647dcdfa922a8d1a8c. title: fix(task): canonicalize attempt_completion result parameter
body: ### Related Issue **Issue:** #XXXX ### Description This PR fixes an intermittent `attempt_completion` failure where the model sometimes emits `response` instead of `result` under native + parallel tool calling. This specifically happened with gemini flash 3. #### What was happening Runtime evidence from repeated reproductions showed that in failing runs: - `attempt_completion` reached execution with param keys like `['response', 'task_progress']` - `AttemptCompletionHandler` validates `result` as required and returned: - `Cline tried to use attempt_completion without value for required parameter 'result'. Retrying...` In non-failing runs, the same path produced `['result', 'task_progress']`. So this is not a missing-data case; it is parameter-name variance for the same tool call. #### Why this change `AttemptCompletionHandler` should stay strict and focused on canonical tool inputs (`result`). Instead of handler-local fallback logic, this PR canonicalizes the payload in one upstream place: - `ToolExecutor` now normalizes `attempt_completion` params before dispatch: - if `result` is absent and `response` is present, set `result = response` This preserves strict validation semantics while making the execution pipeline resilient to this observed model-output variance. #### Scope - Adds `canonicalizeAttemptCompletionParams()` in `ToolExecutor` - Applies canonicalization once in `ToolExecutor.execute()` prior to handler invocation - Adds unit tests for canonicalization behavior - Adds patch changeset ### Test Procedure 1. Added focused unit test suite: - `src/core/task/__tests__/ToolExecutor.canonicalization.test.ts` - cases covered: - canonicalizes `attempt_completion.response` -> `result` - no-op when `result` already exists - no-op for non-`attempt_completion` tools 2. Ran unit tests with grep: - `npm run test:unit -- --grep "ToolExecutor canonicalization"` - result: 3 passing 3. Runtime verification during debugging (prior to cleanup): - reproduced failing baseline with `['response','task_progress']` - confirmed canonicalization path prevents missing-`result` retries #### Risk assessment - Low risk, narrow scope (single tool + single known alias) - Non-attempt tools are unaffected - Existing valid `result` payloads are unchanged ### Type of Change - [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality) - [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected) - [ ] ♻️ Refactor Changes - [ ] 💅 Cosmetic Changes - [ ] 📚 Documentation update - [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes ### Pre-flight Checklist - [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [x] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - [x] I have created a changeset using `npm run changeset` (required for user-facing changes) - [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Screenshots Before After ### Additional Notes This intentionally uses centralized normalization in `ToolExecutor` (upstream) rather than broad handler-level permissiveness so `AttemptCompletionHandler` remains strict about required canonical params. ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Fixes `attempt_completion` parameter inconsistency by canonicalizing `response` to `result` in `ToolExecutor`. > > - **Behavior**: > - Fixes `attempt_completion` parameter inconsistency by mapping `response` to `result` in `ToolExecutor`. > - Ensures `AttemptCompletionHandler` receives consistent `result` parameter. > - **Functions**: > - Adds `canonicalizeAttemptCompletionParams()` in `ToolExecutor.ts`. > - Applies canonicalization in `ToolExecutor.execute()`. > - **Tests**: > - Adds `ToolExecutor.canonicalization.test.ts` to test canonicalization logic. > - Tests cover cases: converting `response` to `result`, no-op when `result` exists, and non-`attempt_completion` tools. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for df70cfb726c50af1355ccc1c3e2ed09b815dbe17. You can [customize](https://app.ellipsis.dev/cline/settings/summaries) this summary. It will automatically update as commits are pushed.

title: fix(task): prevent duplicate streamed text rows after completion
body: ### Related Issue **Issue:** N/A ### Description This PR fixes an upstream text rendering race in `Task.say(...)` that could create duplicate streamed text rows for MiniMax responses. The previous MiniMax provider fix addressed one provider-emission pattern (`content_block_start.text` repeated in `text_delta`), but runtime evidence showed a separate path where presentation called `say(\"text\", ..., partial=true)` after an identical text row was already finalized (`partial=false`). In that case, the old logic created a new partial row with the same text, resulting in duplicate lines. This change adds a narrow guard in `say(...)`: - If the incoming message is `type === \"text\"` with `partial === true` - And the latest message is a completed `text` row (`partial === false`) with identical content - Skip adding a new partial row. ### Test Procedure - Manual repro before fix (MiniMax): - Prompted with `say hello` and `say \"hi there!\"` - Observed duplicate streamed text lines in chat output. - Manual verification after fix (MiniMax): - Re-ran the same prompts. - Duplicate text rows no longer appeared. - Streamed text and completion result still rendered correctly. - Unit test clarification (`src/core/task/__tests__/Task.say.test.ts`): - `skips duplicate partial text when the same completed text was just rendered` directly models the upstream race (partial `text` arriving after identical completed `text`). - The same test fails on clean `main` (old behavior creates another partial row) and passes on this branch (new guard skips it). - `still adds a partial text when content differs` verifies no regression for normal partial updates. - Runtime evidence: - Provider emitted single text deltas. - Duplicate rows came from `Task.say(...)` partial lifecycle. - Post-fix guard prevented duplicate partial insertion while preserving normal updates. ### Type of Change - [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality) - [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected) - [ ] ♻️ Refactor Changes - [ ] 💅 Cosmetic Changes - [ ] 📚 Documentation update - [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes ### Pre-flight Checklist - [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [ ] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - [x] I have created a changeset using `npm run changeset` (required for user-facing changes) - [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Screenshots Before After ### Additional Notes N/A

title: Changeset version bump
body:
This PR was opened by the [Changesets release](https://github.com/changesets/action) GitHub action. When you're ready to do a release, you can merge this and publish to npm yourself or [setup this action to publish automatically](https://github.com/changesets/action#with-publishing). If you're not ready to do a release yet, that's fine, whenever you add more changesets to main, this PR will be updated.


# Releases
## cline@2.1.0

### Minor Changes

-   42ce100: Add Generate API Key on Hicap Provider selection

### Patch Changes

-   195294f: Add support for bundled endpoints.json in enterprise distributions. Extensions can now include a pre-configured endpoints.json file that automatically switches Cline to self-hosted mode. Includes packaging scripts for VSIX, NPM, and JetBrains plugins.

-   a1f2601: Replace the LiteLLM model list with a selector

-   739d75a: Add Claude Code provider support for Claude Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.5 1M variants via both full model names and aliases (`opus[1m]`, `sonnet[1m]`), and align the `opus` alias with Opus 4.6.

-   8440380: Add GitHub Actions workflow to build CLI from any commit for testing

-   b1a8db2: fix(cli): prevent hang when spawned without TTY

-   7c87017: Add Claude Opus 4.6 model support

-   d116ac5: Supports rendering markdown table in chat view.

-   6d8fb85: Fix CLI crashing in CI environments and with stdin redirection (e.g., `cline "prompt" < /dev/null`). Now checks both stdin and stdout TTY status before using Ink, and only errors on empty stdin when no prompt is provided.

-   70a9904: Fix JetBrains sign-in regression by adding fallback for openExternal RPC

-   f440f3a: fix: use vscode.env.openExternal for auth in remote environments

    Fixes OAuth authentication in VS Code Server and remote environments by routing browser URL opening through VS Code's native openExternal API instead of the npm 'open' package.

-   70a9904: fix: use vscode.env.asExternalUri for auth callback URLs only in VS Code Web

    Fixes OAuth callback redirect in VS Code Web (`code serve-web`, Codespaces) by using `vscode.env.asExternalUri()` to resolve the callback URI. This is gated behind a `vscode.env.uiKind === UIKind.Web` check so regular desktop VS Code continues to use the `vscode://` URI directly. The `getCallbackUrl` API now accepts a `path` parameter so the full callback URI (including route) is resolved correctly, and callers pass their path directly instead of appending after.

-   5308ded: Updating script documentation and removing unnecessary continue on error

-   b514f18: Prevent duplicate streamed text rows when a partial text update arrives after the same text was already finalized.

-   26391c9: Fix Bedrock model id

-   d19a877: Unify ViewHeader Styles Across All Views

-   5dcaa8c: Add Vertex Claude Opus 4.6 1M model option and global endpoint support, and pass the 1M beta header for Vertex Claude requests.

title: feat: replace legacy CLI subagents with native use_subagents tool
body: | VS Code subagents UI | CLI TUI subagents UI | | --- | --- | | | | ## TL;DR This PR delivers a full subagents architecture refresh. - Removes legacy CLI-process subagents - Introduces first-class `use_subagents` tool execution in-process - Reuses main agent runtime primitives instead of forking architecture - Adds real progress UX in both VS Code and CLI - Adds cancellation that actually aborts in-flight subagent work - Wires subagent cost/tokens into row-level and task-level reporting - Reintroduces a user-visible Subagents feature toggle in settings The end result is more reliable, easier to maintain, and much clearer to users while subagents are running. ## Why this change The old implementation path had structural issues: - depended on external CLI install/runtime shape - split orchestration logic across two different architectures - weak progress visibility during execution - cancellation behavior was inconsistent under load - higher maintenance overhead for provider/tool-calling differences This PR moves subagents into the same architectural lane as the main agent loop. ## What users will experience 1. Cline calls `use_subagents` with up to 5 prompts 2. User sees one approval row with all prompts 3. On approval (or auto-approval), subagents run in parallel 4. Each subagent shows progress in-place in chat/TUI 5. User can cancel at any time and all in-flight subagents abort 6. Main agent receives aggregated results and usage data ## Architecture overview ### 1) Legacy path removed Subagent execution is no longer routed through legacy CLI-subagent command infrastructure. The active path is now: - tool: `use_subagents` - status messaging: `use_subagents`, `subagent`, `subagent_usage` ### 2) In-process runner that reuses main loop primitives `SubagentRunner` reuses core runtime building blocks already used by the main agent: - provider/API handler creation - system prompt generation through `PromptRegistry` - native tool schema conversion when available - XML tool parsing fallback when native is off - existing tool handlers and coordinator This avoids duplicated orchestration code and keeps provider behavior aligned with the main loop. ### 3) Prompting strategy Subagents use the normal system prompt for the active model/provider plus a focused subagent suffix that asks for: - broad exploration when needed - comprehensive findings - file paths and line references - recommended files for the main agent to read next - readonly command behavior guidance for `execute_command` ### 4) Tool access model Subagents run under an allowlist of research-oriented tools: - `read_file` - `list_files` - `search_files` - `list_code_definition_names` - `execute_command` - `use_skill` Write/edit tools are not available in subagent runs. For commands, we intentionally did not add a hard command-blocking layer in subagent mode. Instead, we: - prompt strongly for readonly commands - rely on existing command safety rails already in the stack ## Approval and safety semantics ### User-facing approval Subagent approval uses standard tool approval UX, not command approval impersonation. The prompt appears as: - `Cline wants to use a subagent` (single) - `Cline wants to use subagents` (batch) with all prompts listed in order. ### Auto-approve mapping `use_subagents` is mapped to the read-files auto-approve bucket. So if a user has auto-approved read files, subagent invocation auto-approves too. ### Internal subagent tool execution Inside subagent runs: - execution uses `isSubagentExecution` - command execution runs in background-exec mode - nested ask/say UI noise is suppressed This keeps the chat timeline clean while preserving actual tool execution. ## Execution flow ```mermaid flowchart TD A[Main agent emits use_subagents] --> B[Render approval row with prompts] B --> C{Approved?} C -->|No| D[Return tool denied] C -->|Yes| E[Create SubagentRunner for each prompt] E --> F[Run in parallel with Promise.allSettled] F --> G[Stream per-subagent progress updates] G --> H[Collect results and usage] H --> I[Emit subagent_usage for task-level metrics] I --> J[Return aggregated result to main agent] ``` ## UI work included ### VS Code webview - dedicated subagent row rendering - improved spacing/alignment consistency with other rows - collapsible per-subagent output sections - in-place streaming updates to avoid duplicate artifact rows - progressive stats visibility during prompt construction - stats text normalization (separator and token wording) ### CLI TUI - dedicated `SubagentMessage` component - tree rendering with wrapped prompt handling - stable left guide lines with long prompts - completion/running visual states - compact stats formatting aligned with chat semantics ## Metrics and cost reporting - per-subagent rows show tool calls, tokens, and cost - task-level cost now includes subagent work via `subagent_usage` This closes a prior visibility gap where subagent spend was not reflected in top-level task totals. ## Provider and protocol compatibility ### Native + non-native tool calling Subagent execution supports both: - native tool calling providers - XML/text tool calling path when native is off This avoids feature regression when users disable native tool calling. ### Cline provider 403 fix Subagent initial user message now includes workspace metadata block required by server-side task-loop checks. This addresses intermittent 403s where parent requests passed but subagent requests were rejected. ## Cancellation behavior Added explicit abort handling in `SubagentRunner`: - abort in-flight API stream immediately - cancel active command execution if running This removes the old behavior where cancellation could stall waiting for another stream chunk. ## Settings and rollout Reintroduced a visible Subagents toggle in `Settings > Features` for both webview and CLI. Wired through all required layers: - proto request field - webview update path - CLI/ACP update path - state round-trip payload back to webview Also documented this in `.clinerules/general.md` to avoid future partial-wiring regressions. ## Testing ### Automated - `npm run lint` passed - targeted subagent unit suites passed: - `SubagentToolHandler` tests - `SubagentRunner` tests Note: - broader unit run still shows one pre-existing unrelated failure in LiteLLM prompt cache tests ### Manual Validated: - approval UX in webview and CLI - progress streaming in both UIs - cancellation + immediate resume edge cases - native on/off tool-calling paths - Cline-provider subagent request behavior post-metadata fix - settings toggle behavior in webview and CLI - task header cost including subagent usage ## Type of Change - [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [x] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality) - [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected) - [x] ♻️ Refactor Changes - [x] 💅 Cosmetic Changes - [x] 📚 Documentation update - [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes ## Pre-flight Checklist - [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [ ] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - [ ] I have created a changeset using `npm run changeset` (required for user-facing changes) - [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) ## Screenshots Will add VS Code and CLI screenshots in the table at the top of this PR. ## Additional Notes If reviewers want smaller slices, I can split follow-ups into separate PRs for: 1. runtime architecture only 2. UI polish only 3. settings/docs only ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Replaced legacy CLI-based subagents with a native in-process `use_subagents` tool that executes up to 5 parallel subagents with real-time progress tracking, cancellation support, and integrated cost/token reporting. > > | VS Code subagents UI | CLI TUI subagents UI | > | --- | --- | > | | | > ## TL;DR > This PR delivers a full subagents architecture refresh. > - Removes legacy CLI-process subagents > - Introduces first-class `use_subagents` tool execution in-process > - Reuses main agent runtime primitives instead of forking architecture > - Adds real progress UX in both VS Code and CLI > - Adds cancellation that actually aborts in-flight subagent work > - Wires subagent cost/tokens into row-level and task-level reporting > - Reintroduces a user-visible Subagents feature toggle in settings > The end result is more reliable, easier to maintain, and much clearer to users while subagents are running. > ## Why this change > The old implementation path had structural issues: > - depended on external CLI install/runtime shape > - split orchestration logic across two different architectures > - weak progress visibility during execution > - cancellation behavior was inconsistent under load > - higher maintenance overhead for provider/tool-calling differences > This PR moves subagents into the same architectural lane as the main agent loop. > ## What users will experience > 1. Cline calls `use_subagents` with up to 5 prompts > 2. User sees one approval row with all prompts > 3. On approval (or auto-approval), subagents run in parallel > 4. UI polish only > 3. settings/docs only > ## Subagent Tool Implementation > - **New `use_subagents` Tool**: Created `subagent.ts` defining a new tool that allows running up to 5 focused in-process subagents in parallel, each with its own prompt (prompt_1 required, prompt_2-5 optional). Tool is only available when subagents are enabled and not already in a subagent run. > - **SubagentToolHandler**: Implemented new `UseSubagentsToolHandler` class that manages subagent execution, approval workflows, concurrent execution of multiple subagents, status tracking with progress updates, and aggregation of usage statistics (tokens, cache metrics, costs). > - **SubagentRunner**: Created `SubagentRunner` class that orchestrates multi-turn conversations with the API for each subagent, restricts tool access to file operations/search/code definitions/bash/skills, handles both native and non-native tool calling formats, and manages abort functionality. > - **System Prompt Updates**: Added `use_subagents` tool documentation to all model variant snapshots and removed references to CLI subagent instructions. > ## CLI Subagent Removal > - **Removed CLI Infrastructure**: Deleted `cli_subagents.ts` component, `subagent_command.ts` utilities, and removed CLI_SUBAGENTS section from all system prompt variants and templates. > - **Removed Slash Command**: Deleted "subagent" slash command from `slashCommands.ts` and `slash-commands/index.ts`. > - **Removed CLI Detection**: Deleted `isCliSubagentContext` function and related CLI subagent detection logic from `cli-detector.ts`. > ## Terminal and Command Execution Refactoring > - **CommandExecutor**: Removed subagent-specific command transformation and telemetry; replaced with generic `CommandExecutionOptions` parameter supporting `useBackgroundExecution` and `suppressUserInteraction` flags. > - **CommandOrchestrator**: Added `suppressUserInteraction` option to conditionally suppress user-facing callbacks during background execution. > - **Terminal Managers**: Removed `subagentTerminalOutputLineLimit` property and `setSubagentTerminalOutputLineLimit` method from `AcpTerminalManager`, `VscodeTerminalManager`, and `StandaloneTerminalManager`; removed `isSubagentCommand` parameter from `processOutput` method. > - **Constants**: Removed `DEFAULT_SUBAGENT_TERMINAL_OUTPUT_LINE_LIMIT` constant. > ## Task and Tool Handler Updates > - **Task Configuration**: Added `isSubagentExecution` boolean property to `TaskConfig` interface; updated `executeCommandTool` callback to accept optional `CommandExecutionOptions`; added `cancelRunningCommandTool` callback. > - **Tool Handlers**: Added `isSubagentExecution` checks to auto-approve and suppress UI callbacks in `ExecuteCommandToolHandler`, `ListCodeDefinitionNamesToolHandler`, `ListFilesToolHandler`, `ReadFileToolHandler`, `SearchFilesToolHandler`, and `UseSkillToolHandler`. > - **Auto-Approval**: Updated `autoApprove.ts` to handle `USE_SUBAGENTS` tool case; added `isSubagentExecution` as auto-approval trigger in multiple handlers. > - **ToolResultUtils**: Added early return in `askApprovalAndPushFeedback` when `isSubagentExecution` is true to bypass approval flow. > ## Settings and State Management > - **Settings**: Removed `subagentTerminalOutputLineLimit` from user settings; moved `subagentsEnabled` to earlier position in settings configuration. > - **ExtensionState**: Removed `subagentTerminalOutputLineLimit` property; moved `subagentsEnabled` to earlier position; added new message types: `use_subagents`, `subagent`, `subagent_usage`. > - **Proto Definitions**: Added `USE_SUBAGENTS` enum value (17) to `ClineAsk`; added `SUBAGENT_STATUS` (34), `USE_SUBAGENTS_SAY` (35), `SUBAGENT_USAGE` (36) to `ClineSay`. > - **Proto Conversions**: Added mappings for `use_subagents` ask type and `subagent`/`use_subagents`/`subagent_usage` say types. > ## UI Components > - **ChatMessage**: Added `SubagentMessage` component rendering for subagent-related messages; added filter to skip `subagent_usage` messages; improved key handling in `renderInlineMarkdown` with unique key prefixes. > - **SubagentMessage (CLI)**: New component for displaying subagent prompts and status updates in CLI with tree-style formatting, compact token/cost statistics, and progress indicators. > - **SubagentStatusRow (Webview)**: New component for displaying subagent execution status in webview with expandable details, status icons, statistics, and result/error messages. > - **ChatRow**: Added conditional rendering of `SubagentStatusRow` for `use_subagents` messages. > - **CommandOutputRow**: Removed subagent command detection and custom rendering logic. > - **FeatureSettingsSection**: Removed CLI installation check and output limit slider; simplified subagents feature toggle configuration. > ## Message Handling > - **messageTranslator**: Added case handler for `subagent_usage` event type that breaks without action (hidden aggregate metrics event). > - **messageUtils**: Added filtering for `use_subagents` messages when followed by subagent say message; added `subagent_usage` to filtered message types. > - **useMessageHandlers**: Added `use_subagents` case to message type switch statement. > - **getApiMetrics**: Updated to include `subagent_usage` messages in usage metric aggregation. > ## Configuration and Cleanup > - **ConfigViewComponents**: Removed `subagentsEnabled` and `subagentTerminalOutputLineLimit` from `EXCLUDED_KEYS`. > - **SettingsPanelContent**: Added subagents feature setting with description for parallel subagent exploration. > - **System Prompt Context**: Added `subagentsEnabled` and `isSubagentRun` optional properties to `SystemPromptContext` interface. > - **Tool Constants**: Added `USE_SUBAGENTS` to `ClineDefaultTool` enum and `READ_ONLY_TOOLS` array. > - **Removed Platform Validation**: Removed macOS/Linux-only platform check for enabling subagents. > ## Tests > - **ChatMessage.test.tsx**: New test file with tests for subagent rendering including tree structure formatting and compact statistics display. > - **SubagentToolHandler.test.ts**: New test suite covering missing parameters, disabled subagents, approval flows, batch approval, auto-approval levels, parallel execution, and failure handling. > - **SubagentRunner.test.ts**: New test suite covering native tool calling, fallback to non-native mode, retry behavior, and workspace metadata handling. > - **getApiMetrics.test.ts**: New test file covering metric aggregation from multiple message types and malformed JSON handling. > - **Removed updateSettings.test.ts**: Deleted platform validation tests for subagent feature. > - **buttonConfig.test.ts**: Added test case for `use_subagents` button configuration. > - **slash-commands.test.ts**: Added test verifying deprecated subagent slash command is not available. > ## Documentation > - **General Gotchas**: Added two new gotcha sections explaining settings plumbing requirements and webview toggle wiring for user-toggleable settings. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for 492d5586170695a531587dca6ff3fc6469cbba1f. You can [customize](https://app.ellipsis.dev/cline/settings/summaries) this summary. It will automatically update as commits are pushed.

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR replaces the legacy CLI-process subagent workflow with an in-process `use_subagents` tool, updates both the VS Code webview and CLI TUI to render subagent approval/progress rows, and threads subagent usage into task-level accounting and settings.

**Changes:**
- Introduce `use_subagents` tool + in-process `SubagentRunner` with parallel execution, cancellation, and usage aggregation.
- Update UI layers (webview + CLI TUI) to display subagent approval/progress and hide aggregated usage events.
- Remove legacy CLI subagent entry points (slash command, CLI detector/command transform) and adjust settings/telemetry plumbing.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 118 out of 120 changed files in this pull request and generated 4 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| webview-ui/src/context/ExtensionStateContext.tsx | Adds `subagentsEnabled` default; removes legacy subagent output limit default wiring. |
| webview-ui/src/components/settings/sections/FeatureSettingsSection.tsx | Replaces legacy CLI-dependent subagent settings UI with a standard feature toggle entry. |
| webview-ui/src/components/chat/chat-view/utils/messageUtils.ts | Filters `use_subagents`/`subagent_usage` message visibility for chat rendering. |
| webview-ui/src/components/chat/chat-view/shared/buttonConfig.ts | Adds approve/reject button config for `ask: use_subagents`. |
| webview-ui/src/components/chat/chat-view/shared/buttonConfig.test.ts | Adds test coverage for `use_subagents` button config mapping. |
| webview-ui/src/components/chat/chat-view/hooks/useMessageHandlers.ts | Enables handling of `use_subagents` ask/say message types. |
| webview-ui/src/components/chat/chat-view/components/layout/WelcomeSection.tsx | Removes dependency on `subagentsEnabled` in banner memo deps after UI refactor. |
| webview-ui/src/components/chat/SubagentStatusRow.tsx | New webview row component for subag

title: fix(cli): preserve OAuth callback paths in CLI and ACP host wiring
body: ## Summary This PR fixes a CLI/ACP OAuth regression introduced after the VS Code Web callback work. In short: callback paths were being dropped in CLI host wiring, so Cline OAuth callbacks landed on `/` instead of `/auth`, which made the local callback server return `Bad request`. A real user-visible symptom was: `http://127.0.0.1:48801/?code=...` instead of: `http://127.0.0.1:48801/auth?code=...` ## Timeline and sequence of events 1. #9144 (merged Feb 7, 2026) - Set out to fix VS Code Web callback routing issues. - Included multiple auth-related changes in one PR, including web callback behavior changes and `openExternal` fallback work. 2. #9173 (merged Feb 10, 2026) - Followed up on #9144 and moved callback generation toward path-aware callback URLs and `asExternalUri` usage for web cases. - `getCallbackUrl` usage changed to path-aware call sites (`getCallbackUrl("/auth")`, etc). 3. Regression surfaced in CLI/ACP - CLI and ACP HostProvider callback factories did not forward the new callback path argument. - They returned base callback URLs, causing redirects to `/`. - `SharedUriHandler` only handles known auth paths like `/auth`, so `/` returns unknown path and the server responds with `Bad request`. ## Root cause The bug was not in OAuth token exchange itself. It was in callback URL construction at the HostProvider boundary in non-VSCode runtimes. After path-aware callback generation was adopted, these call sites still effectively ignored the requested path: - CLI host initialization callback factory - ACP agent host initialization callback factory That caused valid auth codes to arrive at the wrong route. ## Technical approach in this PR This PR applies the smallest behavior-correct fix: - Forward the requested `path` argument through CLI HostProvider callback wiring. - Forward the requested `path` argument through ACP HostProvider callback wiring. - Keep ACP preflight callback URL aligned to `/auth` for consistency with the Cline OAuth flow. Why this is correct: - It preserves path-aware behavior end-to-end instead of hardcoding one route. - It keeps support for other callback routes (for example MCP OAuth callback paths), which hardcoded `/auth` approaches can break. - It aligns runtime behavior with the existing expectation in shared auth services that callback paths are explicit. ## Why not hardcode `/auth` everywhere A hardcoded `/auth` patch can appear to fix Cline sign-in but narrows the callback surface and risks breaking non-Cline callback routes in CLI/ACP contexts. This fix intentionally preserves dynamic path forwarding so callers remain in control of route-specific callback URLs. ## Debugging journey and validation - Traced the callback lifecycle from auth request creation to callback handling. - Verified callback handler route matching behavior and failure mode for `/`. - Compared behavior across #9144 and #9173 to isolate where the CLI path loss was introduced. - Confirmed the fix by checking all host initialization call sites now preserve callback paths. ## Testing - `npm -C cli run -s test:run -- src/index.test.ts` - Passes. Notes on environment validation: - Full project typecheck was not clean in this isolated worktree because root-level generated artifacts and broader dependency graph are not fully provisioned here. - The regression itself is a route-construction bug, and this change is tightly scoped to that behavior. ## Alternatives considered 1. Hardcode `/auth` in CLI/ACP callback factories. - Rejected because it does not generalize to other callback paths. 2. Make `AuthHandler.getCallbackUrl(path)` required and rely on strict typing only. - Helpful for API correctness, but not sufficient alone if host wiring still discards caller-provided paths. 3. Add permissive `/` handling in callback router. - Rejected because it masks incorrect callback URL construction and weakens route intent. ## Impact - Restores Cline OAuth sign-in behavior in CLI and ACP where callbacks were landing on `/`. - Keeps callback path support intact for current and future route-specific auth flows. ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Fixes OAuth callback path handling in CLI and ACP by forwarding `path` argument to ensure correct URL construction. > > - **Behavior**: > - Fixes OAuth callback path handling in CLI and ACP host wiring by forwarding `path` argument in `ClineAgent` and `initializeCli()`. > - Ensures callbacks land on `/auth` instead of `/` to prevent `Bad request` errors. > - **Technical Approach**: > - Modifies `ClineAgent` and `index.ts` to pass `path` to `AuthHandler.getCallbackUrl()`. > - Keeps ACP preflight callback URL aligned to `/auth` for consistency. > - **Testing**: > - Verified with `npm -C cli run -s test:run -- src/index.test.ts`. > - **Impact**: > - Restores correct OAuth sign-in behavior in CLI and ACP. > - Preserves support for dynamic callback paths. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for ae07acee578825410c16efa9cb1de0a7d294912a. You can [customize](https://app.ellipsis.dev/cline/settings/summaries) this summary. It will automatically update as commits are pushed.

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview Fixes a CLI/ACP OAuth regression where the requested callback path (e.g. `/auth`) was not preserved when constructing callback URLs, causing OAuth redirects to land on `/` and be rejected by the local callback server/router. **Changes:** - Forward the `path` argument through CLI `HostProvider.initialize` callback URL wiring. - Forward the `path` argument through ACP `HostProvider.initialize` callback URL wiring. - Ensure ACP “preflight” callback server startup uses the `/auth` route. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | cli/src/index.ts | Updates CLI HostProvider callback URL factory to accept and forward `path` to `AuthHandler.getCallbackUrl(path)`. | | cli/src/agent/ClineAgent.ts | Updates ACP HostProvider callback URL factory to forward `path`, and preflights the auth callback server with `/auth`. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

title: fix(claude-code): add opus 4.6 1m model option
body: ### Related Issue Issue: #9207 ### Description This is a follow-up fix to the Vertex Opus 4.6 1M work, adding the equivalent Opus 4.6 1M option for the Claude Code provider. Problem The Claude Code provider model list had `claude-opus-4-6` but no 1M context variant for Opus 4.6, so users could not explicitly select a 1M Opus 4.6 model in Claude Code provider settings. What changed - Added `claude-opus-4-6[1m]` to `claudeCodeModels` in `src/shared/api.ts`. - It reuses the Opus 4.6 1M metadata from `anthropicModels["claude-opus-4-6:1m"]`. - It keeps Claude Code specific overrides (`supportsImages: false`, `supportsPromptCache: false`) consistent with other Claude Code model entries. - Updated Claude Code settings logic to recognize this model as reasoning-capable by extending the thinking-model list used by `ClaudeCodeProvider`. - Added a provider unit test in `src/core/api/providers/__tests__/claude-code.test.ts` verifying `ClaudeCodeHandler.getModel()` resolves `claude-opus-4-6[1m]` and returns a 1,000,000 token context window. Debugging and design notes - Claude Code model IDs use `[1m]` format, while Anthropic API model metadata in our shared map uses `:1m`. I kept these intentionally different: - Claude Code provider key: `claude-opus-4-6[1m]` - Source metadata key: `claude-opus-4-6:1m` - This keeps provider-facing model IDs aligned with Claude Code conventions while still reusing our centralized pricing/context metadata. - I did not change runtime model ID transformation in `ClaudeCodeHandler` because this provider passes model IDs directly to the Claude CLI, and this patch is scoped to adding the missing option plus UI/test support. Alternatives considered - Adding a `:1m` key to Claude Code models for symmetry with Anthropic provider. - Rejected because Claude Code conventions and docs use `[1m]` for the 1M variant. - Duplicating full model metadata values instead of spreading from `anthropicModels`. - Rejected to avoid drift and keep pricing/context definitions centralized. ### Test Procedure Ran: - `npx biome check src/shared/api.ts webview-ui/src/components/settings/providers/ClaudeCodeProvider.tsx src/core/api/providers/__tests__/claude-code.test.ts .changeset/chilly-birds-fly.md` Validated: - Claude Code model registry includes Opus 4.6 1M variant. - Claude Code provider thinking slider eligibility includes the new 1M model id. - Unit test coverage includes model selection path for `claude-opus-4-6[1m]`. Not run in this worktree: - Full `npm test` / full typecheck, because `node_modules` is not installed here. ### Type of Change - [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality) - [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected) - [ ] ♻️ Refactor Changes - [ ] 💅 Cosmetic Changes - [ ] 📚 Documentation update - [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes ### Pre-flight Checklist - [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [ ] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - [x] I have created a changeset using `npm run changeset` (required for user-facing changes) - [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Screenshots Not applicable for this provider model list and model-resolution change. ### Additional Notes This PR intentionally focuses only on Claude Code provider parity for Opus 4.6 1M model selection. ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Add support for `claude-opus-4-6[1m]` model in Claude Code provider, including tests and UI updates. > > - **Behavior**: > - Added `claude-opus-4-6[1m]` to `claudeCodeModels` in `api.ts`, reusing metadata from `anthropicModels`. > - Updated `ClaudeCodeProvider.tsx` to include `claude-opus-4-6[1m]` in `SUPPORTED_CLAUDE_CODE_THINKING_MODELS`. > - **Tests**: > - Added unit tests in `claude-code.test.ts` to verify `ClaudeCodeHandler.getModel()` resolves `claude-opus-4-6[1m]` and returns a 1,000,000 token context window. > - **Misc**: > - Ensured model ID conventions are consistent with Claude Code provider standards. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for ee441f9b0a6fa7ce0f8f1a7f469c9b0d25d6e15f. title: fix(vertex): add opus 4.6 1m model support on Vertex
body: ### Related Issue Issue: #9207 ### Description This PR fixes the Vertex Claude Opus 4.6 capability mismatch reported in #9207 and discussed in https://github.com/cline/cline/issues/9207#issuecomment-3873889465. Problem Users selecting Vertex `claude-opus-4-6` in Cline could not access a 1M context option and could not use the global endpoint toggle for this model, even though equivalent capabilities were already modeled for Anthropic API and Bedrock variants in our own registry. What changed - Added `supportsGlobalEndpoint: true` for Vertex `claude-opus-4-6`. - Added Vertex `claude-opus-4-6:1m` with `contextWindow: 1_000_000` and `tiers: CLAUDE_OPUS_1M_TIERS`. - Updated the Vertex provider runtime handling to treat `:1m` as a local suffix, call the base model id, and send `anthropic-beta: context-1m-2025-08-07` so the new 1M option actually works at request time. - Updated Vertex docs language that previously said only Gemini supports global endpoint. Debugging journey and rationale I started from the issue comment and compared provider patterns in `src/shared/api.ts`. The Anthropic and Bedrock paths already had a consistent approach for 1M context models: 1. Keep a UI model id with `:1m` suffix for selection. 2. Strip that suffix before sending the actual model name. 3. Attach the 1M beta header/flag during the request. Vertex had none of these for Opus 4.6. Simply adding a `:1m` entry in the model map would have created a regression where requests would attempt to call `claude-opus-4-6:1m` directly, which the backend model id does not use. To prevent that, I mirrored the working Anthropic/Bedrock handling in the Vertex provider. I intentionally did not change `maxTokens` in this PR. The linked code review comment flagged that part for separate verification. This keeps this patch scoped to the two low risk fixes we can validate from existing internal patterns and avoids mixing in a potentially provider contract sensitive output token change. Alternatives considered - Only updating `src/shared/api.ts` model metadata. Rejected because runtime request behavior for `:1m` would be incomplete. - Changing `maxTokens` to `128_000` now. Rejected in this pass to keep the fix narrowly aligned with the referenced comment and avoid shipping an unverified provider limit assumption in the same patch. ### Test Procedure What I ran - `npx biome check src/shared/api.ts src/core/api/providers/vertex.ts docs/provider-config/gcp-vertex-ai.mdx .changeset/soft-cars-move.md` What I validated - Model registry now exposes Vertex Opus 4.6 global endpoint support and a 1M variant. - Vertex request path now strips `:1m` and includes the `anthropic-beta` header for 1M requests. - Docs note on global endpoint support is no longer Gemini-only. What I could not run in this worktree - Full `npm test` and full repo typecheck, because this worktree does not have `node_modules` installed. Risk check - Behavior change is scoped to Vertex Claude model handling. - Non-Claude Vertex model flow remains unchanged. - Existing Anthropic and Bedrock logic was used as a reference pattern for implementation consistency. ### Type of Change - [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality) - [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected) - [ ] ♻️ Refactor Changes - [ ] 💅 Cosmetic Changes - [x] 📚 Documentation update - [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes ### Pre-flight Checklist - [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [ ] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - [x] I have created a changeset using `npm run changeset` (required for user-facing changes) - [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Screenshots Not applicable. This change affects provider model metadata and request construction behavior. ### Additional Notes If maintainers want, I can follow this with a second PR that only addresses the Vertex `maxTokens` value after validating the exact production limit and any required API flags. ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Add support for Vertex Claude Opus 4.6 1M model and global endpoint, updating model handling and documentation. > > - **Behavior**: > - Add `claude-opus-4-6:1m` model with `contextWindow: 1_000_000` and `tiers: CLAUDE_OPUS_1M_TIERS` in `api.ts`. > - Enable `supportsGlobalEndpoint` for `claude-opus-4-6` and `claude-opus-4-6:1m` in `api.ts`. > - Update `createMessage()` in `vertex.ts` to handle `:1m` suffix and add `anthropic-beta` header for 1M requests. > - **Documentation**: > - Update `gcp-vertex-ai.mdx` to include `claude-opus-4-6` in global endpoint support. > - **UI**: > - Add `claude-opus-4-6:1m` to `SUPPORTED_THINKING_MODELS` in `VertexProvider.tsx`. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for 4ed20bca1c9ca9d3b891d32a399cf84858d5b9c8. You can [customize](https://app.ellipsis.dev/cline/settings/summaries) this summary. It will automatically update as commits are pushed.

title: docs: improve PR creation skill to use --body-file flag
body: ### Related Issue **Issue:** #8573 ### Description Improves the PR creation skill documentation to use `--body-file` instead of inline `--body` when creating PRs with `gh` CLI. This approach avoids shell escaping issues, newline problems, and command-line flakiness when dealing with complex markdown content like templates with checkboxes, multiple sections, and special characters. The change was motivated by the work done in #8785, where creating PRs with complex bodies exposed the brittleness of the inline approach. ### Changes - Replace inline `--body` examples with `--body-file` approach - Add step-by-step instructions for writing to temp file, creating PR, and cleaning up - Document why this approach is more reliable - Update both regular and draft PR examples ### Test Procedure - Review the updated skill documentation - title: feat: enable sync-ed deletion for remote mcp servers from remote config to extension
body: ### Related Issue closes: https://linear.app/cline-bot/issue/PF-461/prevent-user-from-circumventing-remotely-configured-servers-cant-be closes: https://linear.app/cline-bot/issue/PF-462/if-a-remote-mcp-is-deleted-on-remote-config-the-corresponding-remote Part of https://linear.app/cline-bot/issue/PF-286/extension-mcp-remote-server-configuration ### Description This PR enhances remote MCP config with the following changes: - Deleted remote servers from remote config should be removed: Previously the `previousRemoteMCPServers` was stored in an in-memory cache that gets wiped on every extension restart. On restart, the sync had no memory of what was previously remote configured, so it couldn't detect removals. This PR added a persistent `remoteConfigured: true` marker to the settings file. The sync now removes any server with this marker that's no longer in the remote config, regardless of in-memory state. The sync also always runs when remote config is active (removed the conditional guard). - Ensure manual JSON edit cannot removes remote server: This PR added logic in the file watcher callback that checks the remote config cache for `remoteMCPServers` and re-adds any that were manually removed from the settings file, writing back with the `isUpdatingFromRemoteConfig` flag to prevent re-triggering. ### Test Procedure Tested locally (note that clicking and unclicking the opt out of remote config in the test is to speed up extension refresh (otherwise it takes an hour). 1) remote mcp server deletion is sync-ed between remote config and extension: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2f5fa8ee-c145-41e8-ab81-c06265abb982 2) user manually removing an mcp server will not actually remove it if such server is remotely configured to exist: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6f267bab-56a4-4924-abd6-a1986775b289 ### Type of Change - [ ] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [x] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality) - [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected) - [ ] ♻️ Refactor Changes - [ ] 💅 Cosmetic Changes - [ ] 📚 Documentation update - [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes ### Pre-flight Checklist - [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [x] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - [ ] I have created a changeset using `npm run changeset` (required for user-facing changes) - [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Screenshots ### Additional Notes ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Enhance remote MCP server synchronization by using a persistent `remoteConfigured` marker to track and manage remote servers across restarts. > > - **Behavior**: > - `syncRemoteMcpServersToSettings` in `syncRemoteMcpServers.ts` now uses a `remoteConfigured` marker to track remote servers, ensuring they are removed if not in remote config. > - `applyRemoteConfig` in `utils.ts` always syncs remote MCP servers when remote config is active, using the `remoteConfigured` marker. > - `McpHub` class in `McpHub.ts` prevents manual deletion of remote servers by re-adding them if removed from the settings file. > - **Tests**: > - New tests in `syncRemoteMcpServers.test.ts` for adding/removing servers with `remoteConfigured` marker and handling edge cases. > - **Schemas**: > - `BaseConfigSchema` in `schemas.ts` updated to include `remoteConfigured` boolean. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for b8c5d987fc100df07266c602bde44a09a23f67a9. title: make trusted npm publish workflow
body: ### Related Issue **Issue:** N/A ### Description This PR introduce a new gh actions workflow for publishing the CLI to npm registry. It uses trusted publishing (https://docs.npmjs.com/trusted-publishers) via OIDC instead of granular tokens. This means that we don't have to worry about token rotation and security issues related to storing tokens in GitHub secrets. The workflow is triggered nightly for nightly releases and manually for main releases. NPM only allows us to trust a single github workflow hence we have to use the same workflow for both nightly and main releases. The workflow checks the trigger event data to determine whether it's a nightly or main release and publishes accordingly. ### Test Procedure I created a test npm project (https://www.npmjs.com/package/trusted-publishing-0209) and set up a new repo with these workflows enabled. I tested publishing both main and nightly builds to the npm registry. I also verified that the workflow fails if the OIDC token is not properly configured or if the npm registry is not set up correctly. I checked that the published packages are available on npm and that they have the correct version numbers. I also tested that the workflow can be triggered manually from the GitHub Actions tab and that it runs successfully. ### Type of Change - [ ] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality) - [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected) - [ ] ♻️ Refactor Changes - [ ] 💅 Cosmetic Changes - [ ] 📚 Documentation update - [x] 🏃 Workflow Changes ### Pre-flight Checklist - [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [x] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - [ ] I have created a changeset using `npm run changeset` (required for user-facing changes) - [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Screenshots ### Additional Notes ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Introduces a trusted npm publish workflow using OIDC, consolidating nightly and main releases into a single workflow with enhanced security. > > - **Workflows**: > - Adds `publish-cli-trusted.yaml` to handle both nightly and main npm releases using OIDC for trusted publishing. > - Replaces `workflow_dispatch` with `workflow_call` in `npm-main.yaml` and `npm-nightly.yaml`. > - Updates Node.js version to `24.x` in `npm-main.yaml` and `npm-nightly.yaml`. > - **Permissions**: > - Adds `id-token: write` permission in `npm-main.yaml` and `npm-nightly.yaml` for OIDC. > - **Behavior**: > - Nightly releases are scheduled and can be forced with `force_nightly_publish` input. > - Main releases require manual confirmation with `confirm_publish` input. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for 5dd78a1bea1a34c0e022540b6a72c02b65e7a3c6. You can [customize](https://app.ellipsis.dev/cline/settings/summaries) this summary. It will automatically update as commits are pushed.

commits.message:
fix: use vscode.env.asExternalUri for web OAuth callbacks

In VS Code Web (Codespaces, code serve-web), OAuth callbacks using
http://127.0.0.1:PORT break because the extension host runs remotely.

Changes:
- getCallbackUrl now accepts a path parameter
- Desktop: uses vscode://extension-id/path directly
- Web (UIKind.Web): uses vscode.env.asExternalUri() for web-reachable URL
- Updated all callers (/auth, /openrouter, /hicap, /requesty, MCP) to
  pass path and use URL+searchParams for proper encoding
- Added regression test asserting web callback URL is not 127.0.0.1
- AuthHandler (localhost HTTP) now only used by CLI/standalone mode

fix: use URL.searchParams for proper callback URL encoding

Callers were using template literal interpolation to embed callback URLs
into query strings, which breaks when the URL contains special characters
(e.g. from asExternalUri with query params). Use URL+searchParams.set()
which automatically encodes values.
chore: revert unrelated whitespace change in account.proto

revert: remove non-essential URL encoding changes in auth callers

Keep only the core fix (getCallbackUrl path parameter + asExternalUri for web).
Revert the URL+searchParams encoding improvement to minimize diff.
Merge branch 'main' into fix/vscode-web-oauth-callback

fix: URL-encode callback_url in auth callers, add encoding test

In VS Code Web, callback URLs from asExternalUri can contain their own
query params (?tkn=...&extra=...). String-interpolating them into
callback_url= causes everything after the first & to be parsed as
top-level params, truncating the callback URL.

Use URL + searchParams.set() in openrouter, hicap, and requesty callers.
Replace tautology test with deterministic round-trip encoding assertions.
