reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR adds auto-approval support for bash commands executed in `attempt_completion` tool calls. Previously, these commands always required manual approval. The changes enable these commands to be auto-approved based on the same settings used for regular `execute_command` tool calls. **Changes:** - Add auto-approval logic for bash commands in attempt_completion handlers - Display auto-approved commands as 'say' messages instead of 'ask' prompts - Add notification system for manual approval requests and long-running auto-approved commands - Fix Logger import path from `@/shared` to `@shared` --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.
I tried the changes out and it works well.  How nice not to have to approve the final command when I have auto-approvals on.

## Pull request overview

This PR simplifies the repository’s GitHub Actions setup by removing automated AI PR review/issue triage workflows and eliminating dependency caching from publish-related workflows.

**Changes:**
- Removed AI review/triage workflow files (`cline-pr-review`, `claude-pr-review`, `claude-issue-triage`).
- Removed `actions/cache` usage from extension publish and NPM publish workflows.
- Adjusted install steps to run unconditionally (by removing cache-hit conditionals).

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 7 out of 7 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| .github/workflows/publish.yml | Removes dependency caching steps from the release publish workflow. |
| .github/workflows/publish-nightly.yml | Removes dependency caching steps from the nightly extension publish workflow. |
| .github/workflows/npm-nightly.yaml | Removes dependency caching step from the nightly NPM publish workflow. |
| .github/workflows/npm-main.yaml | Removes dependency caching step from the main/manual NPM publish workflow. |
| .github/workflows/cline-pr-review.yml | Deletes the Cline-based automated PR review workflow. |
| .github/workflows/claude-pr-review.yml | Deletes the Claude-based automated PR review workflow. |
| .github/workflows/claude-issue-triage.yml | Deletes the Claude-based automated issue triage workflow. |
</details>



<details>
<summary>Comments suppressed due to low confidence (1)</summary>

**.github/workflows/publish-nightly.yml:45**
* `Check for recent commits` currently uses `exit 0`, which only ends that step; the job will still proceed to install/build/publish. If the intent is to skip publishing when there were no commits, set an output (e.g., `skip=true/false`) and gate subsequent steps (or the whole job) with an `if:` condition based on that output.

title: feat(bedrock): Support parallel tool calling in Amazon Bedrock [CLINE-1291]
body: ### Related Issue **Issue:** [CLINE-1291] ### Description This PR introduces parallel tool calling support for the Amazon Bedrock provider. This will enable us to add Bedrock as a `nextGenModelProvider` in `src/utils/model-utils.ts` (in a follow-up PR). ### Test Procedure _**Note:** I'm using the Cline CLI in these steps, but you can also use the VSCode extension to do the same. The configuration button clicks are specific to the extension UI, and the task directory is in a different place, but it's conceptually the same._ #### 1) Configure the Cline CLI to use the Amazon Bedrock provider and a model that your credentials have access to. I won't detail how to set up AWS credentials in these testing notes, but that's prerequisite before this step. #### 2) Run a task that we're confident will cause multiple tool calls. For example, research tasks tend to require at lease lots of parallel `read_file` tool uses. ``` echo "Please come up to speed on the architecture of the cline-core standalone." | cline ``` #### 3) One-liner command to check for parallel tool calling (i.e. more than 1 `tool_use` block in a single assistant message): ``` TASK=$(ls -t ~/.cline/data/tasks | head -1) && jq '[.[] | select(.role=="assistant") | select([.content[]? | .type == "tool_use"] | map(select(.)) | length >= 2)] | length' ~/.cline/data/tasks/$TASK/api_conversation_history.json ``` ### Type of Change - [ ] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [x] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality) - [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected) - [ ] ♻️ Refactor Changes - [ ] 💅 Cosmetic Changes - [ ] 📚 Documentation update - [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes ### Pre-flight Checklist - [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [x] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - [ ] I have created a changeset using `npm run changeset` (required for user-facing changes) - [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Screenshots n/a ### Additional Notes n/a ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Adds support for parallel tool calling in Amazon Bedrock by integrating tool configuration and handling into the existing system and updating tests. > > - **Behavior**: > - Adds support for parallel tool calling in `AwsBedrockHandler` by integrating tool configuration into `createMessage`, `executeConverseStream`, and `formatMessagesForConverseAPI`. > - Updates `mapClineToolsToBedrockToolConfig` to convert Cline tools to Bedrock tool configuration. > - Modifies `getModelId` to handle cross-region inference for new models. > - **Tests**: > - Adds tests in `bedrock.test.ts` for tool use handling, tool config mapping, and native tool calling integration. > - Tests cover scenarios like multiple tool calls, tool use interleaving with text, and tool configuration mapping. > - **Misc**: > - Updates `isNextGenModelProvider` and `isNativeToolCallingConfig` in `model-utils.ts` to include Bedrock as a next-gen provider. > - Adds Bedrock to `getNativeConverter` in `ClineToolSet.ts`. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for d42ca4f53d6989e5efc38295ac1c2ef83192ef65. reviews.body:
This PR works and I have verified this, it adds support for Native tool calling and parallel tool call. 

It also works for older XML based conversation continuation and it also limits to the model that support NTC therefore this is good to merge

## Pull request overview Adds native (parallel) tool-calling support to the Amazon Bedrock provider so Bedrock models that qualify for “next-gen / native tools” can emit multiple tool calls in a single assistant turn, and Cline can send tool definitions/results using Bedrock’s `toolConfig`/Converse formats. **Changes:** - Mark `bedrock` as a next-gen provider to enable native tool calling when the model family qualifies. - Extend `AwsBedrockHandler` to: accept `tools`, map Cline tools into Bedrock `toolConfig`, parse streamed Bedrock tool-use blocks, and format `tool_use` / `tool_result` blocks into Converse API message content. - Add/extend Bedrock tests for tool-use streaming, toolConfig mapping, message formatting, and next-gen/native-tool-calling eligibility. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | src/utils/model-utils.ts | Adds `bedrock` to next-gen provider list; small parsing cleanup. | | src/core/prompts/system-prompt/registry/ClineToolSet.ts | Enables Bedrock to use the native tools converter that produces Anthropic-style `input_schema`. | | src/core/api/providers/bedrock.ts | Implements Bedrock toolConfig mapping + streamed tool-use handling + tool_use/tool_result message formatting; threads `tools` through message creation. | | src/core/api/providers/__tests__/bedrock.test.ts | Adds comprehensive tests for Bedrock tool calling, toolConfig mapping, formatting, and native-tool-calling eligibility. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started

commits.message: feat(bedrock): Create agent implementation plan for supporting parallel tool calling.
Add Bedrock tool calling support
Improve Bedrock tool calling test guidance
Add Bedrock CLI parallel tool calling test script
fix: add ALLOW_AWS_DEFAULT_CHAIN support to live integration test script
chore: add changeset for Bedrock parallel tool calling

feat(bedrock): enable native parallel tool calling for Bedrock provider

- Add 'bedrock' to isNextGenModelProvider() so native tool calling is enabled
- Add 'bedrock' to getNativeConverter() to use Anthropic-format tool specs (input_schema)
- Fix empty tool description validation error in mapClineToolsToBedrockToolConfig
  (Bedrock requires description length >= 1)
- Update CLI test to use Sonnet 4.5 (Haiku too small for native tool calling)
- Add <invoke> XML detection to CLI test to catch XML fallback

Verified: conversation history shows 3 native tool_use blocks in a single
assistant response with 3 matching tool_result blocks — true parallel
tool calling via Bedrock Converse API.
docs: mark all phases complete in bedrock parallel tool calling implementation plan
chore: switch test scripts default model to Haiku 4.5 (cheaper for testing)
feat: enhance CLI verification suite with 3 test cases (single, parallel, round-trip)
Remove bedrock parallel tool calling implementation plan doc.

refactor: simplify to single CLI verification script for bedrock parallel tool calling

Remove the handler-level test script (test-bedrock-tool-calling.ts) and consolidate
into a single focused CLI test that proves parallel tool calling works end-to-end:
- Spawns Cline CLI with Bedrock config
- Asks it to read 3 files
- Verifies ≥2 parallel native tool calls (not XML fallback)
- Task completion proves tool result round-trip works

refactor(bedrock): improve type safety and code quality for parallel tool calling

- Add typed interfaces (ToolUseStart, ToolUseDelta) for Bedrock stream
  events instead of relying on `as any` casts
- Extend ContentBlockStart and ContentBlockDelta interfaces with toolUse
  fields so stream parsing uses typed property access
- Remove dead `inputBuffer` field from activeToolCalls Map (was tracked
  but never read — tool input deltas are yielded immediately)
- Add JSDoc to mapClineToolsToBedrockToolConfig explaining its purpose
  and return semantics
- Document why createDeepseekMessage intentionally ignores the tools
  parameter (DeepSeek R1 uses InvokeModel, not Converse API)

refactor(scripts): improve test script readability and resource cleanup

- Add try/finally with cleanupDirs() to remove temp workspace and config
  dirs after each run (previously accumulated in $TMPDIR)
- Extract named constants for CLI_TIMEOUT_SECONDS and HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS
- Add CliResult interface for the runCli return type
- Rename cryptic variables: hb → heartbeatInterval, c → chunk, p/d → filePath/data
- Add JSDoc to parseReadFilePaths and hasXmlFallback
- Add explanatory comments to empty catch blocks
- Log stderr on non-zero exit code for easier debugging
- Extract createTestWorkspace() to separate workspace setup from main flow
- Add section separator comments for visual structure

test(bedrock): add missing edge-case tests and remove dead describe block

- Add tests for mapClineToolsToBedrockToolConfig edge cases:
  undefined/empty input returns undefined, tools without input_schema
  are silently dropped
- Add test for formatMessagesForConverseAPI with array tool_result
  content (multi-block text responses)
- Add test for tool_result is_error → status:'error' mapping
- Remove empty 'reasoning content handling (deprecated)' describe block

35 tests passing (was 31).

test(bedrock): add integration-level tests covering E2E script gaps

Add 'native tool calling integration' test suite that validates the
concerns previously only covered by the live E2E CLI script:

- Bedrock + Claude 4 is recognized as native tool calling eligible
  (catches silent regression if Bedrock is removed from
  isNextGenModelProvider or Claude 4 from isNextGenModelFamily)
- Bedrock + Claude 3.x correctly does NOT qualify (pre-4.0 guard)
- Native tool calling disabled when user setting is off
- createAnthropicMessage passes toolConfig to ConverseStreamCommand
  (catches the tool spec not reaching the API)
- Full multi-turn tool call round-trip formatting (tool_use in
  assistant → tool_result in user → reformatted for next API call)

40 tests passing (was 35).
Remove functional verification script before code review

title: feat: consolidate ViewHeader and styling
body: ### Related Issue **Issue:** N/A - Cosmetic improvement for UI consistency ### Description This PR addresses inconsistencies in section header styling across different views in the Cline webview UI. Previously, the History, Account, Settings, and MCP Servers views each had their own header implementation with varying styles, padding, and layouts. **Changes made:** - Created a shared `ViewHeader` component (`webview-ui/src/components/common/ViewHeader.tsx`) - Standardized header styling across all views: - Text with normal font weight (`text-lg font-normal`) - Compact "Done" button with reduced vertical padding - Consistent horizontal padding (`px-5`) - Enhanced Account view header with environment subtext (e.g., "Staging environment") using absolute positioning to prevent layout shifts - Fixed asymmetric padding issues in both logged-in and logged-out Account views - Environment text now displays in sentence case The shared component ensures consistency and makes future header modifications easier to maintain. ### Test Procedure **Manual testing performed:** 1. Verified all section headers (History, Account, Settings, MCP Servers) have identical positioning and styling 2. Tested Account view in both logged-in and logged-out states 3. Confirmed environment subtext displays correctly without causing layout shifts 4. Verified symmetric left/right padding across all views 5. Tested in both production and staging environment modes **What could potentially break:** - Layout or styling in views that use the ViewHeader component - Environment indicator display logic **Verification performed:** - Checked all four views (History, Account, Settings, MCP Servers) display correctly - Confirmed header alignment is consistent regardless of environment subtext presence - Verified no regression in existing functionality (buttons, navigation, etc.) ### Type of Change - [ ] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality) - [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected) - [x] ♻️ Refactor Changes - [x] 💅 Cosmetic Changes - [ ] 📚 Documentation update - [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes ### Pre-flight Checklist - [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [ ] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - [x] I have created a changeset using `npm run changeset` (required for user-facing changes) - [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Screenshots **Before:** Headers had inconsistent styling, bold text, and varying padding https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/af286ea9-c889-455e-aab3-ae0e1c2ecf5e **After:** - All headers now use consistent text with normal font weight - Environment indicator displays as subtext below "Account" title in sentence case - Symmetric padding across all views - Compact button styling https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0b1bf275-890e-444b-9804-d7d3c0a540c6 ### Additional Notes This is a cosmetic refactor that improves UI consistency without changing any functionality. The changes are isolated to the webview UI components and do not affect core extension logic. ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Consolidate header styling across views using a shared `ViewHeader` component for consistency and maintainability. > > - **Behavior**: > - Introduced `ViewHeader` component in `ViewHeader.tsx` for consistent header styling across views. > - Updated `AccountView.tsx`, `HistoryView.tsx`, and `McpConfigurationView.tsx` to use `ViewHeader`. > - Standardized header text to normal font weight and consistent padding. > - Added environment subtext in `AccountView.tsx`. > - **Styling**: > - Adjusted button size variants in `button.tsx` to include `header` size. > - **Misc**: > - Removed redundant imports and code in affected files. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for 5a07dde29b4f1ae7dcfd7e4f3565c635d96336b1. title: fix(cli): ensure telemetry delivery on shutdown
body: ### Related Issue Issue: #XXXX ### Description This PR fixes CLI telemetry undercounting by addressing delivery reliability and event shape consistency. Problem observed: - We launched CLI 2.0 and expected a DAU increase in PostHog, but active user counts remained unexpectedly low compared to install and usage signals. What was happening: - The CLI process had no deterministic telemetry flush path before process exit. - The CLI is often short-lived (especially in non-interactive/piped/command-style usage), which increases the chance that batched PostHog events never get shipped before process termination. - Some interactive callbacks were also exiting directly, which could bypass cleanup in certain flows. - `user.extension_activated` was emitted via a low-level path that skipped the standard metadata enrichment pipeline, making platform-segmented analytics less reliable. Technical approach: - Introduce centralized CLI teardown helpers in `cli/src/index.ts`: - `disposeTelemetryServices()` to flush/close both telemetry and PostHog client resources. - `disposeCliContext()` to run state/controller/error cleanup and telemetry disposal in one place. - Route all CLI shutdown paths through this centralized teardown (plain text mode, interactive cleanup, and signal handling), so telemetry has a chance to flush before process exit. - Keep process exit behavior intact while ensuring cleanup runs first. - Update `TelemetryService.captureExtensionActivated()` to go through `capture(...)` instead of direct provider dispatch so normal metadata is included. Why these choices: - The root issue was not event call sites alone; events were frequently emitted but not reliably delivered. - The safest fix is to guarantee teardown consistency rather than patch individual command paths ad hoc. - Using `capture(...)` for activation aligns this event with every other metadata-enriched event, reducing dashboard/filter mismatch. Alternatives considered: - Emitting more required events (`captureRequired`) to bypass opt-out: rejected, because this would violate expected telemetry controls. - Adding per-command ad hoc delays before exit: rejected, brittle and does not guarantee provider shutdown semantics. - Keeping activation event as-is and only fixing shutdown: rejected, because missing metadata can still undercount platform-segmented dashboards. ### Test Procedure What I validated during implementation: - Confirmed CLI command/event paths in `cli/src/index.ts` and telemetry provider lifecycle in `src/services/telemetry/*`. - Verified published npm artifacts (`cline@2.0.0` and `cline@2.0.5`) include telemetry event capture paths but no explicit CLI telemetry disposal/shutdown path. - Ensured all primary exit paths in CLI now route through centralized cleanup. - Confirmed `captureExtensionActivated` now passes through the metadata-enriched telemetry path. Manual verification recommended after deploy: 1. Run short-lived CLI commands (`cline task "..." --json`, piped stdin flows, quick auth flow). 2. In PostHog, verify increased counts for: - `host.detected` with `name=cline_cli` and `content=initialized` - `user.extension_activated` 3. Validate those events include expected metadata fields (`platform`, `cline_type`, `extension_version`, etc.) for dashboard segmentation. Notes: - I could not run local TypeScript/lint test suites in this worktree because dependencies were not installed (`node_modules` absent in this environment). Validation here was code-path and artifact inspection focused. ### Type of Change - [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality) - [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected) - [ ] ♻️ Refactor Changes - [ ] 💅 Cosmetic Changes - [ ] 📚 Documentation update - [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes ### Pre-flight Checklist - [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [ ] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - [ ] I have created a changeset using `npm run changeset` (required for user-facing changes) - [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Screenshots N/A (non-UI telemetry delivery fix) ### Additional Notes The `Related Issue` is currently left as `#XXXX` because I was not given a specific issue number in this thread. I can update it immediately if you share the correct issue. ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Ensure CLI telemetry delivery by centralizing cleanup and enriching event metadata in `cli/src/index.ts` and `TelemetryService.ts`. > > - **Behavior**: > - Introduce `disposeTelemetryServices()` and `disposeCliContext()` in `cli/src/index.ts` for centralized cleanup of telemetry and CLI context. > - Route all CLI shutdown paths through these functions to ensure telemetry flush before exit. > - Update `captureExtensionActivated()` in `TelemetryService.ts` to use `capture(...)` for metadata enrichment. > - **Functions**: > - Replace direct cleanup calls with `disposeCliContext()` in `runTaskInPlainTextMode()`, `createInkCleanup()`, and other functions in `cli/src/index.ts`. > - Ensure `captureExtensionActivated()` in `initializeCli()` and `runAuth()` uses enriched telemetry path. > - **Misc**: > - Add `telemetryDisposed` flag in `cli/src/index.ts` to prevent redundant disposal calls. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for 9dd324f004b7919879af390c394c506b96226a34. You can [customize](https://app.ellipsis.dev/cline/settings/summaries) this summary. It will automatically update as commits are pushed.

title: fix: restore reasoning behavior parity after #9168
body: ### Related Issue Issue: N/A (follow-up to #9168) ### Description This PR is a targeted behavior-parity follow-up to #9168. The core goal is not to redesign reasoning controls, but to reduce regression risk by restoring or preserving pre-#9168 runtime behavior where that behavior was relied on, while keeping the post-#9168 configuration model in place. What problem this PR solves: - #9168 intentionally centralized reasoning into mode-scoped model config. - In practice, users reported altered request behavior and settings behavior, especially in OpenAI-compatible paths where deployments vary a lot across proxies and custom gateways. - We needed a minimal, surgical patch that restores expected defaults and request payload behavior to reduce breakage risk. Technical approach and why each piece exists: 1) Restore medium fallback as the canonical normalized default - Updated shared normalization fallback in `src/shared/storage/types.ts` from `low` back to `medium`. - Updated CLI invalid-flag fallback message/behavior in `cli/src/index.ts` to `medium`. - Updated UI selector fallback in `webview-ui/src/components/settings/ReasoningEffortSelector.tsx` to `medium`. - Updated Requesty and OCA provider fallbacks to `medium`. Why: - Pre-#9168 behavior and user expectation were centered on medium defaulting. - Low fallback changed observable behavior for users who never intentionally changed this setting. 2) OpenAI-compatible (`openai.ts`) parity for reasoning-family detection and default send behavior - Kept the historical reasoning-family model-id detection path: - `o1`, `o3`, `o4`, `gpt-5`, excluding `chat` ids. - In that branch, restored medium-by-default behavior when unset, with explicit `none` still disabling send. Why: - OpenAI-compatible is heavily used with custom and proxy backends. - Users depended on the historical model-id-driven behavior in this provider. 3) Preserve OpenAI Native UX semantics around reasoning picker instead of Anthropic-style thinking budget coupling - OpenAI Native chat and responses now use reasoning effort semantics directly. - `none` disables reasoning payload. - Unset uses normalized medium. Why: - In OpenAI Native UI, users get reasoning effort controls, not the Anthropic thinking budget slider. - Coupling to thinking budget in this provider was inconsistent with UI and caused confusing behavior. 4) Keep gateway transforms explicit and safe for unset values - In OpenRouter and Vercel transforms, normalization is now only applied when a reasoning effort value is actually set. - This avoids injecting an implicit effort value simply because normalization function has a default. Why: - Avoid surprising payload changes for users who never touched reasoning settings. - This is especially important in proxy or gateway environments where unknown params can fail hard. 5) Proto cleanup for removed legacy enum value - Replaced `MINIMAL = 3` with `reserved 3` in `proto/cline/state.proto`. Why: - Keeps schema evolution safe and avoids accidental reuse. 6) Unify webview and backend reasoning-support detection logic - Added shared helper `src/shared/utils/reasoning-support.ts`. - Backend now re-exports `supportsReasoningEffortForModel` from shared helper. - Webview provider utility delegates to the same shared helper. Why: - Prevents drift where UI says reasoning is supported but backend does not, or vice versa. - Reduces duplicated, subtly different heuristics. Debugging journey and non-obvious gotchas: - During investigation, I compared current behavior directly against pre-#9168 snapshots (`6c53daa88^`) for each provider/transform rather than reasoning from memory. - The biggest hidden gotcha was that shared normalization defaults can change runtime payload behavior even when a user never set reasoning effort, depending on where normalization is invoked. - Another subtle issue was provider heterogeneity: - OpenAI-compatible often needs conservative behavior because custom stacks vary widely. - OpenAI Native is first-party but had a historical coupling to thinking budget that does not match current UI mental model. - A webview/backend mismatch around reasoning support checks was another source of potentially confusing UX. This PR aligns both sides to one helper. Alternatives considered: - Strictly revert all behavior to pre-#9168 in every path. - Rejected because it would re-introduce known inconsistencies and keep some buggy coupling in place. - Keep all #9168 behavior as-is and only add tests. - Rejected because users were already seeing altered behavior and needed runtime parity fixes first. ### Test Procedure Validation executed locally: - `npm run check-types` - Runs proto generation plus type checks for root, webview-ui, and CLI. - `npm run lint` - Runs biome lint plus proto lint checks. What I verified manually in code and behavior logic: - Fallback defaults are consistently medium across shared normalization, CLI warning fallback, and reasoning-effort selector fallback. - OpenAI-compatible reasoning-family branch uses historical model-id parsing and sends medium by default when unset. - OpenAI Native relies on reasoning effort semantics and respects explicit `none`. - OpenRouter/Vercel transform logic does not implicitly derive effort from normalization when effort is unset. - Webview and backend reasoning-support checks call a single shared helper. Potential break areas reviewed: - Provider-specific payload construction for reasoning fields. - Model family detection gates. - Proto enum backward compatibility safety via reserved slots. ### Type of Change - [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality) - [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected) - [x] ♻️ Refactor Changes - [ ] 💅 Cosmetic Changes - [ ] 📚 Documentation update - [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes ### Pre-flight Checklist - [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [ ] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - [ ] I have created a changeset using `npm run changeset` (required for user-facing changes) - [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) Notes: - I ran `npm run check-types` and `npm run lint` successfully. - I did not run full `npm test` in this pass. - No changeset included in this follow-up. ### Screenshots N/A. This PR is behavior and request-payload focused. ### Additional Notes This PR intentionally emphasizes behavior parity and reduction of regression risk after #9168. If maintainers want an even stricter parity mode for gateway transforms (for example only applying reasoning effort to `openai/o*` in those paths), that can be done as a small follow-up with minimal churn. ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Restores reasoning behavior to pre-#9168 defaults, ensuring consistent `medium` default and unified reasoning-support logic across components. > > - **Behavior**: > - Restores `medium` as the default reasoning effort in `types.ts`, `index.ts`, `ReasoningEffortSelector.tsx`, and `oca.ts`. > - Maintains historical reasoning-family model-id detection in `openai.ts` and restores `medium` default when unset. > - Ensures OpenAI Native uses reasoning effort semantics directly, with `none` disabling reasoning payload. > - In `openrouter-stream.ts` and `vercel-ai-gateway-stream.ts`, normalization is applied only when reasoning effort is set. > - **Models**: > - Removes `MINIMAL = 3` and reserves it in `state.proto`. > - Adds `reasoning-support.ts` for unified reasoning-support detection logic. > - **Misc**: > - Updates `supportsReasoningEffortForModel` to use shared helper in `model-utils.ts` and `providerUtils.ts`. > - Adjusts `normalizeOpenaiReasoningEffort` to default to `medium` in `types.ts`. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for 559c328ba43c28f808988418eda93245d7810ef9. You can [customize](https://app.ellipsis.dev/cline/settings/summaries) this summary. It will automatically update as commits are pushed.

title: feat(cli): add max-consecutive-mistakes flag for yolo runs
body: ### Related Issue Issue: N/A ### Description This PR adds a CLI task flag, `--max-consecutive-mistakes `, so automated runs can control when YOLO mode stops after repeated tool/flow errors. Problem this addresses: Recent Harbor Terminal Bench runs using Gemini via Vercel were repeatedly ending with `Too many consecutive mistakes (3)`. In these runs, the default threshold of 3 was terminating many trials before the model could recover. We already fixed other major run configuration problems (model routing, prompt delimiter handling, timeout), but this specific threshold remained hardcoded from a CLI operator perspective. That meant we could not isolate model quality from safety-stop sensitivity without code changes. Why this change is needed: The core task engine already supports configurable `maxConsecutiveMistakes` in global state, but current CLI options do not expose that setting for task execution. As a result, benchmark runners and CI wrappers cannot tune this value at invocation time. This PR closes that gap by exposing a first-class flag and applying it during task option setup. Technical approach: - Added a new CLI option on both task entry surfaces: - `cline task ... --max-consecutive-mistakes ` - `cline ... --max-consecutive-mistakes ` - Extended task option parsing to include `maxConsecutiveMistakes`. - Added normalization and validation logic: - accepts integer values >= 1 - invalid values produce a warning and are ignored - When valid, writes `maxConsecutiveMistakes` into state before task execution and emits a telemetry host event. Debugging journey and non-obvious gotchas: During investigation, there was an assumption that this might already be externally configurable through historical syntax (`-s max_consecutive_mistakes=...`). That path appears in older/deprecated CLI docs and comments, but does not exist in the current `commander`-based CLI command parser. The key gotcha is that configurability existed in core state and internal flows, yet was not surfaced by current public CLI flags. This made run-time tuning look available in theory while still unavailable in practice. Decisions and alternatives considered: I considered bypassing the mistake limit entirely in YOLO mode for benchmark runs, but rejected that because it weakens protection globally and risks runaway loops in normal automation usage. Exposing a threshold knob is safer and more flexible: - default behavior remains unchanged at 3 - benchmark operators can explicitly raise the limit - regular users keep existing safeguards unless they opt in How to test: 1. Parser-level tests (already added) verify the new option is accepted in both command forms. 2. Run `cline task "echo test" --max-consecutive-mistakes 10` and confirm no parse errors. 3. Run with an invalid value (for example `0` or `abc`) and confirm warning is emitted and execution continues with existing configured/default limit. 4. In benchmark wrappers, append `--max-consecutive-mistakes 999` to confirm YOLO stops are no longer dominated by the threshold in early recovery scenarios. ### Test Procedure I validated this change with focused CLI tests and static checks: - `npm -C cli run test:run -- src/index.test.ts` - `npm -C cli run typecheck` I also added explicit parser tests for: - task command form parsing (`--max-consecutive-mistakes 999`) - default command form parsing (`--max-consecutive-mistakes 7`) Potential break surface considered: - Existing invocations are unaffected because the new flag is optional. - Invalid values are handled defensively with warning + no state write. - Default threshold behavior remains unchanged when the flag is omitted. ### Type of Change - [ ] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [x] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality) - [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected) - [ ] ♻️ Refactor Changes - [ ] 💅 Cosmetic Changes - [ ] 📚 Documentation update - [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes ### Pre-flight Checklist - [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [ ] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - [ ] I have created a changeset using `npm run changeset` (required for user-facing changes) - [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Screenshots N/A (CLI flag only) ### Additional Notes Primary intended usage for benchmark runs: `cline -y --thinking 6000 --double-check-completion -t 4800 --max-consecutive-mistakes 999 -- ''` ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Adds `--max-consecutive-mistakes` flag to CLI for controlling mistake threshold in YOLO mode, with input validation and telemetry support. > > - **Behavior**: > - Adds `--max-consecutive-mistakes ` flag to CLI in `index.ts` for controlling mistake threshold in YOLO mode. > - Validates input as integer >= 1; invalid inputs trigger a warning and are ignored. > - Updates `applyTaskOptions()` to set `maxConsecutiveMistakes` in global state and emit telemetry event. > - **Tests**: > - Adds tests in `index.test.ts` to verify parsing of `--max-consecutive-mistakes` in both task and default command forms. > - Confirms valid input is parsed correctly and invalid input triggers warnings. > - **Misc**: > - Updates `normalizeMaxConsecutiveMistakes()` in `index.ts` for input validation and normalization. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for 3b3d4383c92ffba953e684da90e92a44eb7ad336. You can [customize](https://app.ellipsis.dev/cline/settings/summaries) this summary. It will automatically update as commits are pushed.

title: feat: bundled endpoints.json
body: # Add bundled endpoints support for enterprise deployments ## Summary This PR adds support for bundling custom `endpoints.json` files directly into Cline distribution packages (VSIX and CLI tarball). This enables enterprise customers to distribute pre-configured Cline installations that automatically connect to their self-hosted infrastructure without requiring manual configuration. ## Problem Enterprise customers using self-hosted Cline deployments currently need to manually create `~/.cline/endpoints.json` files on each installation. This creates friction in enterprise rollouts and increases support burden. ## Solution Cline now checks for bundled `endpoints.json` in the package installation directory before checking the user directory: 1. **Priority order**: `/endpoints.json` → `~/.cline/endpoints.json` → standard mode 2. **Automatic detection**: When found, Cline automatically switches to self-hosted mode 3. **Auto-update protection**: CLI auto-update is disabled for bundled enterprise packages to preserve configuration 4. **Clear logging**: Logs "Cline running in self-hosted mode with custom endpoints" 33a35 5. Auto-update is automatically disabled for bundled packages to preserve enterprise configuration ## How It Works ### For VS Code Extension 1. Build standard VSIX: `npx @vscode/vsce package` 2. Bundle endpoints: `./scripts/add-endpoints-to-vsix.sh input.vsix output.vsix endpoints.json` 3. Distribute the bundled VSIX to users 5. On installation, extension finds and loads the bundled `endpoints.json` ### For CLI 1. Build standard tarball: `npm pack` 2. Bundle endpoints: `./scripts/add-endpoints-to-npm.sh input.tgz output.tgz endpoints.json` 3. Distribute the bundled tarball to users 4. On installation, CLI finds and loads the bundled `endpoints.json` ## Key Changes ### Core Implementation - **`src/config.ts`**: Updated `ClineEndpoint.initialize()` to check bundled location first; added `isBundledConfig()` method to detect bundled enterprise packages - **`cli/src/utils/update.ts`**: Updated `autoUpdateOnStartup()` to skip auto-update for bundled enterprise packages - **`cli/src/index.ts`**: Moved auto-update call to run after endpoints initialization; fixed Logger subscription order to capture initialization logs- **`cli/src/vscode-context.ts`**: Fixed ES module path resolution for CLI ### Packaging Scripts - **`scripts/add-endpoints-to-vsix.sh`**: Bundle endpoints into VSIX - **`scripts/add-endpoints-to-npm.sh`**: Bundle endpoints into CLI tarball - **`scripts/add-endpoints-to-jetbrains.sh`**: Bundle endpoints into JetBrains ZIP - **`scripts/test-bundled-endpoints.sh`**: Complete build and test workflow ### Documentation - **`docs/enterprise-solutions/bundled-endpoints.mdx`**: Complete enterprise guide - **`.changeset/big-turkeys-shake.md`**: Patch changeset entry ## Testing ### Automated Test ```bash ./scripts/test-bundled-endpoints.sh ``` This builds both VSIX and CLI packages with bundled staging endpoints and verifies: - Packages contain `endpoints.json` - Files are in correct locations - JSON structure is valid ### Manual Testing **VS Code Extension:** ```bash code --install-extension ./dist-bundled/claude-dev-3.56.2-with-endpoints.vsix # Open Output > Cline, verify: "LOG Cline running in self-hosted mode with custom endpoints" ``` **CLI:** ```bash npm install -g ./dist-bundled/cline-2.0.3-with-endpoints.tgz cline config cat ~/.cline/data/logs/cline-cli.1.log # Verify: "LOG Cline running in self-hosted mode with custom endpoints" ``` ## Deployment Workflow 1. Build official release packages as normal 2. Use packaging scripts to bundle custom endpoints 3. Distribute bundled packages to enterprise users 4. Users install and automatically connect to self-hosted infrastructure ## Breaking Changes None. This is a backward-compatible addition: - Existing installations without bundled endpoints work unchanged - User `~/.cline/endpoints.json` files still work and are checked as fallback - Standard (non-enterprise) installations are unaffected ## Important Notes ### Auto-Update Protection for Enterprise Packages The CLI now includes intelligent auto-update protection: - **Bundled enterprise packages**: Auto-update is automatically disabled to prevent overwriting custom configuration - **User `~/.cline/endpoints.json`**: Auto-update remains enabled (users can manage updates themselves) - **Standard installations**: Auto-update works normally - **Manual override**: Set `CLINE_NO_AUTO_UPDATE=1` environment variable to disable auto-update in any scenario This ensures enterprise customers' bundled packages remain stable and don't get silently replaced with standard npm versions. ## Checklist - [x] Code changes implemented and tested - [x] Documentation added - [x] Changeset created - [x] Both VS Code extension and CLI verified working - [x] Packaging scripts tested - [x] No breaking changes ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Adds support for bundling `endpoints.json` in Cline packages for enterprise deployments, prioritizing bundled configurations and disabling auto-updates. > > - **Behavior**: > - Cline checks `/endpoints.json` before `~/.cline/endpoints.json` for configuration. > - Auto-update is disabled for packages with bundled `endpoints.json`. > - Logs "Cline running in self-hosted mode with custom endpoints" when using bundled config. > - **Core Implementation**: > - `ClineEndpoint.initialize()` in `src/config.ts` now checks for bundled `endpoints.json`. > - `autoUpdateOnStartup()` in `cli/src/utils/update.ts` skips updates for bundled packages. > - Adjusted initialization order in `cli/src/index.ts` and `cli/src/agent/ClineAgent.ts` to ensure logging captures. > - **Packaging Scripts**: > - Added `scripts/add-endpoints-to-vsix.sh`, `scripts/add-endpoints-to-npm.sh`, and `scripts/add-endpoints-to-jetbrains.sh` for bundling `endpoints.json`. > - `scripts/test-bundled-endpoints.sh` added for testing bundled package creation. > - **Documentation**: > - New guide `docs/enterprise-solutions/bundled-endpoints.mdx` for enterprise distribution with bundled endpoints. > - **Testing**: > - Updated `src/__tests__/config.test.ts` to test bundled and user `endpoints.json` handling. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for e51409100bc5364c5bf0b3f8634a293ae4f18007. title: fix(api): preserve selected Vercel model when model metadata is missing
body: ### Related Issue Issue: N/A ### Description This PR fixes a model selection bug in the Vercel AI Gateway provider that can silently route requests to the wrong model. Problem statement: - In Harbor benchmark runs, we configured trials with `vercel:google/gemini-3-pro-preview`. - At runtime, many trial artifacts (`prompt_manifest.latest.json`) reported `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.5`. - This produced misleading benchmark conclusions because the configured model and the effective model diverged. Root cause: - `VercelAIGatewayHandler.getModel()` only returned the configured model when both `openRouterModelId` and `openRouterModelInfo` were present. - If model info was missing, the method fell back to `openRouterDefaultModelId`, which is currently `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.5`. - That fallback changed the actual model identity, not only metadata. Why this happened in practice: - The configuration path can set a provider model ID even when per-model metadata is unavailable or not hydrated. - In that state, using a default model ID is dangerous because it changes model behavior rather than preserving intent. Technical approach in this PR: - Updated `VercelAIGatewayHandler.getModel()` to match the already-correct behavior in `ClineHandler.getModel()`. - New behavior: 1. If `modelId` and `modelInfo` exist, return both. 2. If only `modelId` exists, preserve that ID and use `openRouterDefaultModelInfo` as metadata. 3. If no `modelId` exists, use default ID and default info. Why this is the right tradeoff: - Preserving the configured model ID prevents silent model swaps. - Falling back only metadata is safer than falling back model identity. - This keeps runtime behavior aligned with user or evaluator intent even when metadata is incomplete. Debugging journey and validation context: - We started from benchmark anomalies where Gemini-labeled runs showed unexpectedly Claude-like outcomes. - We compared `config.json` model settings with runtime prompt manifests in trial artifacts. - We consistently observed configured Gemini plus runtime Sonnet in affected Vercel runs. - We then traced model resolution from provider config to `buildApiHandler()` to `VercelAIGatewayHandler.getModel()`. - We found the strict `modelId && modelInfo` guard causing default-model fallback. - We verified that `ClineHandler` already contained the safer fallback and mirrored that pattern here. Alternatives considered: - Fail hard when `modelInfo` is missing. - Rejected because this would turn recoverable config states into hard execution failures. - Hydrate model metadata eagerly in every auth or config path before task start. - Useful follow-up, but larger surface area and not required to fix incorrect model routing. - Keep existing fallback to default model. - Rejected because it silently violates configured model selection. Non-obvious gotcha discovered: - A fallback that seems like a metadata default can become a model routing default if `id` and `info` are coupled in one condition. - This is especially risky in benchmark pipelines where model identity is the primary controlled variable. ### Test Procedure What I ran locally: 1. `npm ci` 2. `npm run protos` 3. `npm run test:unit -- src/core/api/providers/__tests__/vercel-ai-gateway.test.ts` What was verified: - New unit tests validate all three getModel branches: - configured id and info - configured id without info - missing id - Existing unit suite invoked in this run stayed green in this environment. - Mocha summary from the run: `865 passing`, `4 pending`. Risk assessment: - Low risk, narrow scope. - Behavior only changes when `modelInfo` is missing. - Normal path with both id and info remains unchanged. ### Type of Change - [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality) - [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected) - [ ] ♻️ Refactor Changes - [ ] 💅 Cosmetic Changes - [ ] 📚 Documentation update - [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes ### Pre-flight Checklist - [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [ ] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - [ ] I have created a changeset using `npm run changeset` (required for user-facing changes) - [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Screenshots N/A. This is a backend provider selection fix. ### Additional Notes This fix intentionally aligns Vercel AI Gateway model fallback behavior with the existing Cline provider behavior to keep model identity stable under partial metadata conditions. ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Fixes model selection in `VercelAIGatewayHandler` to preserve configured model ID when metadata is missing, preventing silent model swaps. > > - **Behavior**: > - Fixes model selection in `VercelAIGatewayHandler.getModel()` to preserve configured model ID when metadata is missing. > - Aligns behavior with `ClineHandler.getModel()` to prevent silent model swaps. > - **Tests**: > - Adds `vercel-ai-gateway.test.ts` to test `getModel()` for cases with both model ID and info, only model ID, and missing model ID. > - **Misc**: > - Updates `getModel()` in `vercel-ai-gateway.ts` to use default metadata when only model ID is present. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for e7fc7a788870dc9032c2158161cafa0bafc20d60. You can [customize](https://app.ellipsis.dev/cline/settings/summaries) this summary. It will automatically update as commits are pushed.

title: fix(e2e): increase test timeout for flaky Windows CI
body: ### Related Issue Fixes flaky Windows e2e failures: - https://github.com/cline/cline/actions/runs/21796086171/job/62883802087 - https://github.com/cline/cline/actions/runs/21788641885/job/62864079677 ### Description The diff editor e2e test (`diff.test.ts`) flakes consistently on Windows CI. The test does a lot of setup before the diff assertion: sign in, send a message, click "Start New Task", verify history renders, then send a second "edit_request" message and wait for the diff editor to appear. On Windows CI runners this setup alone can eat most of the 40s budget, leaving `waitForSelector` with not enough time before the test timeout kills the browser (which produces the confusing "Target page, context or browser has been closed" error on retry). The fix bumps the CI/Windows test timeout from 40s to 60s to give enough headroom. The expect timeout (5s for individual assertions) stays the same. ### Test Procedure This is a test infrastructure change. The proof is in CI -- if the Windows e2e job passes on this PR, the fix works. ### Type of Change - [x] Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [ ] New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality) - [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected) - [ ] Refactor Changes - [ ] Cosmetic Changes - [ ] Documentation update - [ ] Workflow Changes ### Pre-flight Checklist - [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [x] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - [ ] I have created a changeset using `npm run changeset` (required for user-facing changes) - [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Increase Windows CI test timeout to 60s in `playwright.config.ts` to fix flaky e2e tests. > > - **Behavior**: > - Increase test timeout from 40s to 60s for Windows CI in `playwright.config.ts` to address flaky e2e test failures. > - `expect` timeout remains at 5s for individual assertions. > - **Files**: > - Modify `timeout` in `playwright.config.ts` for `isCI` or `isWindow` conditions. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for adfdd711f97f47dfe5a4bca6ccc1332e984440cf. You can [customize](https://app.ellipsis.dev/cline/settings/summaries) this summary. It will automatically update as commits are pushed.
author: saoudrizwan

reviews.body:
Reviewed at commit: adfdd711f

Thanks for this CI stability fix, @saoudrizwan\! Flaky tests on Windows CI are a persistent pain point, and this is a well-targeted solution.

## What This PR Does

This PR increases the Playwright test timeout from 40s to 60s for CI environments (and Windows) to address flaky e2e test failures on Windows CI runners.

**The single-line change** is in `playwright.config.ts:12`:
```typescript
// Before
timeout: isCI || isWindow ? 40000 : 20000,

// After  
timeout: isCI || isWindow ? 60000 : 20000,
```

**Why this matters**: The `diff.test.ts` e2e test performs significant setup before its core assertions: sign in, send a message, click "Start New Task", verify history renders, then send a second "edit_request" message and wait for the diff editor to appear. On Windows CI runners (which tend to be slower), this setup chain can consume most of the 40s budget, leaving insufficient time for the final `waitForSelector` call before Playwright's test timeout kills the browser—producing the confusing "Target page, context or browser has been closed" error.

**Context on the timeout architecture**:
- Global test `timeout` (being changed here): 40s → 60s for CI/Windows
- `expect` timeout for individual assertions: 5s (unchanged)
- Local development timeout: 20s (unchanged)

The 60s value provides 50% more headroom while still failing reasonably fast on actual issues.

## Review Findings

**This is a clean, appropriate fix.** The change is minimal, well-scoped, and follows established patterns in this codebase.

**Observations:**

1. **No changeset needed** - Correct. This is CI infrastructure only; no user-facing impact.

2. **No tests needed** - The proof is in CI itself. If Windows e2e passes on this PR, the fix works.

3. **Historical precedent** - This repo has a history of timeout adjustments for CI stability:
   - PR #8547 increased `getSidebar` timeout for macOS CI
   - PR #5241 restructured Playwright timeouts generally
   - PR #6628 fixe

## Pull request overview Adjusts Playwright e2e test timeouts to reduce CI flakiness (notably on Windows) by providing more headroom for slow pre-assertion setup. **Changes:** - Increased the global e2e per-test timeout in `playwright.config.ts` from 40s to 60s when running on CI or Windows. - Left per-assertion `expect` timeout unchanged. --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

commits.message:
fix(e2e): increase test timeout for Windows CI runners

The diff editor e2e test flakes consistently on Windows CI because the
40s test timeout is too tight. The test does signin, message send,
history verification, then a second message send before the diff
assertion -- on slow Windows runners this setup alone can eat most of
the budget. Bumping to 60s gives enough headroom.
Merge branch 'main' into saoudrizwan/fix-flaky-windows-e2e

title: feat: add output precision and threshold rules to double-check prompt
body: ### Related Issue Builds on #9178 and #9180 ### Description Moves Robin's Terminal-Bench-proven rules (output precision and threshold iteration) from the system prompt into the double-check completion re-verification checklist. These rules were proven effective at fixing specific benchmark failures (log-summary-date-ranges and dna-insert), but they belong in the double-check prompt where they're enforced at completion verification time rather than in every system prompt message. Adds items 5 and 6 to the checklist: - 5: Output files contain exactly what was specified -- no extra columns, fields, debug output, or commentary - 6: If the task specifies numerical thresholds or accuracy targets, verify the result meets the criteria before declaring completion ### Test Procedure The double-check prompt is a string returned as a tool error. The existing unit tests cover the boolean toggle logic. title: feat: add double-check completion experimental feature
body: ### Related Issue Internal -- motivated by Harbor Terminal Bench 2.0 results analysis. ### Description One of the biggest failure patterns we've seen in Harbor Terminal Bench 2.0 runs is premature `attempt_completion`. The model declares the task done before all required work is actually finished -- files not created, steps skipped, edge cases ignored. We initially discussed heuristic approaches (inferring required output files from the task description), but that felt too non-deterministic and fragile. This PR takes a deterministic approach instead: a two-step completion flow. When enabled, the first `attempt_completion` call in a task is rejected with a tool error that asks the model to re-verify its work against the original task. The rejection message includes the initial task text (up to 4000 chars) so the model has concrete context to check against, not just a generic "did you finish?" prompt. The second `attempt_completion` call proceeds normally. The mechanics are simple: a `completionAttemptCount` counter on `TaskState` increments on every `attempt_completion` call. When the setting is enabled and the count is 1, the handler returns early with a `formatResponse.toolError(...)` before any hooks or UI logic run. The counter resets naturally per-task since `TaskState` is created fresh for each task. `consecutiveMistakeCount` is intentionally NOT incremented on rejection since this is expected behavior, not a mistake. The setting (`doubleCheckCompletionEnabled`, default `false`) flows through the full stack following the same pattern as `yoloModeToggled` and `strictPlanModeEnabled`: proto field in `UpdateSettingsRequest` (field 41) and auto-generated `Settings` message (field 176), `USER_SETTINGS_FIELDS` in `state-keys.ts`, `TaskConfig` interface + `TASK_CONFIG_KEYS` + `ToolExecutor.asToolConfig()` wiring, `ExtensionState`, `getStateToPostToWebview()`, and `updateSettings()` handler. How to enable: - VS Code extension: Settings > Features > Experimental > "Double-Check Completion" toggle - CLI flag: `cline --double-check-completion "your task"` - CLI TUI: Settings panel > Features > "Double-check completion" toggle Also adds a `cli:run` convenience script (`npm run cli:run`) for quick CLI testing without needing to type `node cli/dist/cli.mjs`. ### Test Procedure - All three TypeScript targets (extension, webview, CLI) pass `tsc --noEmit` with zero errors - Full unit test suite passes: 858 passing, 0 failing - 9 new unit tests covering: disabled path (no rejection), enabled first call rejected, enabled second call accepted, third+ calls accepted, `consecutiveMistakeCount` not affected by rejection, counter initialization at 0, counter reset with new TaskState - Default is `false` everywhere, so existing users see zero behavior change unless they explicitly opt in ### Type of Change - [ ] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [x] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality) - [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected) - [ ] ♻️ Refactor Changes - [ ] 💅 Cosmetic Changes - [ ] 📚 Documentation update - [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes ### Pre-flight Checklist - [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [x] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - [ ] I have created a changeset using `npm run changeset` (required for user-facing changes) - [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Additional Notes Files changed (15): | Layer | Files | |-------|-------| | Proto/state | `proto/cline/state.proto`, `src/shared/storage/state-keys.ts`, `src/shared/ExtensionMessage.ts` | | Controller | `src/core/controller/state/updateSettings.ts`, `src/core/controller/index.ts` | | Task core | `src/core/task/TaskState.ts`, `src/core/task/ToolExecutor.ts`, `src/core/task/tools/handlers/AttemptCompletionHandler.ts` | | TaskConfig | `src/core/task/tools/types/TaskConfig.ts`, `src/core/task/tools/utils/ToolConstants.ts` | | Webview | `webview-ui/src/.../FeatureSettingsSection.tsx`, `webview-ui/src/.../ExtensionStateContext.tsx` | | CLI | `cli/src/index.ts`, `cli/src/components/SettingsPanelContent.tsx` | | Tests | `src/core/task/tools/handlers/__tests__/AttemptCompletionHandler.doubleCheck.test.ts` | ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Add `doubleCheckCompletionEnabled` feature to require task re-verification before final completion, with CLI support and tests. > > - **Behavior**: > - Introduces `doubleCheckCompletionEnabled` setting to reject first `attempt_completion` call, requiring re-verification before acceptance. > - Implemented in `AttemptCompletionHandler` to check `TaskState.doubleCheckCompletionPending`. > - Default is `false`, no behavior change unless enabled. > - **Settings**: > - Added `doubleCheckCompletionEnabled` to `Settings` in `state.proto` and `state-keys.ts`. > - Updated `FeatureSettingsSection.tsx` and `ExtensionStateContext.tsx` to handle new setting. > - **Tests**: > - Added `AttemptCompletionHandler.doubleCheck.test.ts` to test rejection and acceptance logic. > - **CLI**: > - Added `--double-check-completion` flag to CLI options in `index.ts`. > - **Misc**: > - Updated `TaskConfig` and `ToolConstants.ts` to include `doubleCheckCompletionEnabled`. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for 8d869d68dc8fc4bcea5c684cb24cff228a066e66. You can [customize](https://app.ellipsis.dev/cline/settings/summaries) this summary. It will automatically update as commits are pushed.

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Introduces an experimental “double-check completion” flow to reduce premature `attempt_completion` by rejecting the first completion attempt (when enabled) and prompting the model to re-verify against the original task text.

**Changes:**
- Adds a `doubleCheckCompletionEnabled` setting that flows through proto/state, extension/webview, task config wiring, and CLI.
- Tracks `completionAttemptCount` on `TaskState` and adds early-rejection logic in `AttemptCompletionHandler`.
- Adds unit tests and a `cli:run` convenience script.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 16 out of 16 changed files in this pull request and generated 4 comments.

<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| webview-ui/src/context/ExtensionStateContext.tsx | Adds default state for the new setting in webview context. |
| webview-ui/src/components/settings/sections/FeatureSettingsSection.tsx | Adds the experimental feature toggle and includes it in posted settings. |
| src/shared/storage/state-keys.ts | Registers the setting with a default value in persisted user settings fields. |

| src/core/task/tools/utils/ToolConstants.ts | Adds the key to `TASK_CONFIG_KEYS`; modifies `removeClosingTag` regex (currently problematic). |
| src/core/task/tools/types/TaskConfig.ts | Adds `doubleCheckCompletionEnabled` to the tool execution config interface. |
| src/core/task/tools/handlers/AttemptCompletionHandler.ts | Implements the “reject first completion attempt” logic and task preview injection. |
| src/core/task/ToolExecutor.ts | Wires the new setting into `TaskConfig` creation. |
