commits.message:
feat: add double-check completion experimental feature

When enabled, the first attempt_completion call in a task is rejected
with a tool error that instructs the model to re-verify its work
against the original task requirements. The rejection includes the
initial task text for context. The second call proceeds normally.

This is opt-in (default off) and available via:
- Settings > Features > Experimental > Double-Check Completion
- CLI flag: --double-check-completion
- CLI TUI settings panel toggle

Adds completionAttemptCount to TaskState, plumbs the setting through
TaskConfig/ToolExecutor following existing patterns, and includes
9 unit tests.
chore: add cli:run script for quick CLI testing
fix: increase task preview to 8000 chars, revert unintended regex change

fix: preserve existing proto field numbers

The auto-generator renumbered open_ai_headers (175->177) and
openai_codex_oauth_credentials (46->48), and dropped the reserved 146
comment. Restore original field numbers to avoid breaking wire-format
compatibility.

fix: remove partial completion_result message on double-check rejection

During streaming, handlePartialBlock shows the completion_result in
the chat view. When we reject the first attempt, we need to clean up
that partial message so the user doesn't see a stale completion that
was actually rejected.

refactor: switch from counter to boolean toggle for double-check

Use a boolean pending flag instead of a counter so that every
attempt_completion gets double-checked, not just the first one in
a task. The flag toggles: reject (set pending), accept (clear pending),
so if the model does more work and tries to complete again later, it
gets double-checked again.
Merge branch 'main' into saoudrizwan/double-check-completion

title: fix(prompt): add output precision and threshold iteration rules
body: ### Related Issue **Issue:** Part of Terminal-Bench hillclimb effort (follows #9154, #9156, #9159, #9161) ### Description Adds two concise, general-purpose prompt rules to improve agent output quality, tested and validated via Terminal-Bench benchmarks on Harbor. **Rule 1 — Output precision:** "When writing output files, produce exactly what the task specifies—no extra columns, fields, debug output, or commentary. Match the requested format precisely." **Rule 2 — Threshold iteration:** "When the task specifies numerical thresholds or accuracy targets, verify your result meets the criteria before completing. If close but not passing, iterate rather than declaring completion." These rules address two common failure patterns: - Agent adds extra columns/debug output to files (e.g., adding an `instance` column to a CSV that should only have `period,severity,count`) - Agent declares completion without verifying numerical results meet stated thresholds (e.g., Tm difference of 5.44°C when ≤5.0 was required) **What was excluded and why:** A third "no-cleanup" rule was tested and deliberately dropped. Despite using "STRICTLY FORBIDDEN" language, it failed to prevent the agent from deleting deployed content on `configure-git-webserver`, and caused a side-effect on `polyglot-c-py` (prevented legitimate build artifact cleanup). The cleanup behavior is too deeply trained to override via prompt rules. ### Test Procedure Tested on [Harbor](https://github.com/laude-institute/harbor) Terminal-Bench benchmark with Opus 4.6 (job `2026-02-07__16-15-00`, 6 targeted tasks on Modal): | Task | Baseline | With Rules | Rule | |------|----------|-----------|------| | log-summary-date-ranges | ❌ 0.0 | ✅ **1.0** | output precision | | dna-insert | ❌ 0.0 | ✅ **1.0** | threshold iteration | | mteb-leaderboard | ❌ 0.0 | ❌ 0.0 | (context overflow — not a prompt issue) | | polyglot-c-py | ❌ 0.0 | ❌ 0.0 | (build artifacts — tested no-cleanup, excluded) | | configure-git-webserver | ❌ 0.0 | ❌ 0.0 | (self-sabotage — no-cleanup rule failed) | | train-fasttext | ❌ 0.0 | ❌ 0.0 | (8% accuracy gap too large to iterate past) | **2 clear FAIL→PASS flips** from 4 insertions across 2 files. No regressions expected — both rules constrain behavior toward correctness. ### Type of Change - [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) ### Pre-flight Checklist - [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [ ] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - [ ] I have created a changeset using `npm run changeset` (required for user-facing changes) - [ ] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Additional Notes These two rules follow [Cursor's agent best practices](https://cursor.com/blog/agent-best-practices): "Add rules only when you notice the agent making the same mistake repeatedly. Keep rules focused on essentials." Both are concise (1 line each), general-purpose, and empirically validated. ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Adds rules for output precision and threshold iteration to improve agent output quality in multiple snapshot and template files. > > - **Behavior**: > - Adds rule for output precision: "Produce exactly what the task specifies—no extra columns, fields, debug output, or commentary. Match the requested format precisely." > - Adds rule for threshold iteration: "Verify numerical thresholds or accuracy targets before completing. If close but not passing, iterate rather than declaring completion." > - **Files**: > - Updates `rules.ts` and `template.ts` to include new rules in the rules template. > - Adds new rules to `anthropic_claude_sonnet_4-basic.snap`, `cline_devstral-basic.snap`, and `openai_gpt_3-basic.snap` among others. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for e57b64654a976774f45557ede8ee6c5369c1998a. You can [customize](https://app.ellipsis.dev/cline/settings/summaries) this summary. It will automatically update as commits are pushed.
author: robinnewhouse

reviews.body:
Reviewed at commit: 867e0d69b

Thanks for contributing to the Terminal-Bench hillclimbing effort, @robinnewhouse! This is a well-documented PR with clear methodology and empirical validation.

## Summary

This PR adds two new prompt rules to improve agent output quality, specifically targeting failure patterns observed in Terminal-Bench benchmarks:

1. **Output precision rule**: Instructs the agent to produce exactly what the task specifies—no extra columns, fields, debug output, or commentary. This addresses cases where the agent was adding unexpected fields (e.g., an extra `instance` column to a CSV).

2. **Threshold iteration rule**: Instructs the agent to verify numerical results meet specified criteria before completing, and to iterate if close but not passing. This addresses premature completion when results almost (but didn't quite) meet thresholds.

**Files changed:**
- `src/core/prompts/system-prompt/components/rules.ts` (lines 28-29) — Adds rules to the shared RULES component used by generic/devstral variants
- `src/core/prompts/system-prompt/variants/next-gen/template.ts` (lines 74-75) — Adds the same rules to the next-gen variant used by Claude 4.x and Opus

The implementation follows the established pattern from previous TerminalBench PRs (#9154, #9156, #9159, #9161) by adding concise, general-purpose rules that don't require runtime changes.

**Benchmark validation:**
- 2 clear FAIL→PASS task flips: `log-summary-date-ranges` and `dna-insert`
- No regressions observed
- A third "no-cleanup" rule was deliberately excluded after testing showed it was ineffective and caused side effects

## Issues to Address

### Missing Snapshot Updates

The PR description mentions following the pattern from previous PRs, but **the prompt integration test snapshots have not been updated**. The codebase has 55 snapshot files in `src/core/prompts/system-prompt/__tests__/__snapshots__/`, and none of them contain the new rules yet.

To fix this, run:
```bash
UPDATE_SNAPSHOTS=

commits.message:
fix(prompt): add output precision and threshold iteration rules

Two concise rules proven effective via Terminal-Bench testing:

1. Output precision: produce exactly what's specified, no extra columns/fields/debug output
2. Threshold iteration: verify results meet numerical criteria before completing

Tested on 6 targeted Terminal-Bench tasks (job 2026-02-07__16-15-00):
- log-summary-date-ranges: FAIL→PASS (output precision rule eliminated extra columns)
- dna-insert: FAIL→PASS (iterate rule helped agent meet Tm threshold)

A third rule (no-cleanup) was tested and deliberately excluded: it failed to
prevent self-sabotage on configure-git-webserver despite STRICTLY FORBIDDEN
language, and caused a side-effect on polyglot-c-py by preventing legitimate
build artifact cleanup. The cleanup behavior is too deeply trained to override
via prompt rules alone.
test: update prompt snapshots for new rules

title: fix(cli): route PostHog networking through shared fetch
body: ### Related Issue Issue: N/A ### Description This PR fixes a CLI networking inconsistency that surfaced as noisy terminal errors when telemetry flushes failed in corporate proxy environments. Problem observed: - A CLI user hit repeated `Connection error` retries during PostHog flush. - The root error chain included `SELF_SIGNED_CERT_IN_CHAIN` from Node TLS. - The output looked like a CLI failure even though it was telemetry flush behavior. What was actually broken: - Cline already has a shared network layer in `src/shared/net.ts` that standardizes proxy handling and certificate behavior for standalone/CLI. - PostHog clients were not using that shared fetch path. They were relying on default SDK/Node fetch behavior. - That creates a split-brain networking setup where model/provider traffic and telemetry traffic can behave differently under the same environment. Technical approach: - Inject Cline's shared fetch into every PostHog client constructor, so telemetry/error/feature-flag traffic follows the same network path as other CLI requests. - Kept changes minimal and scoped only to PostHog initialization sites. Files changed: - `src/services/telemetry/providers/posthog/PostHogClientProvider.ts` - `src/services/telemetry/providers/posthog/PostHogTelemetryProvider.ts` - `src/services/error/providers/PostHogErrorProvider.ts` - `src/services/feature-flags/providers/PostHogFeatureFlagsProvider.ts` Each now passes: - `fetch: (url, options) => fetch(url, options as RequestInit)` Debugging journey and non-obvious findings: - At first glance this looked like CLI command failure. - Tracing shutdown paths showed Cline does catch/contain most provider disposal errors, but PostHog core itself logs flush failures to stderr on network failure, which makes the issue very visible. - I validated that the noisy retry/error text is from PostHog SDK internals, not from Cline's Logger. - I also checked whether Cline had existing fallback logic for this specific PostHog error signature and found none. - The strongest fix available within Cline was to remove the network-stack mismatch by using shared fetch in PostHog constructors. Alternatives considered: - Disable PostHog retries/logging globally: would require deeper SDK behavior changes and would reduce useful diagnostics. - Silence stderr around shutdown: risky because it can hide unrelated runtime errors. - Only document `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS`: helpful but not sufficient when the app itself uses two different fetch paths. Why this approach: - Aligns telemetry networking with existing Cline architecture. - Minimal blast radius. - Improves behavior specifically in CLI/standalone proxy and custom CA setups. ### Test Procedure What I tested: 1. Ran formatting/lint checks on all touched files. 2. Reviewed final diff to confirm only PostHog constructor options were changed, plus import ordering/type-only import cleanups from formatter. 3. Confirmed no unrelated files were modified. Commands run: - `npx biome check --write src/services/telemetry/providers/posthog/PostHogClientProvider.ts src/services/telemetry/providers/posthog/PostHogTelemetryProvider.ts src/services/error/providers/PostHogErrorProvider.ts src/services/feature-flags/providers/PostHogFeatureFlagsProvider.ts` - `npx biome check --diagnostic-level=error src/services/telemetry/providers/posthog/PostHogClientProvider.ts src/services/telemetry/providers/posthog/PostHogTelemetryProvider.ts src/services/error/providers/PostHogErrorProvider.ts src/services/feature-flags/providers/PostHogFeatureFlagsProvider.ts` Current confidence and limits: - High confidence in the code-level fix because it is a narrow constructor option injection across all PostHog entry points. - Full repository `tsc` currently fails in this worktree due pre-existing generated proto/module resolution issues unrelated to this PR, so I did targeted validation on touched files. Potential impact checked: - Telemetry provider path - Error provider path - Feature flags provider path - Shared PostHog client provider path ### Type of Change - [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality) - [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected) - [ ] ♻️ Refactor Changes - [ ] 💅 Cosmetic Changes - [ ] 📚 Documentation update - [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes ### Pre-flight Checklist - [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [ ] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - [ ] I have created a changeset using `npm run changeset` (required for user-facing changes) - [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Screenshots N/A ### Additional Notes Operational guidance for affected users still applies: - Set `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS` to corporate CA PEM when TLS interception is in place. - Ensure proxy env vars (`https_proxy`, `http_proxy`, `no_proxy`) are correctly configured. This PR focuses on making PostHog traffic honor the same shared network behavior as the rest of CLI traffic. ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Routes PostHog networking through shared fetch to standardize CLI network behavior. > > - **Behavior**: > - Injects shared `fetch` from `src/shared/net.ts` into PostHog client constructors in `PostHogErrorProvider`, `PostHogFeatureFlagsProvider`, `PostHogClientProvider`, and `PostHogTelemetryProvider`. > - Ensures telemetry, error, and feature-flag traffic use the same network path as other CLI requests. > - **Technical Details**: > - Modifies constructor options to include `fetch: (url, options) => fetch(url, options)`. > - Aims to standardize proxy handling and certificate behavior. > - **Misc**: > - Minor import ordering and type-only import cleanups in affected files. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for 90d014bcdf3d544c4c250fd8a09457668ea3e3b0. You can [customize](https://app.ellipsis.dev/cline/settings/summaries) this summary. It will automatically update as commits are pushed.
author: saoudrizwan

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview Routes PostHog telemetry/error/feature-flag networking through Cline’s shared `fetch` implementation so CLI/standalone traffic consistently respects proxy + certificate configuration (reducing noisy flush failures in corporate proxy environments). **Changes:** - Inject shared `fetch` from `src/shared/net.ts` into all PostHog client constructors. - Minor import/type-only import cleanup from formatting. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | src/services/telemetry/providers/posthog/PostHogTelemetryProvider.ts | Passes shared `fetch` into the PostHog client used for telemetry events. | | src/services/telemetry/providers/posthog/PostHogClientProvider.ts | Ensures the shared/singleton PostHog client uses shared `fetch`. | | src/services/error/providers/PostHogErrorProvider.ts | Routes PostHog error-tracking client traffic through shared `fetch`. | | src/services/feature-flags/providers/PostHogFeatureFlagsProvider.ts | Routes PostHog feature-flag requests through shared `fetch`. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.
This PR does what it claims to do

Approved because the use of `fetch` is fine, but with one nit: please avoid `as` *if possible.*

Worth considering that some customers behind proxies may have had telemetry "turned off" by their proxy rejecting it, and now they will be sending telemetry. Worth advertising that there's a telemetry setting for people who need it.

commits.message: fix(cli): route PostHog networking through shared fetch

remove unnecessary `as RequestInit` casts from PostHog fetch wrappers

PostHogFetchOptions is a structural subset of RequestInit, so the cast
is unnecessary. Also removes a stale comment about shared client support
in PostHogErrorProvider.

title: feat(cli): allow --thinking flag to accept custom token budget
body: ### Related Issue Extends #9168 (reasoning effort model config) ### Description The `--thinking` CLI flag previously only toggled extended thinking on/off with a hardcoded 1024 token budget. This change makes it accept an optional number so users can set whatever budget they want. #### New commands this unlocks ```bash # Before: only boolean toggle (always 1024 tokens) cline "prompt" --thinking # After: optional custom budget cline "prompt" --thinking # 1024 tokens (default, same as before) cline "prompt" --thinking 4096 # 4096 tokens cline "prompt" --thinking 8000 # 8000 tokens ``` Invalid values (negative numbers, non-numeric strings) get a warning and fall back to 1024. #### Context on budget sizing The webview settings UI caps the thinking budget slider at 6000 tokens. You're unlikely to see meaningful benefit going much higher than that for most tasks, but the CLI doesn't enforce a cap -- if you want to experiment with larger budgets, you can. The budget is applied to both plan and act modes (synced when separate models is disabled, which is the default). #### Changes - `cli/src/index.ts`: Changed `--thinking` option from boolean to `[tokens]` (optional argument). Updated `applyTaskOptions` to parse the number with validation and fallback. - `cli/src/index.test.ts`: Added tests for `--thinking` with a numeric argument alongside existing boolean tests. ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > The `--thinking` CLI flag now accepts an optional token budget, defaulting to 1024, with validation for invalid inputs. > > - **Behavior**: > - `--thinking` flag in `cli/src/index.ts` now accepts an optional token budget, defaulting to 1024 if not specified. > - Invalid values (negative, non-numeric) fall back to 1024 with a warning. > - **Functions**: > - `applyTaskOptions` in `cli/src/index.ts` updated to parse and validate `--thinking` token budget. > - **Tests**: > - Added tests in `cli/src/index.test.ts` for `--thinking` with numeric argument and validation of invalid inputs. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for b714ff9e0ce6162ecb62b4fa51843a5dc1565dd7. feat(task): add support for writing prompt metadata artifacts

title: fix(terminal): tune execute_command timeout strategy for long-running tasks
body: ### Related Issue Issue: N/A ### Description TerminalBench failures showed repeated timeout/backgrounding churn on long-running commands (build, compile, test, train style workflows). The previous managed timeout default of 30 seconds is too short for these workloads and causes avoidable retries and failed trajectories. This PR updates managed timeout behavior in execute_command by: - Increasing default managed timeout from 30s to 120s. - Adding a long-running timeout tier of 300s. - Adding command-pattern heuristics to classify likely long-running commands. - Keeping explicit timeout overrides intact. - Extracting timeout decision logic into testable helper functions. Root cause in current behavior: - Managed mode used one short default timeout. - Commands that are expected to run longer were frequently backgrounded too early. Expected benchmark effect: - Fewer premature timeouts for long-running tasks. - Better completion rate on build/training-heavy TerminalBench tasks. ### Test Procedure I validated both static correctness and behavior coverage: 1) Type checks - Ran `npm run check-types` in /Users/saoud/.codex/worktrees/1bd6/cline. - Result: pass. 2) Unit tests - Added timeout-policy tests in src/core/task/tools/handlers/__tests__/ExecuteCommandToolHandler.timeout.test.ts. - Ran `npm run test:unit -- src/core/task/tools/handlers/__tests__/ExecuteCommandToolHandler.timeout.test.ts`. - Result: pass (current unit runner executes broader suite in this repo). 3) Manual runtime check in extension host - Ran a long-running managed command and confirmed no early timeout/background transition during the expected execution window. What could break: - Commands that used to time out quickly in managed mode now wait longer before timeout. Why I am confident: - Explicit timeout override path is preserved. - Non-managed path still returns undefined timeout. - New logic is isolated and covered by focused tests. ### Type of Change - [x] Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [ ] New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality) - [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected) - [ ] Refactor Changes - [ ] Cosmetic Changes - [ ] Documentation update - [ ] Workflow Changes ### Pre-flight Checklist - [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [x] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - [ ] I have created a changeset using `npm run changeset` (required for user-facing changes) - [x] I have reviewed contributor guidelines Notes on checklist: - I ran `npm run check-types` and unit tests relevant to this change. - I did not run full `npm test` end-to-end in this branch. - No user-facing copy or UI surface changed, so no changeset was added. ### Screenshots Not applicable. No UI change. ### Additional Notes Main code changes: - src/core/task/tools/handlers/ExecuteCommandToolHandler.ts - src/core/task/tools/handlers/__tests__/ExecuteCommandToolHandler.timeout.test.ts ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Enhance `ExecuteCommandToolHandler` timeout strategy for long-running tasks by increasing default timeout, adding a longer timeout for specific commands, and introducing tests. > > - **Behavior**: > - Increase default timeout in `ExecuteCommandToolHandler.ts` from 30s to 120s. > - Add 300s timeout for long-running commands using `resolveCommandTimeoutSeconds()`. > - Use regex patterns in `isLikelyLongRunningCommand()` to identify long-running commands. > - **Tests**: > - Add `ExecuteCommandToolHandler.timeout.test.ts` to test timeout logic. > - Test cases for default, explicit, and long-running command timeouts. > - **Misc**: > - Extract timeout logic into `resolveCommandTimeoutSeconds()` and `isLikelyLongRunningCommand()` for clarity and testability. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for 08b973d2c43dd5e00719c88d19eb5a7a5314f60c. title: fix(prompt): guide models to use -- for leading-dash positional args
body: Problem One TerminalBench failure mode is command construction where positional text that begins with `-` can be interpreted as CLI options by downstream tools unless `--` is used to end option parsing. In practice, this can cause hard failures before real task progress if an argument is intended to be data but gets parsed as a flag. This PR takes the prompt-first approach requested for this track. Instead of changing parser behavior in CLI runtime code, it adds explicit guidance to the system prompt to insert `--` before positional values when those values may start with `-`. What changed - Added one rule line in the shared rules component: - /Users/saoud/.codex/worktrees/1bd6/cline/src/core/prompts/system-prompt/components/rules.ts - Added the equivalent rule in next-gen variant template so behavior is explicit for that path: - /Users/saoud/.codex/worktrees/1bd6/cline/src/core/prompts/system-prompt/variants/next-gen/template.ts - Added the equivalent rule in gemini-3 overrides to keep variant parity: - /Users/saoud/.codex/worktrees/1bd6/cline/src/core/prompts/system-prompt/variants/gemini-3/overrides.ts Rule text added When passing untrusted or variable text as positional command arguments, insert `--` before the positional values if they may begin with `-` (for example `my-cli -- "$value"`). This prevents the values from being parsed as options. Why this should help TerminalBench - Some benchmark trajectories fail on command invocation shape and never reach meaningful task execution. - This rule nudges model output to a safer shell pattern that avoids option parsing collisions. - It is a low-risk behavioral guidance change with no runtime execution-path changes. Debugging and decision notes - I previously prototyped a runtime CLI parser normalization approach, then discarded it on request to keep this as a prompt-only solution. - I validated that snapshot changes are exactly the expected prompt-line insertions across the affected model families. - Keeping this as prompt-only preserves strict CLI parser semantics while still improving model command hygiene. Risks and tradeoffs - This does not harden parser behavior for non-model invocations (for example, direct manual CLI usage that omits `--`). - This relies on model compliance. It should reduce incidence, but not guarantee elimination. - If later data shows residual failures, the next step would be runtime parser hardening as a separate PR. ### Test Procedure 1. Ran prompt integration snapshots in test mode to confirm expected mismatches from the new rule line: - `npm run test:unit -- src/core/prompts/system-prompt/__tests__/integration.test.ts` 2. Regenerated snapshots intentionally: - `npm run test:unit -- src/core/prompts/system-prompt/__tests__/integration.test.ts --update-snapshots` 3. Ran repo type checks: - `npm run check-types` Observed outcome - Prompt snapshot suite passed after snapshot update. - Type checks passed. ### Type of Change - [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) - [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality) - [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected) - [ ] ♻️ Refactor Changes - [ ] 💅 Cosmetic Changes - [ ] 📚 Documentation update - [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes ### Pre-flight Checklist - [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs) - [ ] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`) - [ ] I have created a changeset using `npm run changeset` (required for user-facing changes) - [x] I have reviewed contributor guidelines Checklist notes - Ran targeted prompt integration tests and `npm run check-types`. - Did not run full `npm test` end to end. - No changeset added because this is prompt behavior guidance without direct UI or API surface change. ### Screenshots Not applicable. ### Additional Notes Changed files - 16 updated prompt snapshot files in: - /Users/saoud/.codex/worktrees/1bd6/cline/src/core/prompts/system-prompt/__tests__/__snapshots__/ ---- > [!IMPORTANT] > Adds a rule to guide models in using `--` for positional arguments starting with `-` to prevent misinterpretation as options, affecting key files and prompt snapshots. > > - **Behavior**: > - Adds rule to insert `--` before positional arguments starting with `-` in `rules.ts`, `template.ts`, and `overrides.ts`. > - Affects 16 prompt snapshot files to reflect the new rule. > - **Purpose**: > - Prevents positional arguments from being misinterpreted as options in CLI commands. > - **Testing**: > - Updated prompt integration snapshots and ran type checks to confirm changes. > > This description was created by [](https://www.ellipsis.dev?ref=cline%2Fcline&utm_source=github&utm_medium=referral) for ae0b229b230958a1818dadf6183e1eb78a9b2790. reviews.body:
Reviewed at commit: ae0b229b2

Thanks for this contribution, @saoudrizwan! This is part of your ongoing TerminalBench improvement series and addresses a specific failure mode where arguments starting with `-` get misinterpreted as CLI options.

## What This PR Does

This PR adds a single rule to the system prompt that guides models to use `--` (end-of-options delimiter) before positional arguments that may begin with a dash. The rule text is:

> When passing untrusted or variable text as positional command arguments, insert `--` before the positional values if they may begin with `-` (for example `my-cli -- "$value"`). This prevents the values from being parsed as options.

**Files changed:**
1. `src/core/prompts/system-prompt/components/rules.ts` (line 25) - The shared rules component used by generic/standard variants
2. `src/core/prompts/system-prompt/variants/next-gen/template.ts` (line 71) - The next-gen variant (Claude 4, GPT-5, etc.)
3. `src/core/prompts/system-prompt/variants/gemini-3/overrides.ts` (line 134) - The Gemini-3 variant

**Snapshot coverage:**
- 16 snapshot files were updated, covering: `anthropic_claude_sonnet_4`, `cline_devstral`, `openai_gpt_3`, and `vertex_gemini_3` model families across basic, no-browser, no-focus-chain, and no-mcp contexts.

## Code Review

### Approach Assessment

The prompt-only approach is appropriate here. The `--` delimiter is a POSIX standard that most CLI tools respect, and adding this guidance to the system prompt is low-risk. The rule is clear, includes an example, and fits naturally alongside the existing command execution guidance.

### Variant Coverage Analysis

I reviewed which variants have their own RULES overrides vs. using the shared component:

| Variant | RULES Source | New Rule Applied? |
|---------|--------------|-------------------|
| generic | shared `rules.ts` | ✅ Yes |
| next-gen | `template.ts` override | ✅ Yes (explicitly added) |
| gemini-3 | `overrides.ts` override | ✅ Yes (explicitly added) |
|

## Pull request overview

This PR updates the shared system-prompt “RULES” guidance so models are more likely to use `--` to end option parsing when passing positional arguments that may begin with `-`, reducing CLI invocation failures (notably in TerminalBench-style trajectories).

**Changes:**
- Added a new RULES line advising use of `--` before positional values that may start with `-`.
- Mirrored the same rule into the `next-gen` rules template and the `gemini-3` overrides for variant parity.
- Updated prompt integration snapshots to reflect the new rule line across affected prompt outputs.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 19 out of 19 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

This PR makes sense, its worth thinking if we might accidentally be adding too much to the prompt here.
